name: Thank CA Dems (protectvoting.org)
subject: “Bring it on, Trump” 🔥
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
Signature spots going FAST
| **_We are SO PROUD:_** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93834?t=6&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV) We’re inviting 500 Democrats to sign our exclusive letter THANKING California Democrats for defending our democracy from Trump!

[**But with the special election quickly approaching, signature spots are going FAST. Don’t miss your chance: Donate $3 in the next five minutes to add your name »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93834?t=7&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV) | [**DONATE $3 TO SIGN THE LETTER »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93834?t=8&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV)
---
**To:** California Democrats
**From:** A grateful Democrat
**Subject:** THANK YOU! Dear California Democrats,

THANK YOU for launching a special election to combat Trump’s corrupt gerrymandering!

**He and his MAGA minions are plotting to RIG the 2026 midterms plain and simple — thank goodness we have you fighting back.**

Experts predict that if you win, Democrats will FLIP the House and STOP Trump from becoming an untouchable king!

**So please know I have your back every step of the way, especially as the GOP ramps up their attacks on you.**

[**Today I’m signing this letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to help take back the House and join your fight for democracy. Trump has NOTHING on our grassroots momentum! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93834?t=9&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV) | [**DONATE $3 TO SIGN THE LETTER »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93834?t=10&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV)
---
Sincerely,
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
\- The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=11&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=21182%2E878179%2EewVBUV)

subject: The Assassination of Charlie Kirk
body:
The political activist Charlie Kirk was shot and killed in Utah on Wednesday in front of an audience of thousands.

* * *
# [The Assassination of Charlie Kirk](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=173316795&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzE2Nzk1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc1NDkyOTMsImV4cCI6MTc2MDE0MTI5MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.OOmlxCuxGpJKHCLkUQhuLDo0hL0bTCrCAeW3i9NRCWc)

---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8423ec7c-53bb-48e7-b986-4f20d47a33e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The political activist Charlie Kirk was shot and killed in Utah on Wednesday in front of an audience of thousands. He was 31 years old and leaves behind a wife and two young children.
The motivation is yet unknown because his assassin remains at large, but it's virtually guaranteed that Charlie was killed because of his political views and statements. His death is the latest episode of political violence, which is becoming worse here in America.
Some irresponsible statements followed saying that the left is more prone to violence, due to the attempts on Donald Trump's life as well as the killing of the United Health CEO. But Minnesota legislator [Melissa Hortman](https://substack.com/redirect/e8f0654f-7827-4716-af2d-47865ae3181c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and her husband were killed in their home just 3 months ago, and Paul Pelosi was attacked in his home. The truth is that violence is not unique to one side or the other in America. People who monitor the online threat environment say that there is more consistent activity than ever from both sides.
I fear that Charlie Kirk's killing will only heighten the tension and conflict. A young, well-known leader graphically and gruesomely killed in front a huge audience will anger and infuriate those on his side. The best leaders will try to bring the temperature down. The worst will want to channel the anger toward targets and ideological opponents.
Some bad actors may suggest that Kirk somehow called this violence upon himself because of his views and statements. That is wrong. No one deserves to be attacked for their point-of-view or freedom of expression. Violence is never the answer.
I've read up on the [pattern](https://substack.com/redirect/79f5b0d4-327f-4c24-9321-f69396b5b3c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in declining democracies. Typically, as polarization rises violence also goes up over multiple years until something breaks the fever, often a period of sustained conflict that leaves both sides exhausted. Eventually, people say, "Enough is enough" because of losses - and deaths - on both sides. Examples include Northern Ireland in its time of troubles or the United States in the 1960s, another time of intense polarization.
I fear that Charlie Kirk is closer to the beginning of this process than the end. I pray I'm wrong. But the fever is rising more than falling.
One reason I started [Forward](https://substack.com/redirect/ea4a8740-2645-4657-b910-e21ab77b07ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was that I believed that a third force in American politics could reduce polarization. Change the mechanics and incentives, and perhaps cooler heads could prevail. Most of us know that violence is never the answer.
Charlie Kirk should still be alive today. So should [Melissa Hortman](https://substack.com/redirect/e8f0654f-7827-4716-af2d-47865ae3181c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). May their deaths bring us back from the brink. Do all you can to remind people of our shared humanity so that others don't lose their mothers and fathers.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/699faca3-9dcb-444b-8940-0afb0abf9491?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

name: Kristi Mellum
subject: Stand up for public lands by telling your story! 📝
body:
https://secure.everyaction.com/DY1lYLtZ7Uaw8M0gzn9eKg2?contactdata=85nqIW70S8kyaiGP2VCHcC7UHP2m951EaQgIlOAJdiGyzGl5ybL4cLbP9dNheT3e0beB2P6QMdWqsEE5jS7rpvzwKCsq/WI84lJMWmkgobotzCZXLjxGkHhH2oarDWHB [https://secure.everyaction.com/DY1lYLtZ7Uaw8M0gzn9eKg2?contactdata=85nqIW70S8kyaiGP2VCHcC7UHP2m951EaQgIlOAJdiGyzGl5ybL4cLbP9dNheT3e0beB2P6QMdWqsEE5jS7rpvzwKCsq/WI84lJMWmkgobotzCZXLjxGkHhH2oarDWHB]
Hi Peter,
Summer vacations are coming to an end. Were you able to get outside this summer and enjoy our public lands? Whether or not you did, our public lands are under threat - most recently with the proposal to eliminate the Roadless Rule ( read more here [https://oregoncapitalchronicle.com/2025/08/28/trump-officials-plan-to-remove-protections-on-2-million-acres-of-national-forests-in-oregon/] ). Will you speak up for our public lands by telling your story? [https://secure.everyaction.com/DY1lYLtZ7Uaw8M0gzn9eKg2]
Tell your story [https://secure.everyaction.com/DY1lYLtZ7Uaw8M0gzn9eKg2?contactdata=85nqIW70S8kyaiGP2VCHcC7UHP2m951EaQgIlOAJdiGyzGl5ybL4cLbP9dNheT3e0beB2P6QMdWqsEE5jS7rpvzwKCsq/WI84lJMWmkgobotzCZXLjxGkHhH2oarDWHB]
Our public lands shape who we are, shelter our wildlife, and protect our ecosystems. They are so much more than a place to visit. The memories we create on our public lands are lasting. Read this story from one of our members, Lynn Norris, from Redmond, Oregon:
As many of my generation did, my family practically lived in our public lands and National parks. One place in particular was magical for me, White Sands New Mexico. We camped in the middle of glorious white dunes, looked at full moons shine on sparkling nights, and I formed a love of the land and all of nature. I also learned this place held horses that ranchers left when the government forced them to sell and used White Sands for military bombing practice. The book , ‘Nobody’s Horses’ explains their rescue. I love so many of our beautiful parks and lands, and know they must shine for us always.
Our public lands can’t speak up to our legislators. They need your voice and your story to remind those in power just how important these places are, and how much we care about them. Will you join Lynn in sharing your public lands story? [https://secure.everyaction.com/DY1lYLtZ7Uaw8M0gzn9eKg2]
Tell your story [https://secure.everyaction.com/DY1lYLtZ7Uaw8M0gzn9eKg2?contactdata=85nqIW70S8kyaiGP2VCHcC7UHP2m951EaQgIlOAJdiGyzGl5ybL4cLbP9dNheT3e0beB2P6QMdWqsEE5jS7rpvzwKCsq/WI84lJMWmkgobotzCZXLjxGkHhH2oarDWHB]
Your voice is important for our public lands, no matter if you’ve been able to visit one or not. Stories about why you want to protect our public lands or parks you’d like to visit are just as valuable as stories of your favorite memory on our public lands. There are no length requirements. Please share yours today! [https://secure.everyaction.com/DY1lYLtZ7Uaw8M0gzn9eKg2]
Thank you for taking a stand,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/Kristi.png]
Kristi Mellum (she/her)
OLCV Engagement Organizer, Central Oregon
P.S.: You can also give a gift now to support our work in protecting our public lands and national forests, because this fight isn’t over. Give now! [https://secure.everyaction.com/JxkJ6ys7PUGmqohXVRrhKg2]
https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv [https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv] https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org [https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org] https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F [https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F] https://x.com/OLCV [https://x.com/OLCV]

Oregon League of Conservation Voters
1110 SE Alder Suite 220
Portland, OR 97214
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/ReVeUSnqh0mAa9rjhFdl7A2?unsubscribedata=UqdAAFMV%2FKRWDJlUFNc4%2Bu3rRYUItGvenQ9QpA0QXaUvtMgVkxphQ9zMs7l3QihFVXLHCl0%2BQW4giEol%2BIot22SjNgJD96%2Bl2bp3MJhet7PE7fA2%2B3swCiFeWJmINDtYu1HUCzmDVBZYaglDEiR0T3%2B%2Bg3xCFQTNs1KwEh98f3wbtShU64r8gPFCpftBEBCFxG2J0ztDxsWzV%2BodH5uVlPzPhFlMQFE1ouZrB02xbkLzFdHbH9otBsFc9ezpiIPDNcVT4WW6Wr3nPgQscedBVIke6A8vs%2FsEJCfReCdvD61BrbKD%2FdIx4RWhT1%2Fz4M6OvRf1zGKOSoi91k8NvsdDse%2FRM8w%2FSHLQeLD9XQX96bOkqWbRVS4Dtg5avCTfyWbEwEAlmvlw7U4IIwHuys5p1lUtvsJml7PvjOorfpgacE4%3D .

name: Your signature is missing, DOTL
subject: Don’t let Trump END Social Security!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democracy on the Line PAC
body:
Take a stand now >>

|

| _**We can’t believe this!!!**_ **“Social Security Predicted to Run Out of Money Sooner Due to Trump Bill”** _**Peter, the Trump administration could seriously end Social Security if Democrats like you don’t intervene!**_

### Take Advantage of [our 15% off Sale on a Paid Subscription to Blue Amp While it Lasts!](https://substack.com/redirect/33c28c3e-1892-4d52-91b3-3463f218e33e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[I want 15% off!](https://substack.com/redirect/33c28c3e-1892-4d52-91b3-3463f218e33e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bc6429a4-b404-4691-b5b4-14ccff1cc1f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A whole lot of people concentrated on a very different act of gun violence today. So much so that many missed a uniquely despicable American tragedy that occurred yet again.
So I'm going to cover it, [another mass school shooting in the United States of America.](https://substack.com/redirect/068141f1-0d8b-44d2-8007-da5672aab8bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The second of these assaults on decency in the two to three weeks most kids have been back in school. The second offering to Moloch by our very own Morlocks, rabid, [dictatorial "men" lacking any empathy](https://substack.com/redirect/8052cf95-18ca-4035-b4e6-0ee31a81885a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who just can't be inconvenienced by background checks and such.
Today, it was Evergreen High School, 28 miles Southwest of Denver--an area that from Columbine to Aurora has suffered more than its fair share of these horrific tragedies.
> "We've received three victims, all minors from Evergreen High School. They are currently being actively treated at St. Anthony Hospital. We are a Level 1 Trauma Center and prepared for situations like this," said Kevin Cullinan, President, CEO of CommonSpirit St. Anthony Hospital. "Each of the victims that we have sustained gunshot wounds. They are all, all three victims are all either in the Emergency Department or operating rooms at this time."
> Law enforcement later confirmed that one of those three students is the suspect, and the scene at the school is now safe. They said a fourth student was also sent to the hospital with unknown injuries.
So, we have a whole new locale where our nation's kids were treated to a lifetime trauma by the GOP.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.JsGEJg_F7DORSsa-aA-stqVkqmBqXa4sonlV1JOgM4M?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=173315555)
Because congressional Republicans--who work for [a group of bratty billionaires](https://substack.com/redirect/0bf0f13a-25e9-4f5d-bb08-33df75413bb6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and the *we built bunkers and recycle our own urine* lobby who turn out in their primaries--and a confused, tiny-handed, [hated cumquat of a President](https://substack.com/redirect/20bfc100-d5d7-4f40-a855-911f38f66008?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), refuse to pass the gun laws every other advanced nation has.
And because Republicans in robes--i.e. The Sharia Wing of Opus Dei that performed a hostile takeover of our Supreme Court--willfully misinterpreted a quite simple definition of the very straight forward words "well regulated militia."
To them the quite obvious translation of this declaration is "any asshole with a few hundred bucks and a grudge." So, they turned guns into the number one killer of children in America. Though, they're [working hard to bring some competition from measles or smallpox](https://substack.com/redirect/94e603ec-0286-40df-a6eb-ecdfbc300c4a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
How proud they must all be.
And because of it, we regularly get treated to this, from the Jefferson County, Co area today:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/823d8e61-cb17-42ee-864b-9c3cbc978dac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
You may remember a whole, I dunno, two weeks ago, was it, we had little children--basically babies--murdered by gun in their classrooms in Minnesota.
At the time I made a video about it, where I released the exact same rant I would right now. You can watch that here:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d3d1d242-44f5-476d-9b48-7a50cf702cff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.JsGEJg_F7DORSsa-aA-stqVkqmBqXa4sonlV1JOgM4M?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=173315555)
In the end, the way to stop this disease--that by numbers as high as 90% Americans support passing measures to end-- is actually pretty easy.
Because the two political parties have made it simple for us to do the math.
One party post-Newtown, the Democratic Party, voted 97% _in favor of_ key measures to stop this sick blight on our society. While the other, Republicans, voted 97% _against_ these common sense reforms (yes, it really is this symmetrical).
So to summarize: [Vote every single fucking cowardly, cruel, corrupt Republican out of office](https://substack.com/redirect/7fe12730-712a-418e-b137-813c3f36de12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And we'll put a stop to this child-killing virus.
Because, as I said in some of my most viral tweets I ever posted, this:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c1f3e7da-fa47-40d2-a658-a45a834ec745?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

---|---|---
**Vote every single Republican out. And end the scourge of gun violence in this country.**

The Madness Must Stop | | | Sep 11 | | | | | | | | | | | | Take Advantage of | | ---|---|--- A whole lot of people concentrated on a very different act of gun violence today. So much so that many missed a uniquely despicable American tragedy that occurred yet again. So I'm going to cover it, The second of these assaults on decency in the two to three weeks most kids have been back in school. The second offering to Moloch by our very own Morlocks, rabid, who just can't be inconvenienced by background checks and such. Today, it was Evergreen High School, 28 miles Southwest of Denver--an area that from Columbine to Aurora has suffered more than its fair share of these horrific tragedies. "We've received three victims, all minors from Evergreen High School. They are currently being actively treated at St. Anthony Hospital. We are a Level 1 Trauma Center and prepared for situations like this," said Kevin Cullinan, President, CEO of CommonSpirit St. Anthony Hospital. "Each of the victims that we have sustained gunshot wounds. They are all, all three victims are all either in the Emergency Department or operating rooms at this time." Law enforcement later confirmed that one of those three students is the suspect, and the scene at the school is now safe. They said a fourth student was also sent to the hospital with unknown injuries. So, we have a whole new locale where our nation's kids were treated to a lifetime trauma by the GOP. Because congressional Republicans--who work for and the we built bunkers and recycle our own urine lobby who turn out in their primaries--and a confused, tiny-handed, , refuse to pass the gun laws every other advanced nation has. And because Republicans in robes--i.e. The Sharia Wing of Opus Dei that performed a hostile takeover of our Supreme Court--willfully misinterpreted a quite simple definition of the very straight forward words "well regulated militia." To them the quite obvious translation of this declaration is "any asshole with a few hundred bucks and a grudge." So, they turned guns into the number one killer of children in America. Though, they're . How proud they must all be. And because of it, we regularly get treated to this, from the Jefferson County, Co area today: You may remember a whole, I dunno, two weeks ago, was it, we had little children--basically babies--murdered by gun in their classrooms in Minnesota. At the time I made a video about it, where I released the exact same rant I would right now. You can watch that here: In the end, the way to stop this disease--that by numbers as high as 90% Americans support passing measures to end-- is actually pretty easy. Because the two political parties have made it simple for us to do the math. One party post-Newtown, the Democratic Party, voted 97% in favor of key measures to stop this sick blight on our society. While the other, Republicans, voted 97% against these common sense reforms (yes, it really is this symmetrical). So to summarize: . And we'll put a stop to this child-killing virus. Because, as I said in some of my most viral tweets I ever posted, this: | | ---|---|--- Continued ... | | ---|---|--- Vote every single Republican out. # [Our Children's Father, At Risk of Deportation](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=173138017&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTM4MDE3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc1NTk0NTUsImV4cCI6MTc2MDE1MTQ1NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.APwzbhoNbOE_4T3pEhOFAOt7bgS9YOUF9q3h4Qzpb9s)
### Our children's names mean Peace and Justice. Now those values are being tested as the U.S. government tries to deport their father into danger. Washington State must act. Here's how.
| | [Melissa Chaudhry](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---
| Sep 11
---
| [](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=173138017&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTM4MDE3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NzU1OTQ1NSwiZXhwIjoxNzYwMTUxNDU1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzI2MDExMSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.gObQiRLywN1n6aZvynkcbZco4A3x6DRHrPK0jGM6rYE&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

Friends,
Yesterday, we received word that the Respondent in my husband's 9th Circuit case has asked the court not to grant his Motion to Stay Removal.
It get a little technical, so bear with me. When our side filed the Motion to Stay Removal (MSR - the formal, legal request to be protected from deportation) on August 22nd, the day after he was taken, the Court acknowledged it received it that day.
The rules are, that if a MSR has been filed, then a stay is granted immediately, pending the Court's decision on the motion. Basically, once someone lets the Court know they're asking not to be deported, the default is to grant that protection, while the matter is being considered. This is a check on the abuse of deportation, which we all know is rampant.
In any case. That default, protective stay has been in effect since August 22nd, but the Court has _not_ ruled on the Motion to Stay Removal itself.
This filing by the Respondents asks the Court not to grant that motion - in effect, they're asking for permission to deport my husband - a decorate, disabled American veteran with two little kids, beloved by his community for over 25 years - as soon as possible. Make it make sense.
What it means for us is that instead of waiting months until the 9th Circuit decides on the merits of his appeal, we might only have weeks.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=173138017&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTM4MDE3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc1NTk0NTUsImV4cCI6MTc2MDE1MTQ1NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.APwzbhoNbOE_4T3pEhOFAOt7bgS9YOUF9q3h4Qzpb9s)
* * *
I often hear, "What about Pakistan? That's your husband's home country, wouldn't it be ok for him to go there?"
Well - here are the issues with Pakistan, from our perspective.
_First, in full disclosure: I 've been there. Spent 3 months with my husband's family in Lahore, plus a stunning midwinter trip up into the Himalayas. I love the country, wholeheartedly. I love the people, I really do. Generous, kind, hospitable to a fault. Beautiful place, truly. BUT:_
1. Pakistan has one of the worst histories of terrorist attacks in the region - mostly targeting Americans and those perceived to be helping them. My husband's US Army Veteran status makes him a bright neon target, for them. And his disability makes him, unfortunately, a sitting duck.
2. The government of Pakistan - since the ouster of democratically elected, beloved, Prime Minister Imran Khan (in what the vast majority of people believe was a US-driven coup, further inflaming anti-American sentiment...) - is very keen on appeasing Washington DC. And this is the same country that has a well-established history of conducting abductions and disappearances - like that of doctor and mother [Aafia Siddiqui](https://substack.com/redirect/708a62e0-3c49-4b9d-a994-db3563f56eff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) \- at the US's request. There are entities in that country that would be perfectly happy to do the FBI a favor and (kidnap, torture, imprison, kill...) someone who's been inconvenient to them, like my beloved husband.
3. The economy is in shambles. The military controls something like 80% of the profitable corporate entities, and the arable land; and the situation with international loans and the IMF is eviscerating the economy for normal people. Inflation has eroded the purchasing power of even well-educated Pakistanis, eg, doctors, like my nieces and nephew. Basic essentials are almost completely out of reach for almost everyone - makes for a very unstable and desperate society. Bounty hunting and kidnapping for ransom is on the rise.
4. Political opponents of the current administration in Pakistan (supporters of Imran Khan's party PTI, which is the "Pakistan Movement for Justice") have been surveilled, targeted, shot with live ammo during peaceful protests, and jailed as political prisoners without trial... by the thousands. Freedom of speech is ... shall we say, unreliable.
5. Pakistan - particularly the Punjab region, where my husband is from - is currently experiencing its worst flooding in over 40 years. _Three years ago_ , it ALSO experienced its worst flooding in decades. Entire portions of the country, tens of thousands of acres, underwater - which damages infrastructure, erodes transportation, and causes disease (mosquito-borne, water-borne, from open sewage - cholera, dysentery, etc, etc).
6. Medical care: very tough to come by, to international standards. And even harder for complex disabilities and interrelated healthcare needs (traumatic brain injury, broken back and neck, wheelchair, etc etc). His thyroid eye disease, if left untreated, causes irreversible blindness - and the treatment is incredibly expensive, hundreds of thousands of dollars here in the States.
7. Lack of wheelchair-accessible housing, infrastructure, sidewalks basically nonexistent... if you've ever tried to drive in a developing country, you know what it's like - if you haven't, imagine lemmings or a crowded salmon run, everywhere, all the time. And so on.

Tl;dr -- not ideal. Shall we say.
Downright dangerous and very possibly deadly, in fact.
That's what they're trying to expedite. Throwing my husband into all of that - without protection, without preparation, very possibly even without warning - is what they are formally, legally, in black and white, with no shame... asking the 9th Circuit for permission to do.
* * *
Our toddler daughter, Salma, asks where Baba is. When she's missing him, she asks to look at pictures on my phone of them together - her hugging him around the neck, him cradling her while she sleeps, etc. When she was little, she used to lean her little head into his hand and fall asleep like that. Now, when she's sad, she has me show her those photos, and she falls asleep that way.
I took Adil in to see him last night. This little 9 month old boy is growing into himself so confidently... he smiles at and charms absolutely everyone. His top two teeth just broke through, and he's getting stronger on his feet. When we meet Zahid at the tight concrete visitor booth, Adil bangs his little hands against the glass and shrieks excitedly to see his dad. Zahid smiles back with all the exuberant joyful father's love in his heart. I hold the phone handset to our son's little ear, and his eyes get big as he hears Zahid's voice. He gets curious about how this black plastic thing has his baba inside, and turns his head to try to chew on it. We find joy in the humor of it, as best we can.
For those who don't know, our children's names mean Peace and Justice.
It is difficult to overstate how deeply my husband and I believe in the principles that make, not just America, but any country and any civil society, great.
And it seems a timely moment to remember an old adage: _**If you want peace, work for justice.**_
* * *
I've spoken a lot with the media in the past three weeks. More than I ever have before in my life combined, including during my campaign for Congress last year.
What I'm learning, and I'll share with you, is that it's never the most compelling, interesting, or important bits of what I say that get quoted. It's never the real analysis that challenges the status quo. It's not the moments of heartbroken authenticity. It's not the fire or the fury; it's not the nuance and the detail.
It's the simple, digestible soundbite, that gets to fit the story they already want to tell. What story that is, depends on who owns them. I've had multiple instances of an interview being twisted to reinforce the government's smear campaign against my husband. It's a bitter pill.
That said, don't get me wrong -- I'm glad of, and grateful for, the coverage, and some [rare reporters](https://substack.com/redirect/bd058279-db59-4ea4-9882-72840987777d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) really do care and do well by us. Alhamdulillah for them, may Allah reward them for uplifting the truth.
The truth is that this really is the travesty it appears to be - and trust me, the more you learn of the detail and the layers of this 30 year saga (20 years in US courts), the more and more apparent it will become to you that Zahid is the victim here, in every way.
I wish it were otherwise, honestly. I wish I could say "yeah, he messed up as a kid, but he grew into a better man" and give his attackers some little bit of righteous ego-feeding self-satisfaction, before asking for their compassion and forgiveness. That would actually make it a _lot_ easier. People get awarded clemency and parole when they're duly apologetic. It's been held against Zahid - including in the shameful bit of "13-year-old-on-Twitter" slander from DHS - that he's not "apologetic" and doesn't "show remorse." How, exactly, is someone supposed to show remorse for something they haven't done?
I stand by his innocence and integrity until the day I die, because he has always been consistent to me, in the most intimate and vulnerable moments of our lives. The _first night we spent together_ , he kept me up late telling me this long story about something that happened decades ago in Australia, when a passenger gave him their passport as surety on a fare, and he tried to return it to the authorities but was accused of having stolen it, and how because he'd been beaten bloody many times and never got justice in court against his white passengers/assailants, he knew he wouldn't get justice if he tried to fight it, so he took a plea deal the way they told him to.
About the time a passenger left a credit card in the back seat of his taxi, and he tried to do the right thing and return it, only to have the person he was returning it to, pocket it, accuse him of stealing it, and take the reward for returning it himself... That credit card had been fraudulently used dozens of times, many of them _for taxi fares_ \- and why would my husband, who at the time was driving a taxi 12-16 hours/day, need to pay a taxi fare??
About how he even _tried_ to track down that conviction, only to be told by the Australian authorities that they didn't have anything on him, his record was clean... but he, in that naive abundance of innocence and integrity that is his particular specialty, pushed back and asked them to look again...then proactively, himself, brought that information to USCIS...
And about the time, later, in Yakima, when the judge denied him his day in court after learning who he intended to call as a witness -- the judge, who was on the opposite side of a major schism in the local church, who couldn't be impartial in that small of a town -- the judge rubber-stamped the government's version of the story, creating the case Chaudhry v Napolitano in 2010 that indicts my husband wrongly, but is in black and white in the legal record and most people, if they bother to look at all, will take that as gospel truth... despite the [many dozens of letters](https://substack.com/redirect/65896f65-2c6f-41e4-a3a6-00e97734ee25?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of support in the legal record from Senators Murray and Cantwell, state legislators from both sides of the aisle, the Sergeant _who recruited him_ , the Immigration Officer who first handled his case, clergy from four different faiths, academics, fellow veterans, family, friends, community members...all attesting to his integrity, principle, generosity, and value to their lives and our communities...
I didn't know why any of this was important, at the time. I was simply falling in love with the soul of my soul. But I soon learned more about the legal gnarls and knots this beautiful man was caught in. I know now that by telling me these stories that first night, he was preparing me for _this moment_. He wanted to be sure that if it ever came to pass that he couldn't speak for himself, and my voice had to stand, that I could say the truth fully and clearly to you, to the reporters, to ICE, to anyone and everyone -- that he is innocent, and he is wonderful, and we have nothing to hide.
That America would be far, far poorer, for losing him. And that the bare fact that America, in the form of the US Government, is trying... should show _all_ of us that our government doesn't stand for liberty and justice for all.
* * *
But we can.
When I ran for Congress, I emphasized this in every classroom where I was invited to speak. The US Government does not, truly, uphold liberty and justice for all. The principles that we are taught form the foundation of our country...don't. (Racism and exploitation, in fact, are much more central to the design of our social and economic systems. Time we get on fixin' that.)
So, what then do we do? It's a tough problem, and not just when you're 12, like those seventh-graders were. It's a tough problem to grapple with at any age, and all ages.
_And it 's the biggest opportunity of our time. _
We step up. We lead. We take those foundational principles into our own souls, and we make them the North Star of our own lives. And we organize with others who feel the same way. And we pressure our elected officials - not just at the federal level, but **very much** at the State level - to live up to them, in policy and practice, not just in press conferences.
We take personal responsibility for making
We claim the right and the moral duty to make America great... with integrity. WIth principle.
**With liberty and justice for all** \- no asterisks attached.
**With a government of, by, and for the people** \- _all_ the people.
**Upholding the inalienable rights of life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness** \- not letting private corporations profit at taxpayer expense from stripping dignity, belonging, and freedom from tens of thousands of contributive, hardworking people, with only the barest veneer of due process and legal recourse.
Washington State can do a lot to protect Washingtonians from abuse of power at the federal level. I'm working on policy ideas for how to do just that. Here's a starting point, and I hope you'll read it, and I hope you'll share it, and I hope you'll send it to your elected representatives _in Olympia_ , not just DC:

**" A Declaration of Interposition"**
When in the course of human events, it becomes manifest that federal powers no longer hold themselves bound by law, decency, proportion, humanity, or even the Constitution itself--when they protect the corrupt and the violent while targeting the innocent for imprisonment in concentration camps, when they desecrate the social compact on which this nation rests--then those who hold office in the States, and who have sworn an oath to defend the Constitution against all enemies foreign and domestic, are not only empowered but duty-bound to interpose, to withdraw cooperation from such actors, and to claim their rightful and God-given responsibility to protect democracy and the rule of law within their own borders.
Wherefore, the Great State of Washington declares that it shall:
**1\. Cease Voluntary Cooperation with Abusive Federal Agencies.**
• Suspend voluntary data-sharing between state agencies and federal immigration authorities, unless explicitly compelled by judicial order.
• Bar state and local law enforcement from participation in joint task forces that target Washington residents on the basis of religion, ethnicity, or immigration status.
**2\. Protect State Revenues and Institutions.**
• Place federal tax withholdings collected within Washington into escrow pending constitutional compliance audits, ensuring Washington's wealth does not subsidize lawless persecution.
• Direct state pension funds, investments, and banking functions away from federal debt instruments, shifting instead toward state-chartered financial institutions.
**3\. Assert State Sovereignty over Infrastructure and Commerce.**
• Deny use of state highways, ports, and airports for federal actions that contravene constitutional rights.
• Review and, where permissible, revoke contracts and leases that allow federal agencies to occupy state or municipal real estate for the purpose of unlawful detention or discriminatory enforcement.
• Condition business licenses and registrations on compliance with inalienable rights--life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness--as articulated in our founding documents.
**4\. Strengthen Interstate Compacts for the Public Good.**
• Form binding compacts with sister states to preserve free commerce, healthcare access, climate action, and freedom of movement, thereby insulating our people from federal neglect or abuse.
• Establish joint state protections for immigrants, refugees, and targeted communities, ensuring that no one within our borders is deprived of safety or dignity.
**5\. Defend Human Rights at the State Level.**
• Enact laws guaranteeing that immigrants and refugees in Washington are afforded due process, housing stability, healthcare access, and freedom from arbitrary detention.
• Establish independent state-level commissions to investigate and, where appropriate, prosecute federal actors who commit crimes against residents of Washington within state jurisdiction.
For the truth remains: the sovereignty of the United States resides not in federal agencies, but _in the people_ , and in the several States who serve them. When the federal government departs from its own compact, the responsibility falls to us to sustain the Union by guarding the liberty of our people.
**Therefore, let it be known** : the State of Washington will fulfill its duty--_not in defiance of the Constitution, but in its defense._ And in doing so, we reaffirm the most ancient of truths: that governments derive their just powers from the consent of the governed, and that when power is abused, it is the right and obligation of free people, and of free states, to set limits, restore balance, and protect the inalienable rights endowed by the Creator upon all.
* * *
Here's how you find and contact your [elected officials](https://substack.com/redirect/5da4527d-6ce7-44be-9a82-74f0eae5dee0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Olympia.
And here's the phone number and [email](https://substack.com/redirect/74bf5770-b76b-457d-9969-d25bb3fe4273?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the State Treasurer's office (Mike Pelliciotti): 360-902-9000
And here's Governor Ferguson's office: 360-902-4111
And here's Attorney General [Nick Brown](https://substack.com/redirect/eea3fd50-0e76-4b5a-9c8d-0c52ca06dd9c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): 360-753-6200
Call them. Email them. Talk to them. Tell them in no uncertain terms that you expect them to rise to the occasion with principle and courage to protect **all Washingtonians**.
History has its eyes on us.
* * *
If you've read this far, thank you.
There are ways you can support our family, and this mission.
1. Please share and amplify the media coverage that has done the most justice to this situation. Best right now is Northwest Asian Weekly's [piece from this morning](https://substack.com/redirect/bd058279-db59-4ea4-9882-72840987777d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). If you see bad coverage, or negative comments, and you have the bandwidth to engage, please do. This is a war for public opinion more than anything else, even before it's a legal war. We've seen public pressure change outcomes before (think Mahmoud Khalil). Our family is counting on you. Thank you
2. If you're so moved, here's are three ways to support our family:
1. [GiveInKind](https://substack.com/redirect/2c81ffe5-94a1-43dc-a499-eccb893c8f56?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) \- if you're local, and you'd like to cook a meal or help us directly, this is how.
2. [GoFundMe](https://substack.com/redirect/d62f0962-3fa2-43b8-a9fd-4eb3b67e76e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) \- here's how you can chip in financially, as we navigate the fierce legal battle, parenting two little kids, the likely loss of Zahid's disability compensation, and the terrifying possibility of his deportation abroad.
3. [Petition](https://substack.com/redirect/e3340631-3caa-49c5-bc1b-c361c84495b4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) \- stand up and be counted, showing that you know how much of a travesty this is.

Stay fierce. Stay kind. Keep going.

Here's how. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Our children's names mean Peace and Justice. Now those values are being tested as the U.S. government tries to deport their father into danger. Washington State must act. Here's how. | | | Sep 11 | | | | | | | | | | | | Friends, Yesterday, we received word that the Respondent in my husband's 9th Circuit case has asked the court not to grant his Motion to Stay Removal. It get a little technical, so bear with me. When our side filed the Motion to Stay Removal (MSR - the formal, legal request to be protected from deportation) on August 22nd, the day after he was taken, the Court acknowledged it received it that day. The rules are, that if a MSR has been filed, then a stay is granted immediately, pending the Court's decision on the motion. Basically, once someone lets the Court know they're asking not to be deported, the default is to grant that protection, while the matter is being considered. This is a check on the abuse of deportation, which we all know is rampant. In any case. That default, protective stay has been in effect since August 22nd, but the Court has not ruled on the Motion to Stay Removal itself. This filing by the Respondents asks the Court not to grant that motion - in effect, they're asking for permission to deport my husband - a decorate, disabled American veteran with two little kids, beloved by his community for over 25 years - as soon as possible. Make it make sense. What it means for us is that instead of waiting months until the 9th Circuit decides on the merits of his appeal, we might only have weeks. I often hear, "What about Pakistan? That's your husband's home country, wouldn't it be ok for him to go there?" Well - here are the issues with Pakistan, from our perspective. First, in full disclosure: I 've been there. Spent 3 months with my husband's family in Lahore, plus a stunning midwinter trip up into the Himalayas. I love the country, wholeheartedly. I love the people, I really do. Generous, kind, hospitable to a fault. Beautiful place, truly. BUT: 1. Pakistan has one of the worst histories of terrorist attacks in the region - mostly targeting Americans and those perceived to be helping them. My husband's US Army Veteran status makes him a bright neon target, for them. And his disability makes him, unfortunately, a sitting duck. 2. The government of Pakistan - since the ouster of democratically elected, beloved, Prime Minister Imran Khan (in what the vast majority of people believe was a US-driven coup, further inflaming anti-American sentiment...) - is very keen on appeasing Washington DC. And this is the same country that has a well-established history of conducting abductions and disappearances - like that of doctor and mother \- at the US's request. There are entities in that country that would be perfectly happy to do the FBI a favor and (kidnap, torture, imprison, kill...) someone who's been inconvenient to them, like my beloved husband. 3. The economy is in shambles. The military controls something like 80% of the profitable corporate entities, and the arable land; and the situation with international loans and the IMF is eviscerating the economy for normal people. Inflation has eroded the purchasing power of even well-educated Pakistanis, eg, doctors, like my nieces and nephew. Basic essentials are almost completely out of reach for almost everyone - makes for a very unstable and desperate society. Bounty hunting and kidnapping for ransom is on the rise. 4. Political opponents of the current administration in Pakistan (supporters of Imran Khan's party PTI, which is the "Pakistan Movement for Justice") have been surveilled, targeted, shot with live ammo during peaceful protests, and jailed as political prisoners without trial... by the thousands. Freedom of speech is ... shall we say, unreliable. 5. Pakistan - particularly the Punjab region, where my husband is from - is currently experiencing its worst flooding in over 40 years. Three years ago , it ALSO experienced its worst flooding in decades. Entire portions of the country, tens of thousands of acres, underwater - which damages infrastructure, erodes transportation, and causes disease (mosquito-borne, water-borne, from open sewage - cholera, dysentery, etc, etc). 6. Medical care: very tough to come by, to international standards. And even harder for complex disabilities and interrelated healthcare needs (traumatic brain injury, broken back and neck, wheelchair, etc etc). His thyroid eye disease, if left untreated, causes irreversible blindness - and the treatment is incredibly expensive, hundreds of thousands of dollars here in the States. 7. Lack of wheelchair-accessible housing, infrastructure, sidewalks basically nonexistent... if you've ever tried to drive in a developing country, you know what it's like - if you haven't, imagine lemmings or a crowded salmon run, everywhere, all the time. And so on. Tl;dr -- not ideal. Shall we say. Downright dangerous and very possibly deadly, in fact. That's what they're trying to expedite. Throwing my husband into all of that - without protection, without preparation, very possibly even without warning - is what they are formally, legally, in black and white, with no shame... asking the 9th Circuit for permission to do. Our toddler daughter, Salma, asks where Baba is. When she's missing him, she asks to look at pictures on my phone of them together - her hugging him around the neck, him cradling her while she sleeps, etc. When she was little, she used to lean her little head into his hand and fall asleep like that. Now, when she's sad, she has me show her those photos, and she falls asleep that way. I took Adil in to see him last night. This little 9 month old boy is growing into himself so confidently... he smiles at and charms absolutely everyone. His top two teeth just broke through, and he's getting stronger on his feet. When we meet Zahid at the tight concrete visitor booth, Adil bangs his little hands against the glass and shrieks excitedly to see his dad. Zahid smiles back with all the exuberant joyful father's love in his heart. I hold the phone handset to our son's little ear, and his eyes get big as he hears Zahid's voice. He gets curious about how this black plastic thing has his baba inside, and turns his head to try to chew on it. We find joy in the humor of it, as best we can. For those who don't know, our children's names mean Peace and Justice. It is difficult to overstate how deeply my husband and I believe in the principles that make, not just America, but any country and any civil society, great. And it seems a timely moment to remember an old adage: If you want peace, work for justice. I've spoken a lot with the media in the past three weeks. More than I ever have before in my life combined, including during my campaign for Congress last year. What I'm learning, and I'll share with you, is that it's never the most compelling, interesting, or important bits of what I say that get quoted. It's never the real analysis that challenges the status quo. It's not the moments of heartbroken authenticity. It's not the fire or the fury; it's not the nuance and the detail. It's the simple, digestible soundbite, that gets to fit the story they already want to tell. What story that is, depends on who owns them. I've had multiple instances of an interview being twisted to reinforce the government's smear campaign against my husband. It's a bitter pill. That said, don't get me wrong -- I'm glad of, and grateful for, the coverage, and some really do care and do well by us. Alhamdulillah for them, may Allah reward them for uplifting the truth. The truth is that this really is the travesty it appears to be - and trust me, the more you learn of the detail and the layers of this 30 year saga (20 years in US courts), the more and more apparent it will become to you that Zahid is the victim here, in every way. I wish it were otherwise, honestly. I wish I could say "yeah, he messed up as a kid, but he grew into a better man" and give his attackers some little bit of righteous ego-feeding self-satisfaction, before asking for their compassion and forgiveness. That would actually make it a lot easier. People get awarded clemency and parole when they're duly apologetic. It's been held against Zahid - including in the shameful bit of "13-year-old-on-Twitter" slander from DHS - that he's not "apologetic" and doesn't "show remorse." How, exactly, is someone supposed to show remorse for something they haven't done? I stand by his innocence and integrity until the day I die, because he has always been consistent to me, in the most intimate and vulnerable moments of our lives. The first night we spent together , he kept me up late telling me this long story about something that happened decades ago in Australia, when a passenger gave him their passport as surety on a fare, and he tried to return it to the authorities but was accused of having stolen it, and how because he'd been beaten bloody many times and never got justice in court against his white passengers/assailants, he knew he wouldn't get justice if he tried to fight it, so he took a plea deal the way they told him to. Assert State Sovereignty over Infrastructure and Commerce. • Deny use of state highways, ports, and airports for federal actions that contravene constitutional rights. • Review and, where permissible, revoke contracts and leases that allow federal agencies to occupy state or municipal real estate for the purpose of unlawful detention or discriminatory enforcement. • Condition business licenses and registrations on compliance with inalienable rights--life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness--as articulated in our founding documents. 4\. Strengthen Interstate Compacts for the Public Good. • Form binding compacts with sister states to preserve free commerce, healthcare access, climate action, and freedom of movement, thereby insulating our people from federal neglect or abuse. • Establish joint state protections for immigrants, refugees, and targeted communities, ensuring that no one within our borders is deprived of safety or dignity. 5\. Defend Human Rights at the State Level. • Enact laws guaranteeing that immigrants and refugees in Washington are afforded due process, housing stability, healthcare access, and freedom from arbitrary detention. • Establish independent state-level commissions to investigate and, where appropriate, prosecute federal actors who commit crimes against residents of Washington within state jurisdiction. For the truth remains: the sovereignty of the United States resides not in federal agencies, but in the people , and in the several States who serve them. When the federal government departs from its own compact, the responsibility falls to us to sustain the Union by guarding the liberty of our people. Therefore, let it be known : the State of Washington will fulfill its duty--not in defiance of the Constitution, but in its defense. And in doing so, we reaffirm the most ancient of truths: that governments derive their just powers from the consent of the governed, and that when power is abused, it is the right and obligation of free people, and of free states, to set limits, restore balance, and protect the inalienable rights endowed by the Creator upon all. Here's how you find and contact your in Olympia. And here's the phone number and for the State Treasurer's office (Mike Pelliciotti): 360-902-9000 And here's Governor Ferguson's office: 360-902-4111 And here's Attorney General : 360-753-6200 Call them. Email them. Talk to them. Tell them in no uncertain terms that you expect them to rise to the occasion with principle and courage to protect all Washingtonians. History has its eyes on us. If you've read this far, thank you. There are ways you can support our family, and this mission. 1. Please share and amplify the media coverage that has done the most justice to this situation. Best right now is Northwest Asian Weekly's . If you see bad coverage, or negative comments, and you have the bandwidth to engage, please do. This is a war for public opinion more than anything else, even before it's a legal war. We've seen public pressure change outcomes before (think Mahmoud Khalil). Our family is counting on you. Thank you 2. If you're so moved, here's are three ways to support our family: 1. \- if you're local, and you'd like to cook a meal or help us directly, this is how. 2. \- here's how you can chip in financially, as we navigate the fierce legal battle, parenting two little kids, the likely loss of Zahid's disability compensation, and the terrifying possibility of his deportation abroad. 3. \- stand up and be counted, showing that you know how much of a travesty this is. Stay fierce. Stay kind. Keep going. | | ---|---|--- Love, Melissa You 're currently a free subscriber to "It's a Matter of Integrity" by Melissa Chaudhry. To support the mission and access the full experience, upgrade your subscription. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Melissa Chaudhry 6727 Martin Luther King Jr Way S, Suite M, #1117 Seattle, WA 98118 ? ?

subject: Senate President Carson Statement On Passing Of Charlie Kirk

| | | |
---
| |
---
| Senate President Sharon Carson (R-Londonderry) released the following statement today after hearing about Charlie Kirk’s passing: “I am heartbroken to learn of the shooting that took Charlie Kirk’s life during a campus event in Utah. Our thoughts and prayers are with his wife, family, and the Turning Point community he helped build. While details are limited, what we do know is that political violence is an attack on every American. It will not silence lawful speech, open debate, or the civic engagement Charlie encouraged in young people since he founded Turning Point as a teenager. Charlie often said at the start of his events, ‘If you disagree with me, please come to the front.’ He invited dissent and encouraged all to disagree without being disagreeable. Charlie often urged students to stand up for their ideas with courage and to answer arguments with argument, not anger. The way we honor that legacy is to reject violence, protect free expression, and meet one another in the arena of ideas. Now is a time for prayer, reflection, and an absolute rejection of violence and the rhetoric that fuels it.”
---

name: The Flyover
subject: Charlie Kirk Killed, New Richest Man, and Ancient Martian Life
body:
Charlie Kirk, a prominent conservative activist and founder of Turning Point USA, was [fatally shot Wednesday while speaking](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LEO2yAQxvGnwdtFBkMIg4cu3tK1o0XgEl-DIYKzI7995apv0PlOf-n3_brPd0-J8uunX3HkZ5kzfltCZqxzWHxNhG-q7zelhDHjt1JYcFa9MtA7kHKOntIx6y6OT0SFQ4ejtMaamxnc0C2jtkYH64wy7qaD1W64WRmU9Q_trQ6xo_Gs9U5K2ffOqMszBI_XQXprrld3i0L3GIlLvfwulHnBZzrKjvUSytqlcWH-NDH8EGoSasrHpzQ-T0JNZ1ioqXdCTbIXatoanD6hpn82OHHwKa3RIx3Avr6QMQJlaEspTPkFnmFjv8DuU8IDtkw71kZ8AO6YGeJWz7eFGvgVKwWfIZQVHz68gctWhZq6OsbPl1KidrVC96_VU_oL4P9afR_VnwAAAP__NviYVw) at Utah Valley University in Orem.

# Thursday, September 11, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 2001, [a terrorist attack in New York City](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2OpDAQxfHTmMzIn4ADB5uQ9aYbopJdtGsxuGWsRtx-1KO5wcQ_6T3p_--xPIAyHc-_sKNva1kOvM6MrWFdQoKaCTeq20Y5YzzwqhQSLkooy4XjUi4RKN-L6aJfERXqDr0c7Wgnq53ukgc3DU6baI2FyQwKpXFGrHqatDYijh35z5pwUkohnFX9GgLgoCWMdhjcFJkRGKmV2v8vdLSEa77LG2sfyt5ln1p7nUz_YWpmar6uq090tlLvjzM1t0Qnj3BzOviPMDVDaxA2Xg4OO1YK0FUfXxflTOcwMiOeO1D-vmi_6vL26isAAP__EqJ3Ig) destroyed the World Trade Center's Twin Towers, claiming nearly 3,000 innocent lives and forever changing the U.S. Today, we report on the heartbreaking loss of Charlie Kirk, who was shot and killed while speaking at Utah Valley University. A husband, father, and leading voice in the conservative movement, Kirk was doing what he’d long done—speaking to young Americans—when his life was taken. Our hearts and prayers are with his family and friends. ## Charlie Kirk Killed at College Event
Charlie Kirk, a prominent conservative activist and founder of Turning Point USA, was [fatally shot Wednesday while speaking](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LEO2yAQxvGnwdtFBkMIg4cu3tK1o0XgEl-DIYKzI7995apv0PlOf-n3_brPd0-J8uunX3HkZ5kzfltCZqxzWHxNhG-q7zelhDHjt1JYcFa9MtA7kHKOntIx6y6OT0SFQ4ejtMaamxnc0C2jtkYH64wy7qaD1W64WRmU9Q_trQ6xo_Gs9U5K2ffOqMszBI_XQXprrld3i0L3GIlLvfwulHnBZzrKjvUSytqlcWH-NDH8EGoSasrHpzQ-T0JNZ1ioqXdCTbIXatoanD6hpn82OHHwKa3RIx3Avr6QMQJlaEspTPkFnmFjv8DuU8IDtkw71kZ8AO6YGeJWz7eFGvgVKwWfIZQVHz68gctWhZq6OsbPl1KidrVC96_VU_oL4P9afR_VnwAAAP__NviYVw) at Utah Valley University in Orem. The shooter remains at large and a manhunt is underway. [Police released a suspect](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L-O4yAQx_GnwR0RDP5HQXENXa690sIwxHMhxgKSKG-_8mrfYOuRvprP7991uTpKtN_-ugeaFvOy47smbA3L4jdXEuGdyv1OKWHY8V3Ib7iAgIELzaVcgqP0WfoumIgIqDo0chqmYR6UVt1m1jl4PfcrrGNUEueh7908OR2iVy6i6sicNaGllELoAS7Re4ejkm4axlHPgfUCA7VcLv8z7W3DmD75heXi86NLZmvtqEz9YWAZ2P1z5NrOEwN7hhlYoRlYKRjYZ-Wnj4H9sfETx-uWc6P9xuuzHugbd6VgbRg47fzZ3MbjSgxsV0w43pQS1XFivbg9HKXvN9qvtnsZ-AoAAP__2RKDEQ) they took into custody Wednesday night, the second 'person of interest' they questioned and then let go in the wake of the shooting. Kirk, 31, was hit in the neck by a single round fired from about 200 yards away, triggering panic and a campus lockdown. The shot rang out while Kirk was [responding to a question about violence](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuhDAQBNCvgc4IGwy4cJHmukubEi32-tiwB8j4sO7vI6L8QfrRjN583cc7ENP6-IQn2hS2ccV8MKaEcXQzRCZcKC4LMaNfMUdyM46qVlrURkg5eiB-j23pbUBU2JRoZa97PejGNOVslWuC8YMPg-9AG3BT6Ac3dUMAN7WyLclebbWRUta10aoKzgF2jYRed50ZfNHW6CltsfreaE0zBn5vJ8bKbc-S7ZzSfhTNR6FuhbrlnKuLkBGXK1Co2x9DXA4RcX9NTA5WccxbEq8EsziBGd-C6UTx2j0kPISSqh-MLqP1eyZmOrq-aOvHE4h_l9O_7jqt-gkAAP__TlGBLQ) during a Q&A session under a tent outside the school's Losee Center. A prominent ally of President Trump, Kirk was known for his influence among young people: [Turning Point USA](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykz72OhCAUxfGngW4MHwJaUGxjN9tuSRCu411RDDJj5u037itMfU5-yf_n7u4eE26Pb7-CrVN2G5xHglqhuDD7khAWLMuCKUHc4CwYZnCCCXVj_Y1zFz2mt2tptBOAAEnBcqOM6pTsJZ2taRmHsdM-KMGMjIb7bmQtdCDGSXFN0V4a6znnjPVKNFMIHrTk3iit-y6SlkHEmkvzm3GrM0zpnV9QmpBXmuxc634Q-UXEQMRQ9-fhr4WIYQWo1z0_twiFiIEWG_cTU8JDG9Kyx-ox_TP1o_aXFX8BAAD__xdJbXc) advocated for conservative politics on high school and university campuses. His killing drew [bipartisan condemnation](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L-O4yAQx_Gngc4RfwyGguIad7n2SgvBEOaMTYTZWHn7laN9g-1GGukrfX7_7svdY8H98ddv4Hqqyw7nUaB3aEvIvhWEFdu6YikQdzgbhgyLYEINzA6cL9FjeS8jjS4BCJAUHJ_UpIySVtLsUjTBS21TMsIIw3wI4HWISicjUlAU3VVjlnPOmFXilkLwoCX3k9LamkhGBhF7bbf_FfeeIZV3fUG7hbrR4nLvz4PIP0TMRMw9Q8ZSrh8Rc64bXKDP-XUAEbMarbZyHIo_N79CO4YGPnSs-_DjHS4wETNtLj5PLAUPPZGRPTaPnzDtv1rq5cR3AAAA___RAn30) and reignited concern over rising political violence in the U.S. He leaves behind a wife and two young children.
## NATO Scrambles as Russian Drones Enter Poland
Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk said [19 Russian drones crossed into Poland’s](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL_GZI1sA8M4cHAJ2V56Ieq1m6WPHhvZ3kHz96e5X9i0VKp6en8-1g9k4fT1Gx_k25bXRFcVao3KGnYswnRwOQ4WoZjoKhx2Wq22E2gHxqwRWV7r2EW_EVkaOvJmnubpPg1u6Hav3WQmY-2MNsRgxk8Tt-3uPl2Ms8G76di_17QzxmjtJttvISDdBoPzdLu5e1Sjpsgtl_5v5tR22uSVn1T6kB-d-L21s6rhl7KLsst1Xb3gWahW6r_rO8lForLL-0XZRTtlF6OVXc4smCK073qAcYBc6omB4MlZsHFOFQqFXCJFuAguFgFOz3wQYGkchGCEvEHbCRK2DK0QtpeyS1d8PN99rrdZjfrrgSz_cduPHD-9_RcAAP__7h2Q_Q) airspace overnight, marking a serious breach of NATO territory. NATO fighter jets and helicopters were scrambled in response, and [Warsaw invoked Article 4](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uwyAYAJ8Gb0T8GGMGhi7e0rWjReBz_DVfwAKaKG9fpa_Q-aQ76b7O6zkgYb5-hjv4vpU1w7MR9A51jXuohHDDershEaQMz4pxh1UJZbhwXMo1BaTXOg7JbwAK9ABeWmPNbLTTw-6lmS5ulHrWIFRQY3SbNVY4fUk2Cm0H9G-bcFJKIZxRpy3GAJOWwZppcnNio4CEvdTTd8Hcd9joVR5QT7HcB_J770dj-oOphaml77Aj0ZsxtRyFML6YWjB3qDl0LDkQU4sZ3SSmmefQC6_QjpIb8PrTGobMUy0ZeKgdIwEfmVqG6tPxRCJsk2WjuN4D_kWG_q9rD69-AwAA__9WsYDr) of the alliance treaty to consult allies, but stopped short of activating Article 5, which would require collective defense. The incursions, which began late Tuesday and continued into Wednesday morning, followed a massive strike on Ukraine and included drones entering from Belarus. Poland borders both Ukraine and Belarus. The [Kremlin has downplayed](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2unDAQhZ_GdEb-WX5cuNiriG7TpIiUBjn2ABPMGBnnWvv2EXmF243m0zk6-n6-5pfDiLR-dwfYsqSZoF4RSoE8-83liLBj3neMEQJBzeg3mJVQHReGSzkHh_E9P5pgFwAFugErh27oxk4b3WxW_9YiBOmNFyIYMUoxCmOMhn4J3eBVg_ZuE0ZKKYTpVLt476DX0g1d35sxsIeAgCXl9k9CKhss8Z0-Ibc-HU20WynnxfSTqYmpqdbaHhfdjKkJiP-9mJrOFLGgv8_1jtIBVJia9gxHROIBfESCi5fEfTpuyhPxkBMBR_IY7g8SP1N0FJiaXObPp3yt9eMX01PyGJj-9nGLhHr9gHw22YazYox49QN7iPVwGP9PLl_y_GnVvwAAAP__Oc6TqQ) the incident, deferring to its defense ministry and denying any intent to provoke. The U.S. ambassador to NATO reaffirmed that Washington would “defend every inch” of allied territory.
## Ellison Overtakes Musk as Richest Man
Oracle chairman Larry Ellison, 81, [topped Bloomberg’s billionaire index](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D3u4yAUBPDT2J0tPmwcCopt3GXbLS0Mj_DWzxABGyu3XyVX-PejGf3mz327WyRMj9_2BNNC3hJclaA1KJuLthDCgeU4kAh8gqugi7AJJuaB6YHzzVuk9zb13gQAAbIHw5d5mW-z1LKPRgUbZinF4padOa33fVJMKrlbxRywW4_m08Y055wxPYsxOGdBSW6XWSl9893EwGPLZfybMbUIgd75BWV0-ezJxNaetZO_OrF2Yg2YbHIwvm3M-ZPoxPohdWI9_9VjoFyhDq7kKw1XLuTr8CXVNvBFKiEV12NsJ_XF-OeFRFjV0k3scVqk72T70U8vI_4HAAD__z_WfLI) Wednesday after a historic surge in Oracle’s stock added roughly $100 billion to his net worth, more than any person has ever made in a single day. clean_body: Charlie Kirk, a prominent conservative activist and founder of Turning Point USA, was at Utah Valley University in Orem. | | | | | | Thursday, September 11, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 2001, destroyed the World Trade Center's Twin Towers, claiming nearly 3,000 innocent lives and forever changing the U.S. Today, we report on the heartbreaking loss of Charlie Kirk, who was shot and killed while speaking at Utah Valley University. A husband, father, and leading voice in the conservative movement, Kirk was doing what he’d long done—speaking to young Americans—when his life was taken. Our hearts and prayers are with his family and friends. If you’re worried about trusting Wall Street or the government with your future, here’s a little-known way to take control of your retirement savings on your own terms with today's sponsor, | Charlie Kirk Killed at College Event Charlie Kirk, a prominent conservative activist and founder of Turning Point USA, was at Utah Valley University in Orem. The shooter remains at large and a manhunt is underway. they took into custody Wednesday night, the second 'person of interest' they questioned and then let go in the wake of the shooting. Kirk, 31, was hit in the neck by a single round fired from about 200 yards away, triggering panic and a campus lockdown. The shot rang out while Kirk was during a Q&A session under a tent outside the school's Losee Center. A prominent ally of President Trump, Kirk was known for his influence among young people: advocated for conservative politics on high school and university campuses. His killing drew and reignited concern over rising political violence in the U.S. He leaves behind a wife and two young children. NATO Scrambles as Russian Drones Enter Poland Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk said airspace overnight, marking a serious breach of NATO territory. NATO fighter jets and helicopters were scrambled in response, and of the alliance treaty to consult allies, but stopped short of activating Article 5, which would require collective defense. The incursions, which began late Tuesday and continued into Wednesday morning, followed a massive strike on Ukraine and included drones entering from Belarus. Poland borders both Ukraine and Belarus. The the incident, deferring to its defense ministry and denying any intent to provoke. The U.S. ambassador to NATO reaffirmed that Washington would “defend every inch” of allied territory. Ellison Overtakes Musk as Richest Man Oracle chairman Larry Ellison, 81, Wednesday after a historic surge in Oracle’s stock added roughly $100 billion to his net worth, more than any person has ever made in a single day. Gov. Greg Abbott issued an executive order yesterday to [ban the sale of THC products](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAQhp8GOwywuGhBsY3V7eW6Kw3KoCQIHozr7ttf3OSe4Nr5ki_z_d_34W588HH-NCtofLo0RDhKAETIAy7TlpPdJyyjiRGsS3n1MeVBMNFQ1lHOB2t8eA2ysnpktuMVaK4a1bStbFW1aCZH09pGcQFSdVZY1snWuQtXUtjL2FVenzLWcc4540LWjYOrahwz3ag455JIBtZjyjUugPA0xYVXekCup7RWQS-IWyGXGxE9Ef1xHPVa4smI6CHSvRDRrynCi4h-SutmoofzNmeYqRnHhEh9KTsUmrKFTM9WH2eKy0T_-qlLmb7bCy2AePKEC2Sa9_DWmUxvN35fPn6-qqztdvgQfLkqItm8Gh_e3-L_Nn5o8RsAAP__yWmTkw) to minors and require retailers to check buyers' IDs. The move comes after Abbott vetoed an outright ban on the products and the legislature failed to pass a bill that would further regulate them.

# Thursday, September 11, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1842, a force of 1,200 Mexican soldiers led by Adrian Woll [captured San Antonio](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGO4yAUgOHT4M4WEBybgmIbd9l2S-sZHjEK5kXwNk5uP5q5wlzg_6Xv3229Qcqp3P_CgY7fkdaCZ8vIjHXl3T8rhf-e2walYIhUj1SorlrqsZe2V2oNkPJnNV1wmwxWdejUNE7jPJt56nZnI0hrTPDxOscQ0UDY0Hir_MXKGGKX3HdMWqWUkkqbYYx4ncYowW6TUsoIIzEkpjrwjoxvaDF_6IV18HR02e3MzyYuf4RehF7O8xy47UAlp4ID1bvQyw4lbEQPoRcsXBM2oZcGGQL1viI--g2YM_YUu-rC80w5p3adhJH3A1L-OfHvfF5OfwUAAP__bmB8oQ). Woll and his men were expelled a week later. Today marks 24 years since the terrorist attacks on September 11, 2001. The George W. Bush Presidential Center at SMU provides a solemn [look back](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGuqzAQRVdjOpDHGAyFi9_Q5bevRAYPMJLByJ5AsvunZAuvu9XVOefnMT4cBTrW_25Hy68ljgfeOSAzppG3-UzRP2fOkzsO9EtMOx0xjUqqppR9CTB6R-E96sLbSfoeCrRgGtN0ne5Msdl2krWD3pu5xt5g61vd9Q58J-ta-U4XZD9nsgcAkKB01SzYmmaRrp8MAGihJXrimCrekPHl8hLe8cJUzXEvgt2Yzyzqf0INQg33fVcrxrTiPT3zFmhKLr2rNV5CDfg6Q0z4XRtNxFmo4WNR8oZlZsRQxqV0Oyaa3VEmzDFcKFS9OzqKZP15UwiUWyO0XHdH4QvBf0t3WfUbAAD__2lahJY) at that horrific day. Today's sponsor is [Roundhouse Provisions](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOqyAYxvGrwa3mBVFgYDhJ4xkaO5ylo0F5qSQoBrC2d3_S7xa-_ZfnSf6PYRyMD3573s2KurxdHDc8c8BSMI1lmfcU7TGXPJltQ-tiWv0W08iAtRdQF0pHa3z4jLyyegKraIWaila0UnIpqkUbkFxN6NjUWDWjE2CUcqYxwCRHmCuvv2OgKKUUKON167ATrQOjJkEp5YQDWl9iqsuCBd8mu_CJL0z1HNcq6KWUPZPmD2E9Yf15nnWyz5yPff8Cwvp_f8X9fiWsb4bbbXi0X9j0h7ekuUreEdblY6KkuXYSoEra7qcPwedOEA7P1fjw81R-1-el2f8AAAD__4N9dtM). If you’ve ever wondered why the Baby Boomer generation seemed healthier and more energetic in their 30s than people today, [Roundhouse Provisions](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOqyAYxvGrwa3mBVFgYDhJ4xkaO5ylo0F5qSQoBrC2d3_S7xa-_ZfnSf6PYRyMD3573s2KurxdHDc8c8BSMI1lmfcU7TGXPJltQ-tiWv0W08iAtRdQF0pHa3z4jLyyegKraIWaila0UnIpqkUbkFxN6NjUWDWjE2CUcqYxwCRHmCuvv2OgKKUUKON167ATrQOjJkEp5YQDWl9iqsuCBd8mu_CJL0z1HNcq6KWUPZPmD2E9Yf15nnWyz5yPff8Cwvp_f8X9fiWsb4bbbXi0X9j0h7ekuUreEdblY6KkuXYSoEra7qcPwedOEA7P1fjw81R-1-el2f8AAAD__4N9dtM) uncovers the surprising 1974 decision that may have secretly reshaped America’s metabolism.

## Abbott Bans THC Sales for Minors
Gov. Greg Abbott issued an executive order yesterday to [ban the sale of THC products](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAQhp8GOwywuGhBsY3V7eW6Kw3KoCQIHozr7ttf3OSe4Nr5ki_z_d_34W588HH-NCtofLo0RDhKAETIAy7TlpPdJyyjiRGsS3n1MeVBMNFQ1lHOB2t8eA2ysnpktuMVaK4a1bStbFW1aCZH09pGcQFSdVZY1snWuQtXUtjL2FVenzLWcc4540LWjYOrahwz3ag455JIBtZjyjUugPA0xYVXekCup7RWQS-IWyGXGxE9Ef1xHPVa4smI6CHSvRDRrynCi4h-SutmoofzNmeYqRnHhEh9KTsUmrKFTM9WH2eKy0T_-qlLmb7bCy2AePKEC2Sa9_DWmUxvN35fPn6-qqztdvgQfLkqItm8Gh_e3-L_Nn5o8RsAAP__yWmTkw) to minors and require retailers to check buyers' IDs. The move comes after Abbott vetoed an outright ban on the products and the legislature failed to pass a bill that would further regulate them. The order directs the Texas Department of State Health Services and the Alcoholic Beverage Commission to create rules to implement the restriction and review testing and labeling requirements. Abbott didn’t [specify a minimum age](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFy6yAQBuDToA4NrJGwCorX0Pm1KTUgVhITJDywsezbZ-RMTpB6Z__d7_-4jTcXU9yX_25DQ885jzseNSERlpHW6V5y-JqoerfvGOZctrjnMoKAjouBSzkGF9NrVE0wXoRBNmik7nR3vaqrblbjhPaDEAi6nyGA6tUlSDH0vvegJjc10ZxhYpBSSiFBtd2Mve5m4QavpZSKKYEhUi4trUj4dHVOr_zA0k55a5JZie6VXf4xsAzscRztZ3V0DhnYU8PAvtcY2PMUAysGBlYKBnbJD74UXLjzPhPxXAKWymmd-C-d_9j5XPLG3_wzqSkm3I-YUqy9Zkosm4vp_RH9rceHge8AAAD__4IAhsQ) for purchase. "_Texas will not wait when it comes to protecting children and families_ ," said Abbott, claiming his order [ensures](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uwyAUQOGnwVsiLsYGBoYs7pROldrNAnOJkbCJ4Obv7av0Fbqe4RvO93k-u5TTfvl0G1p6xjLv-GgZibDOtC7XWsJtoebdvmOIpW5pL3UWXAwHbg4Ac3Apv2bZBet5MNChBTWoQWupVbda75GDWYbYC6F5EBJ6EwLv4zIqlAq7ZN8YNwAAHIQ8DhFHNUTujFcAIJnkGBKVeqQVCZ-uxfwqd6zHpWxdtivRtbH-xMTExPR8Vyamj4qXk_eFaP76YWJq5OjWmJjAjIPmZlRaA-hejHLsqg3XR8o5tVExyS-bS_lPp_89uVvxGwAA___PznMA) "_Safety for kids, freedom for adults_." Industry representatives for the more than 8,000 consumable hemp retailers welcomed the development, saying tighter regulations would force bad actors out of business. State Sen. Charles Perry (R-Lubbock), the author of legislation to ban THC products, [insisted](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2OwyAQxfHT2F0iZsJgU1Bs4y7bbmkRGMdI2EQw-br9KnuF7Z5e8Sv-P-f57FNO-_Xbb-zktZR552fLLMJ1ljXcaon3IO3i953jUuqW9lJnVEgHZQ8Ac_Qpv2fdR3dR0ULPDgYaaBz1OPSrOyk0GK3xy6g0Bk3EBinaEIAwwNIn98GUBQBQgPpIC5uBFuXtZQAA3WnFMUmpR1lZ-OXbkt_lwfUYytZnt4rcWnf66nDqcHp93g4nzhwkrL5mbh1OTbzcPwOsIUKjSJE1Vhsg6KuLt2fKOTUzdFpdN5_yHy7_S_Jw-BsAAP__JL5ylg) the "debate isn’t over."
## Houston Imam Threatens Muslim Store Owners
A Houston imam is warning Muslim-owned businesses to [stop selling "haraam"](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2O2zAQRk8jdTQ4XMq0ChZp1G3alMJIHJlE-CNwxrv27QPnClt9zYeHh_fnc_3ElFO9_8ZCXp5HWyt9cyYR6qvE_ewtPHbhDWulcLReUm19NdpMSs8KYA2Y8mu1Y_CbDjOM5MFNbrrd7M2N0SMFtO5jc3Cb7DHNDsyMeIDb3LxBuI7Jv2F6BgDQYOxlOujqpkPjvDkAsIPVFJK0fpFIQk_kI7_aF_XL3sqYfRQ5efj4NZhlMEvAnJHpeXZifj8Gs7Cg0Hsj9oTqROkts-IT-1_VHtLxTipVFduDpVWFrFLBogIVrIEVS-vEKiTZo4rYEYtKQoUHs4zdh_M75Zz46gar7wVT_m8mP-v55c2_AAAA__-7UI3T)—items like alcohol, pork, and lottery tickets that are forbidden by Islam—or face protests. In a [viral video](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2u4yAQQOGnwV2iGQzGFBRp3GXbLS1shhgtNhFM_t5-lfsKtz3FV5y_1_nqU07H7Y_fyfE7lvmgV8vETHXmbb3XEh4rt8UfB4VY6p6OUmcJUp_AnhDn4FP-zKoLboFgsSOHRhs9jmo03eZCJBtGIu-tBhp8pEBrpF7TEHoYli65LwYWERFQqrOONBgdwdvFIKISCigkLvXMGzG9fYv5U55Uz2vZu-w25nsT_UXIScjp_a1CTpf9c6V_Qk6NPT-akBPaQalRGug1WAto7NhVF-6vlHNqgxEKbrtP-Yfl3814Ovk_AAD___u5ceM), Imam F. Qasim ibn Ali Khan is seen entering a convenience store and confronting the clerk. "_You know as a merchant you have every legal right to sell whatever you want, but as Muslim I have the responsibility to educate people_ ," he said. In response, Gov. Greg Abbott [said](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LtugzAUgOGnMVsin4MvMHjIQqd0qtRuyOBDsGRwZJ_c3r5KX6HrP3zD_30ezz6muF8-_UaOn0sed3rURMxURl7na8nhNnOd_L5TWHLZ4p7LiBL1QfYHgDH4mF6jaoKbZOihIQdWW911qrPN6kzrUfWgW_AqdMvSUzd36MHOlrxB3UT3xmQPACAB1VEvZKxepO8nCwBKKEkhci5HXonp6euSXvlO5TjnrUluZb5W0Z4EDgKH57sKHD4KXU7TlJnHrx-BQ2XPtypwgN5osK0xiAaV6lqUTXHh-ogpxWqsUPKy-Zj-dP7fk7vD3wAAAP__oVZyxw) he "signed laws that BAN Sharia Law and Sharia Compounds in Texas," adding that "No business & no individual should fear fools like this." Likewise, U.S. Sen. ## 43 Texans Among 400 Wealthiest Americans
According to _Forbes_ , more than [three dozen Texans](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykz7Fu6yAUxvGnwVssDhcHGBju4i1dOyLwOcRI2FhA4-Ttq7SP0PHTJ_2k_-fN3XzKab9_-I1sf8bidjpbpt6pur4uRy34tfQW_L4TxlK3tJfqBBfThZsLgEOf8svJAW3gaGAgC2pSk9ZSq2G1hJqu0YMXiEpyE4ORqCGGaHBSix6SfWPcAABwEHKcIl3VFLk3QQGAZJITpl7q2Ffq9PQt5ld5UB2Xsg3Zrr0fjf37z8TMxHye5xhLDdTeNxPz77hIzpmYh2rxOFPOqV0Vk_y--ZR_nP63-ocV3wEAAP__3DBumQ) are among the 400 wealthiest Americans, who are together worth a record combined $6.6 trillion. Greg Abbott issued an executive order yesterday to to minors and require retailers to check buyers' IDs. The move comes after Abbott vetoed an outright ban on the products and the legislature failed to pass a bill that would further regulate them. The order directs the Texas Department of State Health Services and the Alcoholic Beverage Commission to create rules to implement the restriction and review testing and labeling requirements. Abbott didn’t for purchase. "Texas will not wait when it comes to protecting children and families ," said Abbott, claiming his order "Safety for kids, freedom for adults." Industry representatives for the more than 8,000 consumable hemp retailers welcomed the development, saying tighter regulations would force bad actors out of business. State Sen. Charles Perry (R-Lubbock), the author of legislation to ban THC products, the "debate isn’t over." "You know as a merchant you have every legal right to sell whatever you want, but as Muslim I have the responsibility to educate people ," he said. In response, Gov. Greg Abbott he "signed laws that BAN Sharia Law and Sharia Compounds in Texas," adding that "No business & no individual should fear fools like this." Likewise, U.S. Sen. name: Troy Downing
subject: 24 years later, we will never forget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
Today, we honor those we lost and the heroes who rose that day.

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6NDg4MjgxNTEsImUiOiJtZy1lbmM6NWh6RDRoYmhSOTR0SVViRGFVQXlzUGowaHcxSkVGa0EiLCJyaSI6IjI4OTktZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJycSI6IjAyLWIyNTI1NC03YjVlN2UxNjBjMmY0MjgzYWJlMzE3MDE3YzY4Yzc4NSIsIm0iOmZhbHNlLCJ1aSI6IjAiLCJ1biI6IiIsInUiOiJ0cm95ZG93bmluZy5jb20_ZXhfdGlkPTIwMjUwOTExX0wwLVNGLjExMDExN190MTcwNTg2OS0yODk5JnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj0yMDI1MDkxMV9MMC1TRi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3MDU4NjktMjg5OSJ9/juGAJt8TmzhFrtb13_BsLw)Hello Peter,
Twenty-four years ago, our Nation was shaken to its core by an unthinkable act of terror. Nearly 3,000 innocent lives were taken that day, but amid the devastation, we witnessed the very best of America. Brave first responders, ordinary citizens, and countless heroes stepped forward with courage and compassion, a spirit that continues to inspire us all.
Like many of you, I remember exactly where I was when I first heard the news. That moment profoundly changed how I saw life, service, and responsibility. I felt a compelling call to act, leaving behind my business, leisure, and even time with my family to serve in the United States Air Force.
Through that experience, I understood the weight of service and sacrifice, not only in my decision but in the courage of countless others who stepped forward that day. It was a powerful reminder that the impact of such moments extends beyond any one life, shaping how we stand together and remain united in the face of great trials.
Let us pause today to remember the lives lost on that tragic day, honor the courage of those who answered the call to protect and serve, and reaffirm our unity as a Nation in the face of adversity.
We will never forget.
With gratitude,
Troy Downing| |
---

body:

The Maine Legislature’s Health and Human Services (HHS) Committee held a meeting Tuesday afternoon to discuss the state’s relationship with ModivCare, a large Colorado-based company that recently filed for bankruptcy. subject: Charlie Kirk & 9/11 - Two Tragedies that Should Remind America How to Stand Together.

# [Charlie Kirk & 9/11 - Two Tragedies that Should Remind America How to Stand Together. ](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=173327242&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzI3MjQyLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc1OTA3MDgsImV4cCI6MTc2MDE4MjcwOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0._efHHPSlSv6k3XsDTRYH-4Bdq81aNeWpA3uAjOSWAnM)
### 24 hours and 24 years later, our ability to see each other's humanity is vanishing.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Sep 11
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=173327242&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzI3MjQyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NzU5MDcwOCwiZXhwIjoxNzYwMTgyNzA4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.JlmrmK7mNFYL7upUT7ea4ajhBo8NiyDQRwey-4r1_cs&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
