name: Team Katie Porter
subject: Katie wants to hear from you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
At a time when Trump is making it harder for our voices to be heard, this campaign is about getting to the core of what it means to live in a democracy.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40128%2E3422442%2EDD-MyG)
Peter,
Katie Porter is running to be a governor for the people, not special interests. That’s why she doesn’t take a dime from corporations.
Instead, this campaign runs on grassroots support. And Katie wants to hear directly from supporters like you. **[Can you take our short survey to let Katie know what issues matter most to you?](https://act.katieporter.com/survey/em_ms_kp_20250911_1_ak?source=em20250911-40128&akid=40128%2E3422442%2EDD-MyG)**
[Take the Survey](https://act.katieporter.com/survey/em_ms_kp_20250911_1_ak?source=em20250911-40128&akid=40128%2E3422442%2EDD-MyG)
At a time when Trump is making it harder for our voices to be heard, this campaign is about getting to the core of what it means to live in a democracy: listening to people and working together on solutions.
California is facing real challenges—the cost of living is rising, a decades-long housing crisis is continuing, and families are struggling to get affordable health care. The only way we’ll solve these problems is by including everyone’s voice, including people like you, Peter.
**[Please take our brief survey to let Katie Porter know what issues are on your mind.](https://act.katieporter.com/survey/em_ms_kp_20250911_1_ak?source=em20250911-40128&akid=40128%2E3422442%2EDD-MyG)**
[Take the Survey](https://act.katieporter.com/survey/em_ms_kp_20250911_1_ak?source=em20250911-40128&akid=40128%2E3422442%2EDD-MyG)
Thanks so much!
Team Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20250911-40128&amounts=&t=3&refcode2=40128_3422442_DD-MyG&akid=40128%2E3422442%2EDD-MyG)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4g-emails?source=40128&t=4&akid=40128%2E3422442%2EDD-MyG)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4g-em-update-info-footer?source=40128)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=40128&t=5&akid=40128%2E3422442%2EDD-MyG)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Governor 2026
PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)
Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
FFPC #1479597

Peter, Katie Porter is running to be a governor for the people, not special interests. That’s why she doesn’t take a dime from corporations. Instead, this campaign runs on grassroots support. And Katie wants to hear directly from supporters like you. At a time when Trump is making it harder for our voices to be heard, this campaign is about getting to the core of what it means to live in a democracy: listening to people and working together on solutions. California is facing real challenges—the cost of living is rising, a decades-long housing crisis is continuing, and families are struggling to get affordable health care. The only way we’ll solve these problems is by including everyone’s voice, including people like you, Peter. Thanks so much! AMAZING news, Peter!!!
The Trump-ordered maps in Texas are a blatant, illegal partisan gerrymander. These rigged maps were designed to erase Democratic voters, silence communities of color, and hand Trump and Greg Abbott unchecked power.
That’s why brave groups like the NAACP are stepping up and SUING Abbott in court. Now, it’s up to us to show overwhelming grassroots support >>>
https://secure.everyaction.com/mAkF3USLG0eNrJl2Bl3yfA2 [https://secure.everyaction.com/mAkF3USLG0eNrJl2Bl3yfA2]
Trump and Abbott think they can get away with this scheme because they believe people will stay quiet. They are counting on confusion, exhaustion, and silence from voters who’ve already been pushed to the margins.
But history shows that when Americans organize, when we mobilize, and when we fight back, WE WIN.
That’s why your voice matters right now. Every single signature adds pressure, builds momentum, and proves Democrats are united against this shameless attempt to steal power >>>

name: Daniel Cameron
subject: A prayer for our nation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cameron for Senate Inc
body:
| | | |
---
Friend,
Less than 24 hours after America witnessed the shocking murder of Charlie Kirk – a proud Christian, husband and father – we come together to mark the 24th anniversary of 9/11.
It is a heavy time for all of us who are blessed to call ourselves Americans.

In that spirit, I ask you to join Makenze and me in a prayer for our nation.
_Jesus, I pray for our nation._
_There’s an evil that is plaguing our people._
_The devil is working. Lord, let us turn our eyes to you._
_Lord Jesus help us. Help us, Jesus._
_We must repent._
_We call out to you, Jesus._
_As a people, we must return to you._
_In Jesus’s mighty name, amen._

Sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com Cameron for Senate Inc., 838 E High St #240, Lexington, KY 40502, United States

name: Brad Finstad
subject: Political Violence Must End
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Finstad for Congress
body:
| | | | |
---
| Dear Friend, Like so many across our nation, I am heartbroken by the tragic and senseless loss of Charlie Kirk. He was taken from us in a brutal act of violence that has left a hole in the hearts of his family, his friends, and countless Americans who were inspired by his courage and conviction. Charlie was more than a public figure—he was a husband, a father, a man of faith, and a tireless advocate for the things he believed in. He dedicated his life to reaching the next generation, reminding young people that truth matters, that faith endures, and that freedom is always worth defending. His words carried weight because they came from a place of conviction, not anger. His passion was rooted in his belief in America and his trust in God. To Charlie’s family, especially his wife and children, I want you to know that Minnesota grieves alongside you. We wrap you in our prayers, asking for God’s comfort and peace in this time of unimaginable sorrow. The right to speak freely, to gather peacefully, and to live without fear for one’s beliefs must never be undermined by violence. As a nation, we must stand united in rejecting hate and upholding justice. Those responsible must be held fully accountable. But justice alone is not enough. We must also look inward and recommit ourselves to building a society where disagreement does not lead to violence, where differences are debated with civility, and where our children grow up in a nation that values life, liberty, and community above all else. May we come together in prayer, in unity, and in resolve for a better tomorrow. With a heavy heart, Brad Finstad

subject: Grieving Someone Who Rejected Empathy
body:
On Charlie Kirk's death, the temptation of violence, and why we must still choose peace.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..E-0tAcH4Z34VeQmL2D5rfYdpF6DvRBam_kjAZfVmroU?) for more
---
# [Grieving Someone Who Rejected Empathy](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2883939&post_id=173383080&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzgzMDgwLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2MjI1NzgsImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxNDU3OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4ODM5MzkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-SUwrl7QPo0sHfRRn3YKq9-HkZF6nbVmrP0t5t3Dv_Q)
### On Charlie Kirk's death, the temptation of violence, and why we must still choose peace.
| | [Caroline Gleich](https://substack.com/@carolinegleich)
---
| Sep 11
---
| [](https://substack.com/@carolinegleich)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2883939&post_id=173383080&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzgzMDgwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NzYyMjU3OCwiZXhwIjoxNzYwMjE0NTc4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjg4MzkzOSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Xfy7G8feEUf6kj0fDDPIM6qHYLWT92tY18fRpLndOQU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2883939&post_id=173383080&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzgzMDgwLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2MjI1NzgsImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxNDU3OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4ODM5MzkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-SUwrl7QPo0sHfRRn3YKq9-HkZF6nbVmrP0t5t3Dv_Q&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2883939&post_id=173383080&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzgzMDgwLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2MjI1NzgsImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxNDU3OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4ODM5MzkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-SUwrl7QPo0sHfRRn3YKq9-HkZF6nbVmrP0t5t3Dv_Q)
---
| | [](https://substack..E3DOtkUhpvg9XUJNc6UhPxSDxFPpmnNPpdGYPjUO2l4?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/carolinegleich/p/grieving-someone-who-rejected-empathy?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

**Violence cannot be the answer, not in Utah, not anywhere.**
Yesterday, Charlie Kirk, a prominent conservative commentator, was shot and killed at Utah Valley University, just 30 minutes south of Salt Lake City.
I feel compelled to write because it happened in my backyard. Because it could have been me on the campaign trail last year. Because it could have been a teacher, a student, or any one of us.
I despised much of Mr. Kirk's rhetoric. Many of us did. And it feels conflicting to grieve a man who once said he "can't stand the word empathy," who argued that gun deaths were an acceptable cost for the Second Amendment, who claimed the solution to gun violence was simply more guns. Part of me wants to ask, wasn't he fueling the very culture of violence that took his life?
This is where the moral test lies. Mr. Kirk's words harmed people. But no matter how much we disagree, violence is never the answer. It only creates more violence. We cannot live in a world like this.
My heart breaks for his family, the students, the first responders, and our community left reeling. My heart breaks that his shooting was followed almost immediately by another school shooting in Colorado, by the political assassination of Minnesota state Rep. Melissa Hortman, her husband, and their dog, by children gunned down in schools across America, by wars in Gaza and Ukraine, and by a bystander killed at a Utah protest this year. Again and again, my heart breaks.
I notice the selective empathy of our elected leaders. When Melissa Hortman was assassinated, Senator Mike Lee posted jokes about the suspect instead of offering sympathy. When Rep. Nancy Pelosi's husband Paul was attacked in their home and suffered a fractured skull, Donald Trump mocked the assault instead of condemning it. Studies confirm this matters: [Trump's use of violent language has increased over time and is more violent than any other U.S. political leader's](https://substack.com/redirect/bd862b24-aa35-4135-a88c-abbfd52a38fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), according to a UCLA study. [Hateful, dehumanizing speech increases the risk of real-world violence. ](https://substack.com/redirect/224b3f0b-1572-412c-b3c9-bc3373339ab4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

Even when it is conflicting or difficult, empathy cannot be selective or conditional, even if that's what our leaders modeling. It is not too late for Senator Lee to denounce Rep. Hortman's death, to apologize for his tweets, and to reflect on gun violence.
I've been reflecting on the comments I've seen online about Mr. Kirk's assassination. I've read painful words like: "He deserved it." "This was karma." "Violence is the only way."
I hear the exhaustion and fear behind those words, from communities who have endured violence, discrimination and exclusion. That pain is real.
But if are to build lasting change, we must reject the logic that violence is the answer to violence. We share a responsibility to break the cycle and to choose another path, together.
History is clear. Erica Chenoweth and Maria Stephan studied over 300 uprisings across the last century. Their finding: **nonviolent movements were twice as successful as violent ones.** And when just 3.5% of a population engages in sustained, strategic, nonviolent resistance, they almost always succeed. Mass participation becomes possible. Repression backfires. And the moral high ground unites allies.
This is right versus wrong. It's about rejecting America's sickness: our obsession with guns and violence. I condemn violence, I condemn celebrating Mr. Kirk's death, and I condemn resharing graphic videos of it. Out of respect for the victims, their families, and our communities, let's choose dignity over clicks.
I know we share more in common than what divides us. I've seen it in Utah and across the world: when we sit down in dialogue, we can find common ground.

Authoritarian forces, billionaires, and tech companies thrive when we are divided, when we are distracted and blaming each other, instead of holding power accountable. But unity, across differences, across communities, is the only thing that has ever bent the arc of history toward justice.

Refusing violence is how we win back our future. If we want peace, we must advocate for common sense gun reform. We must resolve our differences through dialogue, civic engagement, and at the ballot box. **Never with bullets.**
Because ballots, not bullets, are what build democracy.

name: Eric Swalwell
subject: We promised never to forget - so why are Republicans cutting care?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
Honoring our 9/11 heroes means protecting them |
---
_Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. _
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115045739/569002900/-1590518309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZjI5Yzg2NC00NzhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cSthPhbPqagRfwl5aPRBOfjrDClJa59_bHPm_1Rnrto=&emci=ddebe322-718e-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=1f29c864-478f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=7878003)
Friend,

Today, we pause to honor the nearly 3,000 lives lost on September 11, 2001. We remember the families who still grieve, and we honor the courage of the first responders who risked everything to save others.

For them, “Never Forget” has never been a slogan. It is lived every day in the pain, illness, and loss so many first responders and survivors still endure.

**That’s why it is unconscionable that Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress have moved to slash funding for the World Trade Center Health Program — the program that provides medical care and support to the very people who ran into danger when America needed them most.**

Stripping funding from 9/11 heroes dishonors their sacrifice and breaks our nation’s promise to them. I refuse to let that go unanswered.

I’m fighting in Congress to fully fund the health care and benefits our first responders and survivors deserve.

**“Never Forget” must mean action.** It must mean keeping our word to the people who gave everything for us. Together, we can hold Trump and his Republican allies accountable — and ensure our heroes are never left behind.

In solidarity,
Eric Swalwell

|
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115045740/569002901/1800133675?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZjI5Yzg2NC00NzhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cSthPhbPqagRfwl5aPRBOfjrDClJa59_bHPm_1Rnrto=&emci=ddebe322-718e-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=1f29c864-478f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=7878003)
Democrat Eric Swalwell proudly represents California's 14th Congressional District. He needs your support in order to keep protecting what's important: our healthcare, our paychecks, our future, and our democracy.

Eric runs a people-powered campaign that relies on grassroots supporters like you, and if he's going to win re-election, he's going to need your help. name: Mary Trump
subject: Why I’m endorsing Shawn Harris to defeat Marjorie Taylor Greene

name: Oregon GOP
subject: September 11th: A Day Of Remembrance
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE OREGON REPUBLICAN PARTY (COMMITTEE ID 379) 187 HIGH ST
body:
Honoring the lives lost and the heroes who showed us the best of America.

| | | |
---
---
| | Friend, Today marks 24 years since the attacks of September 11, 2001 – a day of tragedy, but also of extraordinary courage. We remember the nearly 3,000 innocent lives taken. We honor the first responders and everyday heroes who ran into danger to save others. And we reflect on the strength and unity that carried our nation through one of its darkest hours.
---
| |
---
| | September 11th is not only a day of remembrance – it is a reminder of our shared values, our resilience as a nation, and our duty to ensure future generations understand the significance of this day. As we pause together, let us commit to always honoring the fallen, supporting the families who still carry the loss, and preserving the freedoms that define us as Americans. We will never forget. Sincerely, Oregon GOP
---
---
---
---
|
* * *
---

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Sept 11: A renewed resolve
body:
Yesterday we were reminded -- too painfully -- that freedom is not free
[](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org/1/010001993a58012a-743eddaf-e832-46de-bcf1-4e82725f91af-000000/Uk6ft_aK3qQtvO_tKBGrQE2TEi3cpiaQHW5cQywHjiw=422)Patriot–I had planned to send you a very different email today, reflecting on the September 11th attacks. Then yesterday, the shock and pain of life suddenly ripped from us because of ideological hate was made new and raw all over again.Like you, I am still reeling from the assassination of Charlie Kirk.Just moments before Charlie was cruelly taken from us, we rallied near the Capitol in Washington, DC for the conclusion of a month's long bus tour to build support for the SAVE Act. In the pouring rain, hundreds of supporters from all over the country and key leaders in Congress joined us to make a stand for America's founding principles.It was an exciting moment I was looking forward to sharing with you. Then the unthinkable happened.When the time is right, I will share more about our work, but today we reflect, both on the legacy of Charlie Kirk as well as the somber significance of September 11th.Twenty-four years ago, our nation endured a horrific act of terror. On this solemn day, we pause to remember the lives lost, the heroes who emerged, and the resilience of the American spirit. The events of 9/11/01 reminded us of the fragility of freedom and the importance of eternal vigilance.Just as we united in the face of tragedy twenty-four years ago, we must stand together once again as active and engaged citizens. The fight for a government that truly represents "We the People" is a continuation of that very same American spirit Charlie lived every day.Let us carry forward with renewed dedication to our Republic. Let us remain diligent, ensure governmental accountability, and never cease in our pursuit of liberty for all. Your active participation, whether at rallies, through petitions, or in your communities, is what will restore the strength and goodness of America.Thank you for your unstoppable patriotism and commitment to our nation's future. Please keep Charlie's family, and the families of all who lost loved ones on 9/11, in your prayers.In liberty,Jenny Beth Martin _This email list is a joint venture of the following separate organizations: Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc., Tea Party Patriots Foundation, and Tea Party Patriots Citizens Fund. All rights reserved.__Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc. operates as a social welfare organization under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. Contributions to Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc. are not deductible as charitable contributions for income tax purposes._ Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [unsubscribe](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftpot.teapartypatriots.org%2Fcontact-preferences%2F01jp3rk1m05jb3jy5xyw9vqr5t%3Fct=1ebd6a5833cd%26et=a8fe34ad85d9/1/010001993a58012a-743eddaf-e832-46de-bcf1-4e82725f91af-000000/dylh1h5q-EL0ixOiX8ZwCJkBXWbcQIMZ_iQmf80Khps=422) or [update your preferences](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftpot.teapartypatriots.org%2Fcontact-preferences%2F01jp3rk1m05jb3jy5xyw9vqr5t%3Fct=1ebd6a5833cd%26et=a8fe34ad85d9/2/010001993a58012a-743eddaf-e832-46de-bcf1-4e82725f91af-000000/CXd9xgKaTP1yCvFAYwRW_frMp1Ngl_UUtw-nPsKDyOg=422).

name: Tim Burchett
subject: Honoring their memory with action
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Burchett for Congress**
body:
NEVER FORGET 🇺🇲

| | | |
---
| |
---
| | Friend, It’s been 24 years since September 11th—a day forever etched into America’s memory.

2,977 lives were taken.

Countless families forever changed.

And a nation left grieving, determined to ensure such evil would never strike us again.

Yet somehow, decades later, America is still giving money to the Taliban. If you’ve been with me for a while, you know I’ve fought tirelessly to end this disgrace. The House passed my bill, but we’re still waiting on the Senate—and I won’t give up until it’s on President Trump’s desk.
In President George W. Bush’s address to the nation on that terrible day, he said, “Terrorist attacks can shake the foundations of our biggest buildings, but they cannot touch the foundation of America.”
Those words still ring true today, but here’s the hard truth: Every dollar our government allows to slip into the hands of the Taliban chips away at that foundation.
We can never bring back what was stolen from us on that dark day—but we can honor the memory of the innocent lives lost. The best way to do that is by making sure not a single red cent of American money ever finds its way into the hands of those who plotted, carried out, and still celebrate the murder of thousands of our fellow citizens.
Honoring their memory begins with action—and that means putting an end to the payments that continue to fund terrorists.
NEVER FORGET 🇺🇲
Sincerely,
Tim Burchett

---
| | |
---

The best way to do that is by making sure not a single red cent of American money ever finds its way into the hands of those who plotted, carried out, and still celebrate the murder of thousands of our fellow citizens. Honoring their memory begins with action—and that means putting an end to the payments that continue to fund terrorists. NEVER FORGET 🇺🇲 Sincerely, Tim Burchett | | | Stay in Touch 👇 Paid for by Burchett for Congress | | | ---|---|--- | | |

# Braun: Indiana requesting waiver from race, gender-conscious components in federal program
---
| | Fox 59
David Gay
September 9, 2025 [https://fox59.com/indianapolitics/braun-indiana-requesting-waiver-from-race-gender-conscious-components-in-federal-program/](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/czvI2UHEDkyscm3kuf7ukKOvld_RffGvsLpUGYpIVnFjStzFPc2dZMTDjM34AKrNwe3zf5_y0W9X75GFJyNvge_wmzYcga7UkvehzKwyn3k23DUZcmTkwJ3MyvSa0kNyveF2pu29jKS4VWsDFUxhrz16ir1ZTkio7ItuXLF5wmcHxD1qjm6IghmpV35ACtc5CKXC8C-YfkWtEwoozVsqYxzWOO5OyLFc0ByfAcKey5XefFq5KgkF9D8kquxiT_-eJDFdACmqzDmydoZ8o6UO-cC4afG35_vH-AM9Dr61JfK7bbXyFSek6jPR68dQCcbBGneuT0abYyBQU8espV97ktFTIDDjGp_z3m3FGaQcvs7B-30znRQTaRxfwtrZTxVUSPvAnwZeeeTIfbNKnkVh-bc6fG8-PMvlmc-hYZB7DdeMQ1ksDJCcYf_OIPBGemb-n4I52tidmoN86nE8IH0XsveWeXq0lbKwQboPDKiu8Z61darxUkee2HgR1ZBU7SttI6cKS-CE0f6bLqE6yjs1Zveg55VbeLhoqFi_4GxbzSnIXwC6a2br8FC0uBfm18Vlq9UXF3rLbm3jkz3BZMYrO8lMODOhOlNDAYx4LIs82NFlpFM/FMdSoX_MJrp8iEjlNzA61CboKJrDNgrQ/12) INDIANAPOLIS – The state of Indiana is requesting a federal waiver, which Indiana Gov. Mike Braun and Indiana Attorney General Todd Rokita say would end discriminatory business practices imposed by the federal government. According to a news release from Braun’s office, Indiana was the first state to request a waiver from federal requirements to “implement race and gender-conscious components” of the Disadvantaged Business Enterprise, or DBE, program. This waiver was requested by the Indiana Department of Transportation. The release stated that if granted, the waiver would eliminate DBE goals based on race, ethnicity or gender for federally assisted highway construction contracts for three years. “I am a firm believer in offering equal opportunity for all to compete and succeed,” Braun said in the release. “If granted, this waiver will further position Indiana as a state one would want to do business with and as a place to grow your business.” This comes after a recent federal lawsuit was filed involving an Indiana subcontractor that alleges the DBE program unlawfully discriminates, the release said. Under the waiver, the release said that INDOT would focus “exclusively on the availability of economically disadvantaged small businesses within specific geographic and industry markets,” through a race- and gender-neutral strategy. “Forcing INDOT to prioritize contractors based on race or gender is unconstitutional and undermines fair competition,” Rokita said in the release. “Granting the waiver INDOT is requesting is well within the Department of Transportation’s authority and would be a critical step toward ensuring that Indiana’s transportation projects are awarded based on merit, not arbitrary classifications, and aligns with our constitutional duty to uphold equal protection under the law.” On Tuesday, Rokita announced that attorneys general from 17 states will be joining the effort in support of the waiver, including:
* Alabama
* Kentucky
* Ohio
* Oklahoma
* Texas

The release said the waiver’s three-year period aligns with INDOT’s next disparity study, which is expected to be completed in 2028. Officials said this will be used to determine a new DBE goal based on economic criteria alone.

name: Navigating Uncertainty (by Vikram Mansharamani)
subject: Speech Isn't Violence, Violence Isn't Speech
body:
Compromise and respectful disagreement are not signs of weakness; they're preconditions for democracy.

| |
---|---|---

# [Speech Isn't Violence, Violence Isn't Speech ](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=173377885&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzc3ODg1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2MjA5ODAsImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxMjk4MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RTGsBzDE6V-AxsrnG4cqhq6q09sMTfKVQGJJmpRO-pc)
### Compromise and respectful disagreement are not signs of weakness; they're preconditions for democracy.
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---
| Sep 11
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=173377885&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzc3ODg1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NzYyMDk4MCwiZXhwIjoxNzYwMjEyOTgwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTY2MTk5OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.YgtdAbO8R4-vZnWybsRHSDa5DrvKYsjQOik5Y3c_zYg&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=173377885&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzc3ODg1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2MjA5ODAsImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxMjk4MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RTGsBzDE6V-AxsrnG4cqhq6q09sMTfKVQGJJmpRO-pc&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=173377885&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzc3ODg1LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2MjA5ODAsImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxMjk4MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RTGsBzDE6V-AxsrnG4cqhq6q09sMTfKVQGJJmpRO-pc)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/speech-isnt-violence-violence-isnt?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d45f6238-07de-4bc3-82a2-fc29fac8e0a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The news that Charlie Kirk, a young conservative activist, [was shot and killed](https://substack.com/redirect/99dc56e8-f0e6-4de8-bbea-3784a32777c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) while speaking at an event in Utah was shocking. This tragedy is the latest in a rising tide of political violence that has swept the United States.
In just the past year, in addition to Kirk's murder, [a man shot and killed](https://substack.com/redirect/c5ebdcb7-c358-4faa-ba11-9184d0cc5cd5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a Minnesota legislator and her husband and wounded another legislator and his wife; [someone broke into the Pennsylvania's governor's mansion and set in on fire](https://substack.com/redirect/056b4a5e-a3a1-4c2b-bc9b-4371c5387373?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), forcing Governor Josh Shapiro and his family to flee; and then former president and presidential candidate Donald Trump survived not [one](https://substack.com/redirect/648cad03-0b50-4ca8-b01c-0c55c26ce838?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) but [two](https://substack.com/redirect/7aeddaf9-0efd-4965-9276-3fe317493ae2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) assassination attempts. Violent political protests have also become a regular feature of American politics, often involving destruction of property, hospitalizations, and clashes between opposing sides.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..7Wx5Y7pYVT74s1OTMEBZFds4k37awLMUgvT4q_uLJV4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=173377885)
**Far too many Americans now feel that opinions and ideas contrary to their own are a form of metaphorical violence that warrants the use of actual violence in self-defense**. And far too many also think that violence--whether vandalism, intimidation, or physical attacks--is an acceptable mode of political persuasion. I have a very different view, one that I feel compelled to share: **speech isn 't violence and violence isn't speech.**
I have long been a champion of free speech. As someone who has made his living trafficking in ideas, giving speeches and writing books, I have a heightened sensitivity to any attempt to stifle debate. (Perhaps more disturbing is the chilling effect of violence on speech as individuals begin to reconsider sharing ideas and start self-censoring). And as someone who has thrown his hat in the ring and run for office, I am even more sensitive to using political violence to censor views or to control a narrative; there were times during my campaign when my staff was concerned for my safety.
* * *
## [A Time for Unity](https://substack.com/redirect/c14ff38d-c72e-43d6-a6e8-542ecd092d74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| July 16, 2024
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c14ff38d-c72e-43d6-a6e8-542ecd092d74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[The opinion piece below was originally published during the Summer of 2022 in the Concord Monitor when I was a Candidate for the US Senate. In light of the current political climate, including some o...](https://substack.com/redirect/c14ff38d-c72e-43d6-a6e8-542ecd092d74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/c14ff38d-c72e-43d6-a6e8-542ecd092d74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Over the course of my travels across more than 80 countries, I have seen political violence derail countries and legitimacy in governments. I never imagined I'd watch it happen in America. I was on the ground in Pakistan in 2007 and watched political violence escalate until [opposition leader Benazir Bhutto was assassinated](https://substack.com/redirect/29de2416-b889-4d2c-91f1-291857bc2cf6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); the country was immediately and rapidly torn apart by riots. When I was in Beirut, across the street from my hotel was a crater left by the truck bomb that [killed former prime minister Rafik Hariri](https://substack.com/redirect/a61d04af-1687-4559-95e6-b35dc07f95d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), triggering the Cedar Revolution. And Colombia, a country I have visited many times, has seen more than its fair share of assassinations and political violence; in fact, just three months ago, Senator Miguel Uribe Turbay, a presidential candidate, was ambushed at a campaign event and [shot in the head](https://substack.com/redirect/7c2467cf-b14c-4af4-a742-6a64ac733d3c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
The problem with political violence is that it almost always escalates as each side goes after the other, outrage spirals ever higher, and normal life deteriorates. Younger members of society begin to lose faith in the future, leading to brain drain and less innovation and new ideas. Debate (other than among the political warriors fighting the battles) dies off and dissension is squashed. Controversial or contrarian thinkers self-censor and polarization intensifies. The vicious cycle accelerates.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..7Wx5Y7pYVT74s1OTMEBZFds4k37awLMUgvT4q_uLJV4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=173377885)
Our country is on the brink, and the escalating political violence in America is a symptom of a culture in crisis. Free speech will inevitably lead to disagreements. Violence, however, is not a natural or necessary reaction to disagreements. We need to return to civil discourse, respectful disagreement, and a willingness to compromise. Being civil and working with those who have different beliefs is not a sign of weakness; it's the basis of a functioning democracy.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..7Wx5Y7pYVT74s1OTMEBZFds4k37awLMUgvT4q_uLJV4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=173377885)

caption...
**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. **He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/42ec0322-afa5-4e3e-afc2-e56556aca6f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/015a23e3-22f9-4a2d-8444-89c72d5dd4b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/e5965af0-b9a6-4431-906d-3e234650ba43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.]

name: Texas Organizing Project
subject: Hungry for change and fighting for it / Queremos el cambio y nos estamos organizando para el cambio
body:
Lea el boletín en español abajo [#Español]
Condemning a racist Supreme Court ruling that endangers lives
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Crop%20field%20protest.jpg]
The U.S. Supreme Court set a dangerous precedent Monday, ruling that ICE agents in Los Angeles can target, stop, and question people based on race or ethnicity, the language they speak, where they work, and what type of work they do.
Read more about this latest backwards SCOTUS ruling here. [https://www.democracydocket.com/news-alerts/supreme-court-trump-immigration-stops-racial-profiling/]
This court decision threatens communities of color and is a direct attack on our fundamental rights. It means federal agents can racially profile to carry out Trump's heinous mass deportations.
We've all seen videos of ICE using increasingly aggressive tactics to detain people, cruelly trapping people arriving at court appointments and denying them due process. This isn't by mistake; it's all by design — and it's white nationalism in action.
TOP will continue to call out and resist Trump's mass deportation machine, with the full intention of fighting authoritarianism and supporting the rights of immigrant families.
SEPT. 27 IN SAN ANTONIO:
Help shape TOP's Bexar County probation reform campaign
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Laquita%20in%20action%20-%20TX%20Cap.jpg]
Join TOP on Saturday, Sept. 27, from 11 a.m. - 2 p.m. in San Antonio , as we brainstorm over brunch to make local policy changes, so that more residents can successfully complete probation in Bexar County as a rehabilitative experience, allowing them to more freely contribute to the well-being of our families and communities.
Get more event details here and RSVP! [https://secure.everyaction.com/JElzt9IXtEaLa0r72CHK_g2]
Our first visioning brunch was a well-attended success, so come be a part of our fight for a local probation system that works fairly for EVERYONE in Bexar County — no matter race, age, gender, or background.
For more event information, email Michael N. at mnachebe@organizetexas.org [mnachebe@organizetexas.org] or Laquita lgarcia@organizetexas.org [lgarcia@organizetexas.org] .
SURVEY FOR TEXANS:
Help TOP unite around one major campaign priority
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Transform%20Texas%20graphic.jpg]
There's still time! If you live in Texas and care about building a more progressive future for our state, we want to hear from you . TOP is uniting around one major campaign priority that we believe will reflect the Texas we deserve. Your voice is essential in shaping our collective fight for justice and equity.
TEXANS: Take our quick survey here. [https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc0JcmarckcN1aeYp7Qy807cgCm0rIjxGJsg2s5nfS7STQ-cA/viewform]
For more information about this survey and campaign effort, contact Sofia at slopez@organizetexas.org [slopez@organizetexas.org] .
TOP Tip: Protect yourself against AI-powered scams
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Phone%20scam.jpg]
From texts to emails, online scams are getting more sophisticated, and with the rise of AI, it's even more critical to protect your personal information. Check out these tips on how to stay vigilant. [https://www.cnet.com/tech/services-and-software/dont-overthink-it-that-suspicious-link-may-actually-be-a-scam/]
Español
Condenando un fallo racista de la Corte Suprema que pone en peligro vidas
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Crop%20field%20protest.jpg]
El lunes, la Corte Suprema de Estados Unidos sentó un precedente peligroso al dictaminar que los agentes de ICE en Los Ángeles pueden señalar, detener y cuestionar a las personas según su raza o etnicidad, el idioma que hablan, el lugar donde trabajan y el tipo de trabajo que realizan.
Lea más sobre este último fallo retrógrado de la Corte Suprema aquí. [https://www.democracydocket.com/news-alerts/supreme-court-trump-immigration-stops-racial-profiling/]
Esta decisión de la corte amenaza a las comunidades de color y es un ataque directo a nuestros derechos fundamentales. Significa que los agentes federales pueden usar el perfil racial para llevar a cabo las terribles deportaciones masivas de Trump.
Todos hemos visto videos de ICE utilizando tácticas cada vez más agresivas para detener a personas, atrapando cruelmente a quienes llegan a sus citas judiciales y negándoles el debido proceso. Esto no es un error; todo es intencional, y es el nacionalismo blanco en acción.
TOP continuará denunciando y resistiendo la maquinaria de deportación masiva de Trump, con la plena intención de combatir el autoritarismo y defender los derechos de las familias inmigrantes.
27 DE SEPT. EN SAN ANTONIO:
Ayude a dar forma a la campaña de TOP para la reforma de la libertad condicional en el condado de Bexar
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Laquita%20in%20action%20-%20TX%20Cap.jpg]
Únase a TOP el sábado 27 de Septiembre, de 11:00 a.m. a 2:00 p.m. en San Antonio , para intercambiar ideas durante un “brunch" y lograr cambios en las políticas locales, de modo que más residentes puedan completar con éxito la libertad condicional en el condado de Bexar como una experiencia de rehabilitación, permitiéndoles contribuir con mayor libertad al bienestar de nuestras familias y comunidades.
¡Obtenga más detalles del evento aquí y confirme su asistencia! [https://secure.everyaction.com/JElzt9IXtEaLa0r72CHK_g2]
Nuestro primer “brunch” visionario fue todo un éxito, así que participe en nuestra lucha por un sistema local de libertad condicional que funcione de manera justa para TODOS en el condado de Bexar, sin importar raza, edad, género u origen.
Para obtener más información sobre el evento, escriba a Michael N. a mnachebe@organizetexas.org [mnachebe@organizetexas.org] o a Laquita lgarcia@organizetexas.org [lgarcia@organizetexas.org] .
ENCUESTA PARA TEJANOS:
Ayude a TOP a unirse en torno a una prioridad principal de campaña
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Transform%20Texas%20graphic.jpg]
¡Aún hay tiempo! Si vives en Texas y te preocupa construir un futuro más progresista para nuestro estado, queremos saber de ti. TOP se une en torno a una gran prioridad de campaña que creemos que refleja el Texas que merecemos. Tu voz es esencial para dar forma a nuestra lucha colectiva por la justicia y la equidad.
TEJANOS: Responde nuestra breve encuesta aquí. [https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc0JcmarckcN1aeYp7Qy807cgCm0rIjxGJsg2s5nfS7STQ-cA/viewform]
Para más información sobre esta encuesta y la campaña, contacta a Sofía en slopez@organizetexas.org [slopez@organizetexas.org] .
Consejos de TOP: Protéjase contra las estafas de AI
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Phone%20scam.jpg]
Desde mensajes de texto hasta correos electrónicos, las estafas en línea son cada vez más sofisticadas, y con el auge de la AI, es aún más crucial proteger tu información personal. Consulta estos consejos para mantenerte alerta. [https://www.cnet.com/tech/services-and-software/dont-overthink-it-that-suspicious-link-may-actually-be-a-scam/]

clean_body: Lea el boletín en español abajo [#Español] Condemning a racist Supreme Court ruling that endangers lives [] The U.S. Supreme Court set a dangerous precedent Monday, ruling that ICE agents in Los Angeles can target, stop, and question people based on race or ethnicity, the language they speak, where they work, and what type of work they do. Read more about this latest backwards SCOTUS ruling here. [] This court decision threatens communities of color and is a direct attack on our fundamental rights. It means federal agents can racially profile to carry out Trump's heinous mass deportations. We've all seen videos of ICE using increasingly aggressive tactics to detain people, cruelly trapping people arriving at court appointments and denying them due process. This isn't by mistake; it's all by design — and it's white nationalism in action. TOP will continue to call out and resist Trump's mass deportation machine, with the full intention of fighting authoritarianism and supporting the rights of immigrant families. SEPT. 27 IN SAN ANTONIO: Help shape TOP's Bexar County probation reform campaign [] Join TOP on Saturday, Sept. 27, from 11 a.m. - 2 p.m. in San Antonio , as we brainstorm over brunch to make local policy changes, so that more residents can successfully complete probation in Bexar County as a rehabilitative experience, allowing them to more freely contribute to the well-being of our families and communities. Get more event details here and RSVP! [] Our first visioning brunch was a well-attended success, so come be a part of our fight for a local probation system that works fairly for EVERYONE in Bexar County — no matter race, age, gender, or background. For more event information, email Michael N. at mnachebe@organizetexas.org [mnachebe@organizetexas.org] or Laquita lgarcia@organizetexas.org [lgarcia@organizetexas.org] . SURVEY FOR TEXANS: Help TOP unite around one major campaign priority [] There's still time! If you live in Texas and care about building a more progressive future for our state, we want to hear from you . TOP is uniting around one major campaign priority that we believe will reflect the Texas we deserve. Your voice is essential in shaping our collective fight for justice and equity. TEXANS: Take our quick survey here. [] For more information about this survey and campaign effort, contact Sofia at slopez@organizetexas.org [slopez@organizetexas.org] . TOP Tip: Protect yourself against AI-powered scams [] From texts to emails, online scams are getting more sophisticated, and with the rise of AI, it's even more critical to protect your personal information. Check out these tips on how to stay vigilant. [] Español Condenando un fallo racista de la Corte Suprema que pone en peligro vidas [] El lunes, la Corte Suprema de Estados Unidos sentó un precedente peligroso al dictaminar que los agentes de ICE en Los Ángeles pueden señalar, detener y cuestionar a las personas según su raza o etnicidad, el idioma que hablan, el lugar donde trabajan y el tipo de trabajo que realizan. Lea más sobre este último fallo retrógrado de la Corte Suprema aquí. [] Esta decisión de la corte amenaza a las comunidades de color y es un ataque directo a nuestros derechos fundamentales. Significa que los agentes federales pueden usar el perfil racial para llevar a cabo las terribles deportaciones masivas de Trump. Todos hemos visto videos de ICE utilizando tácticas cada vez más agresivas para detener a personas, atrapando cruelmente a quienes llegan a sus citas judiciales y negándoles el debido proceso. Esto no es un error; todo es intencional, y es el nacionalismo blanco en acción. TOP continuará denunciando y resistiendo la maquinaria de deportación masiva de Trump, con la plena intención de combatir el autoritarismo y defender los derechos de las familias inmigrantes. 27 DE SEPT. EN SAN ANTONIO: Ayude a dar forma a la campaña de TOP para la reforma de la libertad condicional en el condado de Bexar [] Únase a TOP el sábado 27 de Septiembre, de 11:00 a.m. a 2:00 p.m. en San Antonio , para intercambiar ideas durante un “brunch" y lograr cambios en las políticas locales, de modo que más residentes puedan completar con éxito la libertad condicional en el condado de Bexar como una experiencia de rehabilitación, permitiéndoles contribuir con mayor libertad al bienestar de nuestras familias y comunidades. ¡Obtenga más detalles del evento aquí y confirme su asistencia! [] Nuestro primer “brunch” visionario fue todo un éxito, así que participe en nuestra lucha por un sistema local de libertad condicional que funcione de manera justa para TODOS en el condado de Bexar, sin importar raza, edad, género u origen. name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: In remembrance.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAPTUR FOR CONGRESS
body:
Marcy Kaptur for Congress [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/95655/images/Kaptur%20Logo.png]

Team,

Twenty-four years have passed since the September 11th attacks, but today will always remain a day of profound national sorrow.

The evil that struck our nation that morning tried to extinguish everything we hold dear — our freedoms; our tolerance of different ways of life; our fight for equality for all people, regardless of gender, ethnicity, or belief. They failed.

I'm holding in my heart the families and loved ones of the nearly 3,000 victims, as well as all of the Americans we've lost in the War on Terror. They will never be forgotten.

With you,

— Marcy
PAID FOR BY KAPTUR FOR CONGRESS

### **[Want more unfiltered conversations with voices like Stefan Smith? Become a paid subscriber to support Endless Urgency](https://substack.com/redirect/400e6f31-41b4-4a77-b384-46d08c550d16?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) 👇**
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/400e6f31-41b4-4a77-b384-46d08c550d16?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Hey folks,
This one's heavy. **Katie Phang** and I jumped on a live to unpack the chaos and pain of the past 24 hours--Charlie Kirk's assassination, the right-wing disinfo-storm, and the fear so many of us are sitting with. We didn't plan this, but we needed to process it together--and with all of you. It's raw, it's honest, and it's essential. Don't miss this one.
* **[00:06:45]** Katie breaks down the chaos around the Charlie Kirk investigation--and how Kash Patel's botched handling has shattered trust in the FBI.
* **[00:14:18]** I talk about what it means to extend empathy to someone like Charlie Kirk, even when he wouldn't return the favor.
* **[00:22:51]** We expose the double standard: why the right gets away with escalating rhetoric while Democrats get blamed for everything.
* **[00:30:33]** Katie shares the heartbreaking moment she had to explain the shooting to her 10-year-old daughter.
* **[00:40:12]** We close by talking about community, courage, and how we keep showing up even when we feel afraid.

**[If you're feeling overwhelmed, angry, or just trying to make sense of it all--you are not alone. This is a must-watch. And if you want to be part of a community that refuses to look away, that's committed to truth, and that shows up even when it's hard--become a paid subscriber today.](https://substack.com/redirect/400e6f31-41b4-4a77-b384-46d08c550d16?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
With urgency,
--Mike

This Substack is where he writes with endless urgency about the issues of the day--and how we can save the Democratic Party and our democracy. You 're currently a free subscriber to Endless Urgency with Mike Nellis. name: James Talarico
subject: Charlie Kirk
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter —
Yesterday, Republican activist Charlie Kirk was shot and killed.
At the time, I was headed to San Antonio for what was supposed to be a rally for our campaign for the U.S. Senate.
The truth is, I almost canceled the event. But instead, I changed the rally to a community discussion about political violence in this country.
Because the goal of political violence is to scare us. To isolate us. It's meant to tear us apart and make us afraid of one another.
In times like this, we need each other more than ever. We need to be together. We need community. We need love.
**[I wanted to share my remarks from last night. It was healing to be in community with my neighbors. I hope my words can help you find comfort as well.](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/655?t=1&akid=28%2E1347274%2E67AN5U)**
[](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/655?t=2&akid=28%2E1347274%2E67AN5U)
**[WATCH](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/655?t=3&akid=28%2E1347274%2E67AN5U)**
Political violence has no place in America.
We have to find our way back to each other. We have to recognize each other as full human beings. We have to love one another again.
Thank you for being a part of this movement to heal what's broken in this country.
James

Paid for by Talarico for Texas
Talarico for Texas
PO Box 14508
Austin, TX 78761
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. James is grateful to have you in our movement. We try to send only the most important updates and opportunities to participate via email, but if you want to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.jamestalarico.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=4&akid=28%2E1347274%2E67AN5U).

subject: Help power the organizing we’re counting on

body:

Powered by People has already racked up more than $300,000 in legal fees to challenge Ken Paxton in court.
**Ken Paxton is trying to shut down Powered by People and put Beto in jail. So, today we’re asking if you can[donate $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/67109?t=2&akid=240349%2E444211%2E7cRxH0) to help us push back.**

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: What I told students who walked out to protest Trump’s militarization of DC
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Yesterday students across Washington, DC walked out of their classrooms to protest Donald Trump’s militarization of our capital city.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20250911-5421-footer&t=1&akid=5421%2E420207%2EI0sTTn)
Peter,
This week, students across Washington, DC walked out of their classrooms to protest Donald Trump’s militarization of our capital city. As a Georgetown alum, I was proud to join students there as they made their voices heard.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250911-1?refcode=em20250911-5421&amount=3&refcode2=5421_420207_I0sTTn&akid=5421%2E420207%2EI0sTTn)
My message was simple: _We are not powerless_. The power does not flow from the top down — it flows from the bottom up. That means **the people have the power** , and it’s up to us to stand up in nonviolent resistance against Trump’s authoritarian acts.
We’re in a dangerous time for our democracy, but if we stand together, we can defeat Trump’s MAGA movement and build a government that finally puts working people first.
[**I need you with me in this moment, Peter. Can I count on you to pitch in $3 or any amount that works for you to power our progressive movement?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250911-1?refcode=em20250911-5421&amount=3&refcode2=5421_420207_I0sTTn&akid=5421%2E420207%2EI0sTTn)
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250911-1?amount=3&refcode=em20250911-5421&refcode2=5421_420207_I0sTTn&akid=5421%2E420207%2EI0sTTn)**
In solidarity,
Pramila Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20250911-5421-footer&t=2&refcode2=5421_420207_I0sTTn&akid=5421%2E420207%2EI0sTTn)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.

name: Heather G.
subject: Your supporter status enclosed >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
Can you help us beat the September slump??
| Hi friend, my name is Heather and I help run the Voter Protection Project!

**I’m reaching out because I noticed that you have yet to confirm your supporter status to help us stop Trump and elect Democrats across the country.**

The months of August and September are always the toughest for fundraising — and with our entire Democracy at stake, _we absolutely can’t afford to fall behind._

**There’s no better time to step up.** Trump is threatening to serve an unprecedented third term. MAGA Republicans are building their warchest to defeat Democrats in 2026. Grassroots support from Democrats like you is vital right now.

[So today, I hope you’ll help us get through September strong: Will you confirm your status as a top Democrat so we can take back the House and Senate from the MAGA majority and save our Democracy? »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93842?t=6&akid=21191%2E878179%2ElTPthE) | [CONFIRM YOUR SUPPORTER STATUS »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/93842?t=7&akid=21191%2E878179%2ElTPthE)
---
Thank you for all your help!

Heather G.
Executive Director
The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=21191%2E878179%2ElTPthE). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=21191%2E878179%2ElTPthE).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=21191%2E878179%2ElTPthE) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=8&akid=21191%2E878179%2ElTPthE). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=21191%2E878179%2ElTPthE)
---

name: EMILYs List Campaigns
subject: Special elections are putting the writing on the wall for Republicans
body:
EMILYs List women are running in three special elections this year! |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/115047864/569010407/-140265690?refcode=WUEFE917_20250911_SpecialElection_ALL_v1s4&sourceid=1056978&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNTBlYWU0ZS01MDhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aokb8wBEd-uDZoV7Wvx2ZHsfuWywa5Oqdq5uj8khK6E=) Peter,

This year, voters in Texas and Arizona have the chance to elect EMILYs List women in three critical special elections.

These races may not always garner national headlines, but they're one of the best ways to build power before the midterms and show the GOP that they’re in BIG trouble in 2026. name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: We remember. We stand together.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
We honor the lives lost and fight for those still bearing the scars of that day.
24 years ago today, our country changed forever. We lost thousands of lives on September 11th — mothers, fathers, family members and loved ones — and in the years since, too many first responders and survivors have faced serious illnesses caused by the toxic exposure at Ground Zero, the Pentagon and Shanksville. When we say “never forget,” it’s not just about remembering the heartbreak of that day. It’s about standing with the people who gave everything in the aftermath — who ran toward danger while most people ran away. That’s why I’ve fought year after year to ensure the World Trade Center Health Program is fully funded — so every responder, survivor and family member can get the care they deserve for as long as they need it. But with funding shortfalls and administrative delays threatening the care these heroes rely on, _we have to keep pushing until this program is fully funded and secure for the future._ On this anniversary, I’m thinking of the families who lost so much, the survivors still fighting for their health and the first responders who put everything on the line that day and the days that followed. **They represent the best of who we are as Americans — and they deserve the very best from us in return.** Today, we honor them. Tomorrow, we will keep fighting for them. Always. In solidarity, Kirsten Gillibrand

clean_body: We honor the lives lost and fight for those still bearing the scars of that day. 24 years ago today, our country changed forever. We lost thousands of lives on September 11th — mothers, fathers, family members and loved ones — and in the years since, too many first responders and survivors have faced serious illnesses caused by the toxic exposure at Ground Zero, the Pentagon and Shanksville. When we say “never forget,” it’s not just about remembering the heartbreak of that day. It’s about standing with the people who gave everything in the aftermath — who ran toward danger while most people ran away. That’s why I’ve fought year after year to ensure the World Trade Center Health Program is fully funded — so every responder, survivor and family member can get the care they deserve for as long as they need it. But with funding shortfalls and administrative delays threatening the care these heroes rely on, we have to keep pushing until this program is fully funded and secure for the future. On this anniversary, I’m thinking of the families who lost so much, the survivors still fighting for their health and the first responders who put everything on the line that day and the days that followed. They represent the best of who we are as Americans — and they deserve the very best from us in return. Today, we honor them. Tomorrow, we will keep fighting for them. Always. In solidarity, Kirsten Gillibrand *

# [What do we do now? ](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=5233352&post_id=173382353&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzgyMzUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2MjQ1MjIsImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxNjUyMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTUyMzMzNTIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.F3nL2YG_6LC0Z9xuET6M0PjcFl3vT_my3ZebRZPFAZo)
### Plus a round-up of recent appearances and an upcoming series.
| | [Conor Lamb](https://substack.com/@conorlambpa)
---
| Sep 11
---
| [](https://substack.com/@conorlambpa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=5233352&post_id=173382353&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzgyMzUzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NzYyNDUyMiwiZXhwIjoxNzYwMjE2NTIyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNTIzMzM1MiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ci3qZPvopXNFAzL_9nSoNeMg6PnP_pkeZ3a3h9IwYQM&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=5233352&post_id=173382353&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzgyMzUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2MjQ1MjIsImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxNjUyMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTUyMzMzNTIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.F3nL2YG_6LC0Z9xuET6M0PjcFl3vT_my3ZebRZPFAZo&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=5233352&post_id=173382353&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzgyMzUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2MjQ1MjIsImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxNjUyMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTUyMzMzNTIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.F3nL2YG_6LC0Z9xuET6M0PjcFl3vT_my3ZebRZPFAZo)
---
| | [](https://substack..RAkgQnuHwaZeC8aUdYcPcJCIdMstxdCxyhPzlgepH9Y?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/conorlambpa/p/what-do-we-do-now?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

It's hard to put into words how awful the Charlie Kirk shooting was, a horrific crime for which we have to demand justice. This could be anyone tomorrow if we accept violence as normal. We can't do that, on September 11th of all days, if the memory of those victims and all the heroes involved still means something, which I know it does.
Unfortunately, President Trump rushed to blame the "radical left" for "terrorism" even as his own FBI was forced to admit the wrong people were detained and they didn't know who the killer was. Scott Presler, who I [wrote](https://substack.com/redirect/b20e9c62-68f7-42e0-9707-96d73144875f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about earlier this week, [linked](https://substack.com/redirect/60e6c47a-ec8f-445b-b30a-3ead75da8eb7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the murder to the filibuster that same night, saying "They are trying to kill us. What have we got to lose?" The Federalist put up a piece, which I won't link to, entitled "The Democrat Party is a Domestic Terrorist Organization." These and similar posts were designed to radicalize people and likely achieved that effect as they racked up millions and millions of views.
So what do we do now, if there aren't two to tango? That's the problem: the idea that other Americans are your enemy only leads in one direction. Away from productive politics. Toward long and bitter conflicts. But -- and this is the key -- that is not what people in the real world say they want. For years, people on their own doorsteps have described to me how much they hate the gridlock in DC, and the way that politicians talk about each other. These are people who may not know where they stand between Democrats and Republicans. I had a conversation like that just last weekend, with a woman who liked both me and JD Vance. That's Western Pennsylvania, and a lot of the swing states.
If there's one thing I'm confident about, it's that the fate of this democracy will be decided in the near term by people who don't want to be at war with each other. Conflict is exhausting and we need to present a way out. If people begin to accept war as the dominant metaphor, they may very well choose someone like Trump. But he's an awful choice if politics is still about persuasion in any meaningful way.
For that, we need people with a vision of one country, not two. And we need to focus on problems like crime, that are going to be part of this debate whether we like it or not. The general public is ready to hear from us about what _we_ would do, no matter how ridiculous and ineffective we think Trump's ideas might be. And we can't miss that. My father-in-law told me once about Ernie Holmes, a great Pittsburgh Steelers defensive lineman who could become so maniacally fixated on destroying his blocker that the ball-carrier would run right past him. We can't let our eyes off the ball.
In furtherance of that, here are some things I've been up to, and what I'll be doing in the near future:
* Tomorrow (Friday), at this [link](https://open.substack.com/live-stream/58816?utm_source=live-stream-scheduled-upsell) I'll be hosting my first Substack Live conversation, this one with Danny Ceisler, an Army veteran running for Sheriff in Bucks County, Pennsylvania, one of the swingiest of swing counties anywhere in the country. According to polling in Danny's race, Trump's popularity is [sinking](https://substack.com/redirect/762af96b-cb29-4c86-8c08-ee083c896994?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) there, which may tell us something important as Danny steps up to challenge the incumbent Republican sheriff for focusing too much on ICE and not enough on the needs of the community. Tune in on Substack at 1pm EST, Friday 9/12, [here](https://open.substack.com/live-stream/58816?utm_source=live-stream-scheduled-upsell).
* Going forward I'd like to do more of these with people who are trying to make a difference on the ground, especially here in Pennsylvania. Stay tuned.
* I sat this week for a couple of podcasts: [One](https://substack.com/redirect/4b9423af-71e7-4e67-b803-543f097f5ed9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was a joint venture of the "Citycast" teams in Pittsburgh and Philadelphia. The [other](https://substack.com/redirect/443e7e29-8f1c-434c-a5bc-f7519655b160?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was with the Center for American Progress's show, "The Tent." Both of these were before the Kirk shooting and we talked about Fetterman, Trump, town halls, the economy, and more.
* I went on the BBC after the Kirk shooting and my Republican counterpart (a friend from my House days) seemed to compare Kirk to President Lincoln and MLK. These were some of my thoughts:

[](https://substack.com/redirect/bcdfa950-0a66-4952-8f69-7bfda3c1a250?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Hope to see you tomorrow afternoon. Thanks for reading.

name: U.S. Senator Angela Alsobrooks
subject: A note about my friend Wes
body:
I’m fired up to support my friend Wes Moore.
Hi there – it’s Angela Alsobrooks.
I don’t know about you, Peter, but I’m fired up to support my friend Wes Moore in his race to serve another term as Governor of Maryland!
**[If your budget allows for it, would you join me in supporting Wes’s campaign by donating $5 today?_Every dollar will be put to work reelecting Wes so he can keep building a state that leaves no one behind._](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250911-2?refcode=em-20250911-2&refcode2=611_1086043_PboI3V&akid=611%2E1086043%2EPboI3V)**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250911-2?refcode=em-20250911-2&refcode2=611_1086043_PboI3V&akid=611%2E1086043%2EPboI3V)
Take it from me, Peter: There couldn’t be a more crucial time to keep a leader like Wes fighting for Marylanders.
As Donald Trump and extreme Republicans threaten our rights and freedoms, Wes continues to be a tireless defender of Maryland families. And when Trump took to social media last month to attack Wes and spread lies about our state, _Wes didn’t flinch._
That’s because Wes has always been focused on what matters most for Maryland. Since taking office, he’s made major investments in our public schools, taken swift action to support federal workers impacted by Trump’s firings, and delivered real solutions to make the state affordable for every family who dreams of building a life here.
**[I’m all in for Wes – and today, I’m asking you to join me in directly supporting his campaign. Would you donate $5, or any amount that feels right to you, to help reelect my friend Wes?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250911-2?refcode=em-20250911-2&refcode2=611_1086043_PboI3V&akid=611%2E1086043%2EPboI3V)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250911-2?amount=5&refcode=em-20250911-2&refcode2=611_1086043_PboI3V&akid=611%2E1086043%2EPboI3V)**
Your friend in the fight,
Angela Alsobrooks

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20250911-2&t=1&refcode2=611_1086043_PboI3V&akid=611%2E1086043%2EPboI3V)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=2&akid=611%2E1086043%2EPboI3V)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=611%2E1086043%2EPboI3V)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

# [A shot heard 'round the world](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=173387511&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMzg3NTExLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2MjUwMzksImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxNzAzOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.iG8w1fvMpaf8vuqH771OaESIh585ETkE3mIHAJ7IoMM)
### The assassination for Charlie Kirk accelerates more than one alarming trend, as America continues to stumble into the abyss it has been digging for generations
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
| Sep 11| | | ∙| | Preview
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)

It's hard to know where to begin.
Why does the death of a right wing organizer warrant more attention than the deaths of an elected policymaker serving as a state's Speaker of the House, and her husband? Most of the figures who have called for retribution targeting the Left, for instance, had little to say when an even worse attack caused even more death and assaulted democracy even more directly.
### **A writer 's conundrum**
I had planned to write today about the [anniversary](https://substack.com/redirect/fbaa35cf-27f1-411f-b8ca-c76df0abf428?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the attacks on New York and Washington that, 24 years ago, plunged our country into an ignorant spiral of continuing self-destruction & belligerence.
Yesterday's events, however, threaten to accelerate that spiral. While the assassination of right wing pundit Charlie Kirk pales in actual significance relative to the death of 2,977 New Yorkers 24 years ago, the ultimate results of both events may take disturbingly similar shapes.
One big difference is that Kirk's assassination happened a generation after [Washington's bipartisan corruption machine weaponized](https://substack.com/redirect/8bf2f3ad-d7be-4e2e-80ed-3d0a81e13f2b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the 9/11 attacks to degrade democracy, dismantle constitutional rights, and enable state violence with impunity. The aftermath of 9/11 was hateful, destructive, and only enhanced and amplified the cycle of violence to which it responded (and also ironically prompted).
In each of those respects, Kirk's assassination might ultimately make the 9/11 attacks in 2001 pale in comparison.

### **Long simmering crises**
A city that I called home for a decade became a convenient target of our aspiring dictator in the White House this summer. The [deployment of federal troops in Washington, DC](https://substack.com/redirect/ed2c6fd5-494c-4039-a750-59ac852b65dd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) followed a similar, earlier attack on Los Angeles, and paved the path for the more recent [repetition](https://substack.com/redirect/50e51282-2167-42c8-b4dc-c7cb6bdd31d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of that pattern in Chicago, where I also lived for 10 years.
Even before Kirk's assassination, the federal occupations of major cities from coast-to-coast offered disturbing indications of how Trump could mobilize more military assets to suppress dissent, and further destroy whatever remains of our supposed democracy.
No city in America is safe. And while some voices claim that these paramilitary interventions are justified by supposed crime, the most apparent crimes are those by [state actors disregarding human & civil rights](https://substack.com/redirect/ed2c6fd5-494c-4039-a750-59ac852b65dd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), as well as longstanding legal limits constraining their powers.
Meanwhile, Trump opportunistically used [powers pioneered by his predecessors](https://substack.com/redirect/73a109c0-2441-43be-8816-a4aa34f015ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), particularly Obama, to escalate a pattern of arbitrary lethal state violence. The president absurdly claimed that last week's strike targeting a Venezuelan boat--without either charge or trial--was taken in [self-defense](https://substack.com/redirect/9a99cebf-b3b7-4ac7-8859-88e135316bbe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), even though the boat appeared to have [turned around before](https://substack.com/redirect/56fe67e2-0266-4d61-ae70-cf523ebd175f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) being incinerated in a drone strike. This week, a Republican Senator confirmed that the boat was [struck by a drone](https://substack.com/redirect/4797af37-d03b-47f7-9f00-f91953dd1b28?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), making even more painfully clear the connection between [Obama's legacy](https://substack.com/redirect/73a109c0-2441-43be-8816-a4aa34f015ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and Trump's weaponization of it.
