That pattern of casually embracing violence blew back this week, with the assassination of Charlie Kirk.
### **We reap what they 've sown: international double standards**
Some have noted the irony of someone who publicly [preached division](https://substack.com/redirect/a87c3b83-d5fd-4bf5-bd9d-dfc45da66e2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) falling victim to it. It should come as no surprise to witness Kirk fall to the same social ruptures that he built a career out of stoking. Kirk relentlessly punched down at [Blacks](https://substack.com/redirect/6f0ad993-228a-455a-8695-21ed5ec171b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and other minorities, [immigrants](https://substack.com/redirect/78d3fb62-cb48-4c2d-a7fd-33a25ab46f54?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [LGBTQ](https://substack.com/redirect/b06e1411-6632-4914-a43f-b0d639034287?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) people, and others.
All that said, his assassination remains tragic--both because Kirk himself was non-violent, and also particularly of what his assassination will invite. (I'll return to that theme below.)
Other observers have noted the profound irony in so many right wing voices suddenly [claiming to care](https://substack.com/redirect/b82b88be-e7a7-41df-a0a6-9635a5a26964?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about political violence, after having disregarded--for years--political violence in any number of arenas.
Most obvious among them is an ongoing genocide in Gaza enabled by American support. Many of those who today voice concerns about political violence within the U.S. relegated themselves to casting stones from a glass house by ignoring the political violence that Washington continues to enable from [Gaza](https://substack.com/redirect/8f54ad92-351c-4094-9056-dd5a07a4e57f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to [Iran](https://substack.com/redirect/a9d52661-c04a-480e-b47d-f489e4867203?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Qatar](https://substack.com/redirect/7730dc9a-c359-4bc2-965f-a67e8bf8023e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and far [beyond](https://substack.com/redirect/b15e4de5-0741-4060-a40b-07dee2c5c1ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that region.
Is there any reason that Kirk's life should be worth more than those of the tens of thousands of Gazans who Israel has murdered in the past two years alone? [Five Gazans died of preventable malnutrition](https://substack.com/redirect/626a8dc7-d0a7-48a4-968c-e132ca1aaea8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on the same day that Kirk was killed.
Why does the world know none of their names?

### **We reap what they 've sown: domestic double standards**
Double standards emerge not only in Kirk's supporters ignoring political violence abroad while braying about it here at home. They have chosen to disregard--and even outrageously invite--political violence within the U.S., as well.
At least one of them serves in the U.S. Senate. Another is among the world's richest people and owns a global social media network.
In the wake of Kirk's death, Elon Musk [posted](https://substack.com/redirect/b82b88be-e7a7-41df-a0a6-9635a5a26964?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a tweet reading, "If they won't leave us in peace, then our choice is to fight or die."
Who is the "they" towards whom Musk invited violence? Our president wasted no time [declaring](https://substack.com/redirect/b82b88be-e7a7-41df-a0a6-9635a5a26964?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that it is "the radical left" that is "directly responsible for the terrorism we are seeing in our country today."
Sen. Mike Lee (R-UT) [responded](https://substack.com/redirect/fe615332-0284-4c79-96a7-9494e4a49c84?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to news of Kirk's murder by decrying "a cowardly act of violence" targeting someone he described as an "American patriot" and "inspiration to countless young people." Yet, when an elected policymaker--who at the time served as the Speaker of the House in the Minnesota state legislature--was assassinated three months ago, Lee's empathy was strikingly absent. Rather than share condolences, he [chose](https://substack.com/redirect/89173024-d86b-4466-885d-9d9628239d68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) instead to irresponsibly spread conspiracy theories falsely (and inexplicably) [blaming](https://substack.com/redirect/73810799-7c67-493f-a52a-5c468443cc2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the attack on unspecified "Marxists."
The June 2025 murder of Melissa Hortman invites further examination in the wake of Kirk's assassination three months later. While Kirk was the only person hurt in the attack targeting him, Hortman was killed alongside her husband, while another state Senator, John Hoffman, and his wife were also injured.
Why does the death of a right wing organizer warrant more attention than the deaths of an elected policymaker serving as a state's Speaker of the House, and her husband? Most of the figures who have called for retribution targeting the Left, for instance, had little to say when an even worse attack caused even more death and assaulted democracy even more directly.
Hortman's assassination was not the first example, either.
Gabby Giffords was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives in 2006, and represented her Arizona district until an [assassination attempt in 2011](https://substack.com/redirect/cd08c619-6cb8-4487-84c4-f169cd686051?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--which killed a federal judge and five others, including a 9 year-old recently elected Class President at her elementary school, while injuring over a dozen others--left Giffords disabled. Worst yet, the attack on Giffords appeared to have been [invited by right wing figures](https://substack.com/redirect/3b594110-94fb-4d3d-b13a-d745c855e1f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), including Republican vice presidential candidate Sarah Palin.
As much as anyone in America, Rep. Giffords holds the moral high ground to reflect on yesterday's events. She [observed](https://substack.com/redirect/4d1d77c1-34f2-482c-bf05-1941ef45fc82?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) this morning that:
> Our stories are unique, but what Charlie Kirk, President Trump, Melissa Hortman and I all have in common is that someone who wanted to kill us had a gun.
> We can and should talk about political violence, and its toxic relationship to political rhetoric. We can and must talk about social media's role in these moments. We all, as individual Americans, need to do a better job considering our words and their impact.
Giffords went on to [argue](https://substack.com/redirect/4d1d77c1-34f2-482c-bf05-1941ef45fc82?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "anyone who responds to preventable tragedies like this--tragedies that over time begin to erode the very fabric of our country--by refusing to face the problem of gun violence and crime head-on is missing the point."
She also correctly observed that "Americans of both political parties desperately want this violence to end," and then noted that "88% of Republicans support background checks on all gun sales, 75% support laws to get untraceable ghost guns off the streets, and a surprising 81% support requiring a license to own a gun (just like Americans need licenses to drive cars). Yet somehow Congress refuses to act."
Many have noted the irony of Kirk being shot at the precise moment that he was defending gun violence and arguing against the sensible limits that, as Giffords explained today, could protect lives. Kirk [said](https://substack.com/@zellieimani/note/c-154546506?r=97w99), "I think it's worth to have a cost of, unfortunately, some gun deaths every single year so that we can have the Second Amendment to protect our other God-given rights."
Beyond the tragic irony of a public figure effectively inviting his own assassination, how Kirk imagined the utility of the Second Amendment offers a lesson in itself.

### **What does the Second Amendment mean, anyway?**
I have argued [in favor of Second Amendment rights](https://substack.com/redirect/bb085bf7-73bd-43ed-ba80-93d683f84163?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as a check on government tyranny, while recognizing that the constitutional right to bear weapons may have been shorn from its original purpose by events including the militarization of domestic police.
[Looking back on that article, I must admit one error: I evaluated the Oathkeepers based on their words, before their subsequent acts revealed those words to be self-serving and insincere. In line with [my longstanding aspiration](https://substack.com/redirect/b32906ff-420a-45dd-92cd-ae89e5230d1b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to build a left-right alliance to challenge the bipartisan corruption that dominates Washington, I had at one point envisioned the Oathkeepers as potential libertarian allies to intersectional movements, before coming to learn that their [primary commitments were political](https://substack.com/redirect/8c51a19c-a9f8-43c2-9e0c-3a765df4de67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), rather than constitutional.]
The Second Amendment was intended by the Founders to offer a constitutional escape hatch, a check on government tyranny should the mechanisms within the Constitution fail. Yet a textual understanding of the Second Amendment offers no such opportunity today: the only predictable outcomes from flexing one's right to bear arms include the right to be [shot by a SWAT team](https://substack.com/redirect/9bd4c45c-2497-4b15-8f71-e19057daa344?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--or, alternatively, the [opportunity](https://substack.com/redirect/cc3329e8-8e85-4f16-81db-801ec78f3dd2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to become a right-wing influencer aligned with Charlie Kirk.
To offer a meaningful check on tyranny today, the Second Amendment must be interpreted to mean [more](https://substack.com/redirect/dc2e42fb-5a42-4954-9f92-79c573f191a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than simply carrying a weapon.
In the digital era, preserving the anti-tyranny function of the Second Amendment would require interpreting it to include [a right to exfiltrate data from government sources](https://substack.com/redirect/bb085bf7-73bd-43ed-ba80-93d683f84163?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). That would entail an actual right to resistance of the sort that could actually offer a check on tyrannical government.
Instead, we enjoy the worst of both worlds: a functionally useless "right" that offers no protection from government excesses, while ultimately empowering mass murderers, and placing children at increasing risk everywhere from [their homes](https://substack.com/redirect/b4123d39-e5e2-4c43-b9c2-cac6524b5413?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to their [schools](https://substack.com/redirect/b66b0c88-018b-4a34-a59f-0d2176ede12e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

### **Violence only further degrades democracy in America**
Many have noted how the right wing will likely seize on Kirk's assassination as a pretext to escalate and expand the demagoguery to which Los Angeles and DC have already been subjected. [What is happening in Chicago now](https://substack.com/redirect/6326e761-ae0d-46c8-a951-780fa8dc3379?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) could become a template for the rest of the country.
The right wing feeds on perceived marginalization. That was among the themes that drove many of his supporters to rally behind Trump and other proponents of the so-called "[white replacement theory](https://substack.com/redirect/b525024e-4dcb-47ee-af9a-7f83fe38600e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." Kirk's assassination will ultimately empower those narratives, driving supporters of figures like Kirk to see themselves as embattled and increasingly at risk.
His apologists have been keen to [emphasize](https://substack.com/redirect/d3b3e5c5-ec86-44bf-ab10-13902c2a4316?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that Kirk himself was non-violent, and that his activism took the form of debates mostly on college campuses.
While I have a little sympathy for his views, I do appreciate Kirk's method, having worked using similar strategies myself in the past. Like Kirk, I was once a student organizer. Like him, I've spoken at dozens of colleges and universities. Like him, I've been [threatened](https://substack.com/redirect/319974d6-a586-4ab5-9a7a-28b3b841a13e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with violence in retaliation for my political speech.
In fact, I vaguely recall speaking at a Turning Points USA event (at a college in the Midwest sometime in the 2010s that I can't seem to pin down) to share my concerns about government surveillance. Not at all ironically, the students and professors mobilized by Kirk's network shared many of my concerns.
That method--reasoned discourse, and the exchange of ideas--often leads so-called "unholy bedfellows" to find common ground. It is also incompatible with violence.
The violence and state repression that Kirk's assassination has [already invited](https://substack.com/redirect/ed409d86-b91b-4fea-a7ea-dcc4f1cee1e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) promises only to further close the space for that exchange. I don't mourn the loss of Kirk's voice, but I absolutely fear what it foreshadows: calcification of political division, the normalization of domestic political violence, and the expansion of federal occupation far beyond the first cities (LA, DC, and Chicago) forced to endure it.
Whatever ideology motivated Kirk's assassin, the act will only accelerate the consolidation of fascism and drive the stake already stabbing the heart of democracy even deeper.
I've seen some voices celebrate Kirk's murder. They are unfortunately confused. A public figure falling prey to his own creation is ironic, and might offer some basis for schadenfreude. But these events--and what they place at stake--are too important to settle for mere self-gratification.
The "other side" losing a voice might seem like a victory to frustrated people eager for long overdue change. But remember what the Pentagon learned in Iraq (after forgetting the same lesson learned in Vietnam): killing one antagonist only ensures that 50 more will take their place.
Ideas can't be killed. Killing people who promote them only further empowers those ideas.
Meanwhile, the continuing degradation of democracy leaves us all with less room to advocate. State violence responding to Kirk's assassination will harm everyone, including the legions of Americans who wish for a sane future.
Kirk was not one of us. But celebrating his assassination is both senseless, and also self-marginalizing.
At the end of the day, Kirk's assassination could offer an [important historical reminder](https://substack.com/redirect/7c855ae0-81b7-43fd-a815-fdb7cc51e6f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): political violence is even [more](https://substack.com/redirect/a6bb313f-ca2a-4dd1-8b05-c233e7909ca2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) American than apple pie. Anyone who forgot [what happened](https://substack.com/redirect/99a81b9a-31ed-4d1d-944a-c6a8ed97b7a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to MLK (and [the movement he helped lead](https://substack.com/redirect/8e7f4efd-6d30-4fcf-aac1-07b7edb94b03?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) might find [a great deal](https://substack.com/redirect/dafb62c9-0af2-4abd-8dbb-806354da040b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to [learn](https://substack.com/redirect/6959915e-7eee-488b-b1cc-797c4906fb41?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from his example.
_Paid subscribers can access an additional section exploring my thoughts on the complex relationship between yesterday 's events and those that made Luigi Mangione into a folk hero..._

The assassination for Charlie Kirk accelerates more than one alarming trend, as America continues to stumble into the abyss it has been digging for generations | | | Sep 11| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | It's hard to know where to begin. Why does the death of a right wing organizer warrant more attention than the deaths of an elected policymaker serving as a state's Speaker of the House, and her husband? Most of the figures who have called for retribution targeting the Left, for instance, had little to say when an even worse attack caused even more death and assaulted democracy even more directly. A writer 's conundrum I had planned to write today about the of the attacks on New York and Washington that, 24 years ago, plunged our country into an ignorant spiral of continuing self-destruction & belligerence. Yesterday's events, however, threaten to accelerate that spiral. While the assassination of right wing pundit Charlie Kirk pales in actual significance relative to the death of 2,977 New Yorkers 24 years ago, the ultimate results of both events may take disturbingly similar shapes. One big difference is that Kirk's assassination happened a generation after the 9/11 attacks to degrade democracy, dismantle constitutional rights, and enable state violence with impunity. The aftermath of 9/11 was hateful, destructive, and only enhanced and amplified the cycle of violence to which it responded (and also ironically prompted). In each of those respects, Kirk's assassination might ultimately make the 9/11 attacks in 2001 pale in comparison. Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! Help inform your friends by sharing this post. | | ---|---|--- Long simmering crises A city that I called home for a decade became a convenient target of our aspiring dictator in the White House this summer. The followed a similar, earlier attack on Los Angeles, and paved the path for the more recent of that pattern in Chicago, where I also lived for 10 years. Even before Kirk's assassination, the federal occupations of major cities from coast-to-coast offered disturbing indications of how Trump could mobilize more military assets to suppress dissent, and further destroy whatever remains of our supposed democracy. No city in America is safe. And while some voices claim that these paramilitary interventions are justified by supposed crime, the most apparent crimes are those by , as well as longstanding legal limits constraining their powers. Meanwhile, Trump opportunistically used , particularly Obama, to escalate a pattern of arbitrary lethal state violence. The president absurdly claimed that last week's strike targeting a Venezuelan boat--without either charge or trial--was taken in , even though the boat appeared to have being incinerated in a drone strike. This week, a Republican Senator confirmed that the boat was , making even more painfully clear the connection between and Trump's weaponization of it. To receive new posts from Chronicles of a Dying Empire,sign up for a free subscription! To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber. That pattern of casually embracing violence blew back this week, with the assassination of Charlie Kirk. We reap what they 've sown: international double standards Some have noted the irony of someone who publicly falling victim to it. It should come as no surprise to witness Kirk fall to the same social ruptures that he built a career out of stoking. Kirk relentlessly punched down at and other minorities, , people, and others. All that said, his assassination remains tragic--both because Kirk himself was non-violent, and also particularly of what his assassination will invite. (I'll return to that theme below.) Other observers have noted the profound irony in so many right wing voices suddenly about political violence, after having disregarded--for years--political violence in any number of arenas. Most obvious among them is an ongoing genocide in Gaza enabled by American support. Many of those who today voice concerns about political violence within the U.S. relegated themselves to casting stones from a glass house by ignoring the political violence that Washington continues to enable from to , , and far that region. Is there any reason that Kirk's life should be worth more than those of the tens of thousands of Gazans who Israel has murdered in the past two years alone? on the same day that Kirk was killed. Why does the world know none of their names? We reap what they 've sown: domestic double standards Double standards emerge not only in Kirk's supporters ignoring political violence abroad while braying about it here at home. They have chosen to disregard--and even outrageously invite--political violence within the U.S., as well. At least one of them serves in the U.S. Senate. Another is among the world's richest people and owns a global social media network. In the wake of Kirk's death, Elon Musk a tweet reading, "If they won't leave us in peace, then our choice is to fight or die." Who is the "they" towards whom Musk invited violence? Our president wasted no time that it is "the radical left" that is "directly responsible for the terrorism we are seeing in our country today." Sen. Mike Lee (R-UT) to news of Kirk's murder by decrying "a cowardly act of violence" targeting someone he described as an "American patriot" and "inspiration to countless young people." Yet, when an elected policymaker--who at the time served as the Speaker of the House in the Minnesota state legislature--was assassinated three months ago, Lee's empathy was strikingly absent. Rather than share condolences, he instead to irresponsibly spread conspiracy theories falsely (and inexplicably) the attack on unspecified "Marxists." The June 2025 murder of Melissa Hortman invites further examination in the wake of Kirk's assassination three months later. While Kirk was the only person hurt in the attack targeting him, Hortman was killed alongside her husband, while another state Senator, John Hoffman, and his wife were also injured. Why does the death of a right wing organizer warrant more attention than the deaths of an elected policymaker serving as a state's Speaker of the House, and her husband? Most of the figures who have called for retribution targeting the Left, for instance, had little to say when an even worse attack caused even more death and assaulted democracy even more directly. Hortman's assassination was not the first example, either. Gabby Giffords was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives in 2006, and represented her Arizona district until an killed a federal judge and five others, including a 9 year-old recently elected Class President at her elementary school, while injuring over a dozen others--left Giffords disabled. Worst yet, the attack on Giffords appeared to have been , including Republican vice presidential candidate Sarah Palin. As much as anyone in America, Rep. Giffords holds the moral high ground to reflect on yesterday's events. She this morning that: Our stories are unique, but what Charlie Kirk, President Trump, Melissa Hortman and I all have in common is that someone who wanted to kill us had a gun. We can and should talk about political violence, and its toxic relationship to political rhetoric. We can and must talk about social media's role in these moments. We all, as individual Americans, need to do a better job considering our words and their impact. Giffords went on to that "anyone who responds to preventable tragedies like this--tragedies that over time begin to erode the very fabric of our country--by refusing to face the problem of gun violence and crime head-on is missing the point." She also correctly observed that "Americans of both political parties desperately want this violence to end," and then noted that "88% of Republicans support background checks on all gun sales, 75% support laws to get untraceable ghost guns off the streets, and a surprising 81% support requiring a license to own a gun (just like Americans need licenses to drive cars). Yet somehow Congress refuses to act." Many have noted the irony of Kirk being shot at the precise moment that he was defending gun violence and arguing against the sensible limits that, as Giffords explained today, could protect lives. Kirk , "I think it's worth to have a cost of, unfortunately, some gun deaths every single year so that we can have the Second Amendment to protect our other God-given rights." Beyond the tragic irony of a public figure effectively inviting his own assassination, how Kirk imagined the utility of the Second Amendment offers a lesson in itself. What does the Second Amendment mean, anyway? I have argued as a check on government tyranny, while recognizing that the constitutional right to bear weapons may have been shorn from its original purpose by events including the militarization of domestic police. to build a left-right alliance to challenge the bipartisan corruption that dominates Washington, I had at one point envisioned the Oathkeepers as potential libertarian allies to intersectional movements, before coming to learn that their , rather than constitutional.] The Second Amendment was intended by the Founders to offer a constitutional escape hatch, a check on government tyranny should the mechanisms within the Constitution fail. Yet a textual understanding of the Second Amendment offers no such opportunity today: the only predictable outcomes from flexing one's right to bear arms include the right to be , alternatively, the to become a right-wing influencer aligned with Charlie Kirk. To offer a meaningful check on tyranny today, the Second Amendment must be interpreted to mean than simply carrying a weapon. In the digital era, preserving the anti-tyranny function of the Second Amendment would require interpreting it to include . That would entail an actual right to resistance of the sort that could actually offer a check on tyrannical government. Instead, we enjoy the worst of both worlds: a functionally useless "right" that offers no protection from government excesses, while ultimately empowering mass murderers, and placing children at increasing risk everywhere from to their . Violence only further degrades democracy in America Many have noted how the right wing will likely seize on Kirk's assassination as a pretext to escalate and expand the demagoguery to which Los Angeles and DC have already been subjected. could become a template for the rest of the country. The right wing feeds on perceived marginalization. That was among the themes that drove many of his supporters to rally behind Trump and other proponents of the so-called "." Kirk's assassination will ultimately empower those narratives, driving supporters of figures like Kirk to see themselves as embattled and increasingly at risk. His apologists have been keen to that Kirk himself was non-violent, and that his activism took the form of debates mostly on college campuses. While I have a little sympathy for his views, I do appreciate Kirk's method, having worked using similar strategies myself in the past. Like Kirk, I was once a student organizer. Like him, I've spoken at dozens of colleges and universities. Like him, I've been with violence in retaliation for my political speech. name: GavinNewsom.com
subject: We could lose.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Yes on 50, The Election Rigging Response Act, Governor Newsom’s Ballot Measure Committee
body:
There’s only one way we’ll win this election, and that is together.

**$3.**
That is the donation we are asking you to contribute to help Gavin Newsom pass the Election Rigging Response Act in California. Yes, $3.
$3 because all we want is a fair chance to take back the House in 2026 — but Republicans are rigging their maps in Texas, Missouri, and other states to rig the next election before a single vote is cast.
$3 because one man with a history of giving to extremist causes and candidates has already contributed $30 million to beat us. That means we are getting outraised and outspent. Not great.
$3 because unless we can catch up before ballots drop in 3 weeks, we could very easily lose this election.
Folks, we know that $3 may not seem like it can do much against what we’re up against, but as you read this lots and lots of people are chipping-in their $3.
Now they hope you’ll do the same. Because there’s only one way we’ll win this election, and that is together.
So what do you say?
[**Can Gavin Newsom and Democrats everywhere count on you to contribute $3 to help us pass the Election Rigging Response Act in California? We would not ask if it was not totally necessary for us to win.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/erra-em?refcode=em250911&refcode2=2538_4767699_BrkIRT&akid=2538%2E4767699%2EBrkIRT)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_

Team Newsom

| | Dear Friend,I hear from people all the time that Thomas Sowell is one of their heroes. For those people--and you may be among them--I'm thrilled to share a digital copy of one of his commentaries with you, absolutely free: _[**_"_****_Trickle Down " Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich._****_"_**](https://e.pm.tarise.com/re?l=D0I2xufe0hIteknop5I8ITmfg5ph2e&s=NBMEAEKGHCNIJPDM&req=l%3DD0I2xufe04Iteknop5I8ITmfg5ph2e%26s%3DOADHCFDBDLNMONPJ)_
---
| [](https://e.pm.tarise.com/re?l=D0I2xufe0hIteknop5I9ITmfg5ph2e&s=NBMEAEKGHCNIJPDM&req=l%3DD0I2xufe04Iteknop5I9ITmfg5ph2e%26s%3DOADHCFDBDLNMONPJ)
---
| | "Tax cuts for the rich" is a favorite argument of those who oppose lower tax rates. But does imposing higher tax rates on wealthy Americans actually increase the amount of revenue coming into the federal government? Hoover Institution Senior Fellow **Thomas Sowell takes an empirical approach** in his commentary, [**_"_****_Trickle Down " Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich._****_"_**](https://e.pm.tarise.com/re?l=D0I2xufe0hIteknop5IaITmfg5ph2e&s=NBMEAEKGHCNIJPDM&req=l%3DD0I2xufe04Iteknop5IaITmfg5ph2e%26s%3DOADHCFDBDLNMONPJ) Sowell separates fact from fiction when it comes to tax rates and uses real life data and examples to prove that lower tax rates for all actually lead to greater revenue for the government. As a friend and supporter of the Hoover Institution, we are proud to offer you this valuable, thought-provoking resource free of charge.[**Click here to get your free copy of**** _ "Trickle Down" Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich" _****now.**](https://e.pm.tarise.com/re?l=D0I2xufe0hIteknop5IbITmfg5ph2e&s=NBMEAEKGHCNIJPDM&req=l%3DD0I2xufe04Iteknop5IbITmfg5ph2e%26s%3DOADHCFDBDLNMONPJ) Thanks,Greg Greg Stamps | _Online Development_ Hoover Institution | Stanford University
---
_With its eminent scholars and world-renowned Library and Archives, the Hoover Institution is a non-partisan, data-driven policy research organization that seeks to improve the human condition by advancing ideas that promote economic prosperity, national security, and democratic governance. Over more than one hundred years, Hoover's work has directly led to policies that have produced greater freedom, democracy, and opportunity in the United States and around the world._

body:
Help me hold Trump accountable.
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115049531/569015867/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYWFmYjliNS01NjhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EGTwTajaGoERbwNfObl7-89-YDNTssBJVgWBj3WAD8I=)
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115049532/569015881/-309737922?refcode=250911_RG_districtfrontlines-porter&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYWFmYjliNS01NjhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EGTwTajaGoERbwNfObl7-89-YDNTssBJVgWBj3WAD8I=)
Hi, it’s Democratic Congressman Robert Garcia.
In redistricting, the 42nd is being redrawn to take on more Republican voters than any other district in California and includes portions of Orange County that Katie Porter represented in Congress.
The newly incorporated areas of our redrawn district were represented by a true fighter. Katie Porter refused to let corporate interests drown out the voices of working Californians and always put people first.
I may not have the whiteboard magic like Katie, but I’m just as committed to making sure you have more power in our government than the morally bankrupt billionaires cozying up to Trump.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115049533/569015896/789508428?refcode=250911_RG_districtfrontlines-porter&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYWFmYjliNS01NjhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EGTwTajaGoERbwNfObl7-89-YDNTssBJVgWBj3WAD8I=)
**Right now, I’m the leading Democrat on the House Oversight Committee — and as you’ve probably seen, we’re fighting like hell to hold Trump accountable for his Epstein cover-up.**
**But here’s the truth: The Trump Administration is corrupt as hell. Whether it’s Stephen Miller’s attacks on immigrants, Trump’s illegal use of our military in American cities, or his refusal to release the Epstein Files, we’ve seen what they are capable of. That means my role on the House Oversight Committee is more important than ever.**
**[That’s why I need you with me in this fight — starting now. Will you chip in what you can to help me defend my new district to fight back against Trump and his corrupt allies in Washington, hold them accountable for violating the rule of law, and end their reign of terror once and for all?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115049534/569015897/789508428?refcode=250911_RG_districtfrontlines-porter&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYWFmYjliNS01NjhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EGTwTajaGoERbwNfObl7-89-YDNTssBJVgWBj3WAD8I=)**
[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115049535/569015898/-309737922?refcode=250911_RG_districtfrontlines-porter&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYWFmYjliNS01NjhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EGTwTajaGoERbwNfObl7-89-YDNTssBJVgWBj3WAD8I=)
Trump already deployed the National Guard to Los Angeles in a retaliatory move against our governor.
This is a man who weaponizes our military against American citizens, simply because he can. In this moment, we cannot afford to back down — we have to keep our opposition strong, and our fight steadfast.
I’m grateful to have a district full of folks like you who refuse to sit back when our democracy is on the line.
Thanks for standing with me.
Robert Garcia
P.S. Here's a picture of the newly-drawn map so you see what my new district looks like:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115049536/569015899/789508428?refcode=250911_RG_districtfrontlines-porter&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYWFmYjliNS01NjhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EGTwTajaGoERbwNfObl7-89-YDNTssBJVgWBj3WAD8I=)

* * *

Whether it’s Stephen Miller’s attacks on immigrants, Trump’s illegal use of our military in American cities, or his refusal to release the Epstein Files, we’ve seen what they are capable of. That means my role on the House Oversight Committee is more important than ever. = = Trump already deployed the National Guard to Los Angeles in a retaliatory move against our governor. This is a man who weaponizes our military against American citizens, simply because he can. In this moment, we cannot afford to back down — we have to keep our opposition strong, and our fight steadfast. I’m grateful to have a district full of folks like you who refuse to sit back when our democracy is on the line. Thanks for standing with me. Robert Garcia P.S. Here's a picture of the newly-drawn map so you see what my new district looks like: = * Paid for by Robert Garcia for Congress Robert Garcia for Congress 65 Pine Ave #348 Long Beach, CA 90802 United States This email was sent to . We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click to unsubscribe.

# [Trump gives BLUNT answer to reporter about Charlie Kirk assassination aftermath](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=173279608&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMjc5NjA4LCJpYXQiOjE3NTc2Mjc1MDUsImV4cCI6MTc2MDIxOTUwNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMwNzI3ODAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7dDidtVjCDN_ltFEy4Ex58ZkuCb8oAhWTDI0k31gwYM)
### "We have a group of radical left lunatics out there,... we're going to get that problem solved..."
| | [Politibrawl](https://substack.com/@politibrawl)
---
| Sep 11
---
| [](https://substack.com/@politibrawl)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=173279608&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMjc5NjA4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NzYyNzUwNSwiZXhwIjoxNzYwMjE5NTA1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzA3Mjc4MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.bKi8nr4cPUnMisSFNXPljCduFuwQHsbiBWS2BEeTMHY&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

---

President Donald Trump told a reporter Thursday evening that he plans to address the presence of **["radical left lunatics" in America:](https://substack.com/redirect/b0ef86dd-9b54-4b11-aa60-3e50b020a3bd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** "We're gonna get that problem solved..."
[](https://substack.com/redirect/563cea00-00a0-4940-8472-09d002d26119?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Authorities are still searching for the shooter after releasing photos of the person of interest earlier today.
The administration **[has offered an $100,000 reward](https://substack.com/redirect/a5ca11b9-5e3d-4561-8401-241e2c688c4f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for information leading to the arrest of the suspect.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f0c76c2f-d1f9-4a24-8981-d1d77f7cf612?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
# **Pete Hegseth stops everything, delivers beautiful prayer & tribute to Charlie Kirk with American soldiers**
[](https://substack.com/redirect/6daf0f60-4570-4edd-bc82-ba24ac61d6b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Click To Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/6daf0f60-4570-4edd-bc82-ba24ac61d6b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# Rifle and ammunition linked to Kirk shooting covered in transgender and Antifa writing: report
Law enforcement **[sources report](https://substack.com/redirect/3cb4be23-d342-4343-8576-9dbb1202ba1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that the recovered rifle--an imported .30-06-caliber Mauser bolt-action--was found wrapped in a towel in a wooded area and contained ammunition engraved with "transgender and anti-fascist ideology," though the Justice Department is still investigating these claims.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3cb4be23-d342-4343-8576-9dbb1202ba1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Shooting specifics** : Kirk was fatally shot in the neck by a single high-caliber round from approximately 200 yards away while answering an audience member's question about mass shootings committed by transgender people during his American Comeback Tour event.
* **Weapon characteristics** : The fatal shot was described as similar to ammunition used in long-range sniper rifles or for hunting big game, indicating the shooter used professional-grade equipment for the assassination.

* * *
# Left-wing social media platform Bluesky tells users not to celebrate Charlie Kirk's death
Left-wing X copycat platform Bluesky **[warned users](https://substack.com/redirect/f8cd3284-fada-4bf5-b226-2abca1e4fb77?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that celebrating Charlie Kirk's assassination violates its community guidelines, while other major platforms including Meta, Reddit, Discord, and YouTube also announced measures to restrict content glorifying the killing or sharing graphic footage.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f8cd3284-fada-4bf5-b226-2abca1e4fb77?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Widespread platform response** : Social media companies scrambled to contain raw footage of the assassination that spread rapidly across platforms, with most mainstream outlets avoiding showing the moment of impact while moderators worked to remove celebratory posts from critics.
* **Political reactions and controversy** : The assassination sparked outrage over some Democrat politicians and liberal pundits' comments that appeared to blame Kirk for his own shooting, while Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez warned the killing "risks an uncorking of political chaos and violence."

* * *
**[Together with Ifrah Law](https://substack.com/redirect/76c196dd-1bc3-426d-8891-091069942082?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **Ifrah Law heightens the success of iGaming clients through every phase of their business cycle**
Led by a managing partner who has been **[at the center of every significant court case affecting online gaming,](https://substack.com/redirect/76c196dd-1bc3-426d-8891-091069942082?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Ifrah Law operates at the cutting edge of technology, innovation, and regulation.
Its lawyers represent industry players throughout the entire business cycle, from the formation of a corporation or licensing relationship, through marketing, partnering, growth, and disputes, to profitable exits.
We're proud to share that all partners at Ifrah Law have been ranked by Chambers and Partners, the gold standard for evaluating legal excellence worldwide.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/76c196dd-1bc3-426d-8891-091069942082?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/76c196dd-1bc3-426d-8891-091069942082?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# "Ugliest moment in politics" \- Democrats refuse to pray for Charlie Kirk
Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-CO) **[requested a spoken prayer for Charlie Kirk](https://substack.com/redirect/b6b746c9-ee94-4dbb-bb11-f067f75271c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** on the House floor, Wednesday evening, after Speaker Mike Johnson held a moment of silence, leading to heated objections from about three dozen Democrats who yelled "no" and demanded gun control laws instead.
* **Chaotic House floor scene** : The dispute escalated into shouting matches, with Rep. Jahana Hayes (D-CT) yelling about school shootings, another Democrat shouting "Pass some gun laws!", and Rep. Anna Paulina Luna responding "You f***ing own this!" before Speaker Johnson repeatedly banged his gavel to restore order.

[](https://substack.com/redirect/04d3c5ed-b192-4023-8c98-7a8dbd16c554?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Boebert's justification** : Boebert defended her request by saying "I believe silent prayer gets silent results," arguing for the need for verbal prayer rather than just the moment of silence that had been observed.
* **Just Ugly:** Daily Mail reporter Jon Michael Raasch **[described](https://substack.com/redirect/01e2d50d-5001-41a1-bfa1-8a1cbe48d0da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the scene as "Possibly the ugliest moment I've ever witnessed in politics."
* **FBI custody update** : FBI Director Kash Patel announced Wednesday night that a person of interest believed to be the shooter had been taken into custody, but later announced the person had been interrogated and released as the manhunt continues.

* * *
# Trump demands death penalty for "animal" suspected killer of Ukrainian girl
President Donald Trump demanded on Truth Social that Decarlos Brown Jr. **[receive the death penalty](https://substack.com/redirect/18486098-c594-4ab5-838f-4b88fed846db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for the killing of Ukrainian refugee Iryna Zarutska, calling him an "ANIMAL" and stating "there can be no doubt" about his guilt, while Attorney General Pam Bondi vowed to seek the maximum penalty.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/18486098-c594-4ab5-838f-4b88fed846db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Federal and State Charges:** Brown, 34, faces both a federal charge of committing an act causing death on a mass transportation system and a state first-degree murder charge for the August 22 stabbing attack on a Charlotte light-rail train that was captured on surveillance footage.
* **Victim's Background:** Zarutska, 23, fled Ukraine in 2022 after Russia's invasion and came to America seeking safety; her family reportedly turned down repatriation of her remains, saying she "loved America," while her father couldn't attend the funeral due to the ongoing war.
* **Suspect's Mental Health and Criminal History:** Brown has been arrested at least 14 times since 2007 and suffers from schizophrenia according to his mother, with his sister believing he attacked Zarutska because he thought she was reading his mind; he was previously released on cashless bail earlier this year after reporting delusions about "man-made material" controlling his actions.

* * *

### **Take Advantage Of Our[ 15% Off Sale On All Paid Subscriptions While it Lasts The Next 32 Hours!](https://substack.com/redirect/263df118-39ef-48c0-93b5-6bbb2e6a5a33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[I want 15% off!](https://substack.com/redirect/f95efa62-ae94-4327-b53c-42c495303884?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Friends,
Yesterday was another brutal reminder of the country we live in.
The one right-wing interests and the Republican Party have delivered to us. It was also a reminder of the country we could be if we have the courage to make it happen, and ignored pinheaded protocols and "traditions" [to fight like hell for our kids' lives](https://substack.com/redirect/a2a9fddb-5ba0-431e-800e-4574ae44ad83?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
While the assassination of Charlie Kirk dominated all the headlines, [a school shooting in Colorado ](https://substack.com/redirect/4db3d500-055d-4440-aee6-219a075a4afa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[that left children bleeding in hospital beds](https://substack.com/redirect/4db3d500-055d-4440-aee6-219a075a4afa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) _was barely a blip on the news radar. And that contrast says everything about this uniquely American pandemic.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0681c6fd-377e-42ad-b29f-ae306f116171?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## America's Gun Plague
Cliff opened the show by laying the truth bare. We're raising generations of kids traumatized by escaping school shootings and "active shooter drills," hiding in closets while their teachers play human shield.
Colorado has lived through two of the worst mass shootings--Columbine and Aurora--[and now more children were shot in their classrooms](https://substack.com/redirect/4db3d500-055d-4440-aee6-219a075a4afa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Yet Republicans are owned by Big Gun, and chose to make this an identifying issue for alienated white men who vote heavily in primaries.
This has blocked even the simplest and most common-sense reforms.
Universal background checks, for example, consistently has 90% support in America.
But congressional gerrymanders, two Senators each from many states with more cows than people and a pasty, deranged, jibbering f*k named Mitch McConnell, who had the GOP filibuster even modest steps after Newtown, sealed many children's fates.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/53b25879-ae01-41c7-b11f-53a53a5bebca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.GTRNpx1mA8ZJOYVYu0aWizn1EL8iM1ATZPWdHvagCpI?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=173372078)
Australia, after its Port Arthur massacre, said "enough" and enacted many of the exact reforms America needs. Gun deaths dropped from American to West European levels, which is to say by 70-80%.
We could do here in the United States tomorrow.
But they've made their message clear. Namely, your child's life is not a priority, which these days they make crystal clear by cutting school lunches, [trying to give your kids the measles in school](https://substack.com/redirect/0d2f1978-be08-4245-8468-bccec49af169?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and protecting the files of a pedophile.
Because those files just may expose [Trump as the loutish, rancid, rotting elephant-dung-heap we all already know him to be](https://substack.com/redirect/a69f0a26-44c3-41bb-a3d9-cc56240cf6f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Republicans has also developed [a love affair with the bro-niverse](https://substack.com/redirect/fcf4274f-a652-472d-ab2f-b0e0e4faba5e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). So any incovenience white guys--who buy guns most often--may endure while suffering through the indignity of background checks to buy battlefield-ready weapons, simply must not be tolerated.
## The Second Amendment Hoax
Cliff reminded us of Warren Burger. He was Nixon's conservative Chief Justice, yet it was he who called the NRA's individual-rights interpretation of the Second Amendment as "the greatest fraud ever perpetrated" by an interest group.
The [Founders intended ](https://substack.com/redirect/d3bd8b60-8ed7-43fa-95a3-34750345e6dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[well-regulated militias](https://substack.com/redirect/d3bd8b60-8ed7-43fa-95a3-34750345e6dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[--not a free-for-all where anyone can stockpile AR-15s](https://substack.com/redirect/d3bd8b60-8ed7-43fa-95a3-34750345e6dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Many states you wouldn't exect--Wyoming, Texas, Kentucky, Ohio, among them--banned concealed carry in the 19th Century.
Tombstone, Arizona, and many towns out West like it, literally confiscated guns at the edge of town (in fact, the infamous Battle at the OK Corral began because members of the Clanton Gang were illegally carrying guns in town).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/389a5b4e-8cb0-4ae8-8e5a-10ca2d2abce5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.GTRNpx1mA8ZJOYVYu0aWizn1EL8iM1ATZPWdHvagCpI?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=173372078)
They knew drunken guys with guns were what we might call "bad for business."
The radical rewrite of the Constitution by this Supreme Court and its right-wing sponsors is nothing more than[ billionaire-funded extremism](https://substack.com/redirect/2ee57a5d-8302-4bd2-97ff-49f188e3cc76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) dressed up as law. (See, also, Trump immunity, voting rights destruction, abortion rights eliminated, money gushing into politics and so much this group of Opus Dei traitors have done to our country).
## The Power of Images
David Schuster made a chilling but undeniable point. Americans won't really get it until they _see_ what these war scenes look like.
The video of Kirk's death horrified millions.
Imagine if Sandy Hook or Parkland had released photos of what those rifles did to children (some parents wanted this, and I'm unclear why it didn't happen. But we should do this with all sorts of shootings every day).
[Parents especially would be innately horrified. ](https://substack.com/redirect/f036ffba-0e6f-4c92-8b53-0efcbb4a5a53?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Many of the children were only identifiable by DNA. Just as Emmett Till's open casket shocked a nation into action, maybe America needs to be forced to confront the carnage it has chosen to ignore while re-watching Sean Hannity's spittle reaching new heights.
Fred Poag, our guest, a Marine veteran and new Blue Amp COO, drove the point home: assault rifles aren't like the movies. They're battlefield weapons that tear apart organs and bone like a bomb going off inside your body.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/19ae76e7-403f-4faa-ad91-540d8c5f6e1e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.GTRNpx1mA8ZJOYVYu0aWizn1EL8iM1ATZPWdHvagCpI?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=173372078)
That's what our kids are facing. That's why teachers' bodies can't even shield them. And yet Republicans treat owning one as some sacred ["masculine identity" ritual](https://substack.com/redirect/91a4e9d1-d486-4726-9631-8f89d5127fdf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
It's madness.
## Democracy or the Bullet Box
Finally, Cliff warned against those--even on the left--who are losing faith in democracy and flirting with violence. That path leads to ruination.
Our job is to argue, organize, protest,[ create the best content](https://substack.com/redirect/f20a6605-126d-4094-9b39-cf0966d3bd43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), use the courts and outvote them. Ballots still work. Democrats just flipped a Virginia seat with 75% of the vote, moving within one seat in the House of forcing the release of Epstein files.
We've recently won in Iowa, Florida, and have another special election on the 23rd for a U.S. House Seat, which a Democrat should win. Then we'll have enough votes to force the release of the Epstein files in the House.
Every vote matters. Every victory matters.
The true danger isn't that ballots have failed us--it's that cynicism convinces us to stop using them. That's when democracy truly dies.
* * *
**Bottom line:** We don't need to repeal the Second Amendment. We need a sane, un-corrupted Supreme Court that actually believes in the law, and political leaders with the spine to say **enough**.
Until then, the gun lobby, billionaire extremists, [a sickly, dementia-addled Donald Trump](https://substack.com/redirect/da1cd58b-5f9d-4357-a2dd-dba8b40bcb47?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and his MAGA enablers will continue to hold this country hostage.
But we're not powerless. We can vote, we can organize, and we can tell the truth--loudly.
Let's do it together.
--Cliff

The Power of Images David Schuster made a chilling but undeniable point. Americans won't really get it until they see what these war scenes look like. The video of Kirk's death horrified millions. Imagine if Sandy Hook or Parkland had released photos of what those rifles did to children (some parents wanted this, and I'm unclear why it didn't happen. But we should do this with all sorts of shootings every day). Many of the children were only identifiable by DNA. Just as Emmett Till's open casket shocked a nation into action, maybe America needs to be forced to confront the carnage it has chosen to ignore while re-watching Sean Hannity's spittle reaching new heights. Fred Poag, our guest, a Marine veteran and new Blue Amp COO, drove the point home: assault rifles aren't like the movies. They're battlefield weapons that tear apart organs and bone like a bomb going off inside your body. | | ---|---|--- That's what our kids are facing. That's why teachers' bodies can't even shield them. And yet Republicans treat owning one as some sacred . It's madness. Democracy or the Bullet Box Finally, Cliff warned against those--even on the left--who are losing faith in democracy and flirting with violence. That path leads to ruination. Our job is to argue, organize, protest,, use the courts and outvote them. Ballots still work. Democrats just flipped a Virginia seat with 75% of the vote, moving within one seat in the House of forcing the release of Epstein files. We've recently won in Iowa, Florida, and have another special election on the 23rd for a U.S. House Seat, which a Democrat should win. Then we'll have enough votes to force the release of the Epstein files in the House. Every vote matters. Every victory matters. The true danger isn't that ballots have failed us--it's that cynicism convinces us to stop using them. That's when democracy truly dies. Bottom line: We don't need to repeal the Second Amendment. We need a sane, un-corrupted Supreme Court that actually believes in the law, and political leaders with the spine to say enough. Until then, the gun lobby, billionaire extremists, , and his MAGA enablers will continue to hold this country hostage. But we're not powerless. We can vote, we can organize, and we can tell the truth--loudly. Let's do it together. --Cliff Take Advantage of Thank you , , , , , and many others for tuning into my live video with and ! Join me for my next live video in the app. | Get more from Cliff Schecter in the Substack app ---|--- Available for iOS and Android You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Cliff Schecter PO Box 8384, Cincinnati, OH 45208 ? ?

name: Josh Harder
subject: My thoughts on Charlie Kirk
body:
Peter,

The news over the last 24 hours has been deeply disturbing.

Charlie Kirk’s murder at Utah Valley University was horrifying, despicable, and utterly against everything our country stands for. Pam and I have sent our heartfelt condolences to his wife and young children. What they’re going through is unimaginable.

Across the country, and across the ideological spectrum, people are rightly shocked. Political violence is a scourge on our democracy. It’s a tragic symptom of a political system that’s become deeply broken.

I’m not going to use this message to offer sweeping commentary about what this moment means. The truth is, I need more time to fully gather my thoughts.

But I do want to say this clearly: every elected official must speak out - right now - and unequivocally condemn this violence.

We can disagree without dehumanizing each other. We can debate without demonizing each other. And above all, we have to keep each other safe.

— Josh

Josh Harder for Congress
PO Box 4220
Manteca, CA 95337
United States

name: Marty O'Donnell
subject: Carrying Charlie’s Torch
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARTY FOR CONGRESS**
body:
We cannot let Charlie’s message fade. His voice may be gone, but his mission must live on. Together, we must carry forward the values he championed and ensure they echo for generations to come. | [](http://links.martynevada.com/u/click?_t=3a5223257aca4a93ac027b228c2c976d&_m=f02ef82ce79842958c2488eb5c643a77&_e=DuR0Hx5ngH-Xe9vhAZqJZst4jUcwLDCi1TaKp2OWyGu5UfXrI3Tmpoo_Ct_CnYyXL5vuzW0tQqYfdmKuZqh9_vp8HpGA8dTKFvOXP1WAXD5UG6TZ0eppxo40b7MWp-KH_lLsPGJFcLDGMAWL7sb3GoHLgs7p8G42JtvElqz04JCyThm_XdlSJemFVdK95YWjuzgDNAg6qrmr4NBvCS-hA0OavmOCz1TKtkQILU1TfXOb-ayLM7v6M6JJA59jX0ZxmRcgW3fsWsc8wcftfKyY9iHnrg-bwfNkgUIDgcDbkozbbvz5AtmJiikO1LnXANOrX-hJZgztnjbGr4ngTW97PA%3D%3D)

Dear fellow American,

Charlie Kirk dedicated his life to a simple but powerful truth: the path to success begins with loving Christ, cherishing your family, and serving your country.

With an infectious smile and unwavering conviction, Charlie made it his mission to share that message with millions. He challenged us, encouraged us, and reminded us of what really matters in life.

Now it’s our turn.

We cannot let Charlie’s message fade. His voice may be gone, but his mission must live on. Together, we must carry forward the values he championed and ensure they echo for generations to come.

Let’s honor Charlie—not just in words, but in action.

With you in the fight,

_**Marty O’Donnell**_

| **ABOUT MARTY**
**Marty O’Donnell isn’t a politician** and he’s not taking any corporate PAC money. Marty will give us a voice – not special interests or Wall Street. It’s time to turn our nation around to make life better for Nevadans.

**Marty O’Donnell has an ear for solving problems.** He’s an award-winning composer who wrote the iconic soundtracks for the video games Halo and Destiny. Marty will cut through the noise in DC.

**Marty O’Donnell will be a champion for Nevada families.** As a husband, father, and grandfather, Marty can’t sit on the sidelines as local families struggle because of the problems created by Big Government.
---

disclaimer_text: Paid for by La Gente for Grijalva_
body:
Just two weekends left to elect Adelita to Congress! We need your help!, join us!!🤩

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/3f376f0371c3/weekend-of-action-for-adelita-10308927?e=f7c3700ccb)

| | | | | |
# 2 WEEKS LEFT!
Adelita for Congress Newsletter
---
| | Hi Peter,
**History doesn’t make itself, people do. And in just days, we have a chance to make it together.** We’re in the final stretch of a race that could send the _first Latina from Arizona_ to Congress and **this is the FINAL weekend before the mail back deadline!**
This is more than a campaign — it’s a movement for representation, justice, and real change. But we won’t get there without**you.**
At a time when our communities are being silenced and our rights are on the line, staying on the sidelines isn’t an option. **We need to show up — for each other, and for the future we believe in.**
Talking to voters. Knocking on doors. Making calls. These aren’t just campaign tasks, they’re acts of power, of hope, of resistance.
Let’s make history, together. Join us.
---
| | **Here’s what’s happening this week:****Canvassing Door to Door!** Help us make phone calls, go door-to-door and connect with voters face-to-face. In honor of the beginning of Hispanic Heritage Month, join us this weekend,

name: Deb Haaland
subject: Some recent pictures from the campaign trail
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
We’ve crisscrossed New Mexico and continued to build support in every corner of the state.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)
Hey, it's Deb.
As the summer winds down, I finally had a moment to look back on the past few weeks — what a whirlwind.
We’ve crisscrossed New Mexico and continued to build support in every corner of the state. Meeting New Mexicans on the campaign trail fills me with so much joy, so I wanted to share a few snapshots with you. 📷
**[But before I dive in, will you chip in $5 to power our grassroots campaign? Your support makes it possible for me to meet people face-to-face and share my vision for New Mexico.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250911_1_recap?amount=5&refcode=em20250911-4364&refcode2=4364_400544_gBhdDq&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)**
[****](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250911_1_recap?amount=5&refcode=em20250911-4364&refcode2=4364_400544_gBhdDq&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)_New Mexicans are coming together to build safer communities._
**I participated in a National Night Out in Albuquerque** , a reminder of how we can build safer communities when we come together. Meaningful relationships between our neighbors, law enforcement, and first responders create safe places and reduce crime.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250911_1_recap?amount=5&refcode=em20250911-4364&refcode2=4364_400544_gBhdDq&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)_Meeting New Mexicans in rural areas has been one of my favorite parts of the summer._
**I met with Tucumcari residents to talk about their transportation and renewable energy economies.** Lowering utility bills for hardworking New Mexicans, creating good-paying jobs, and building a clean energy future in rural communities are at the heart of my campaign’s mission.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250911_1_recap?amount=5&refcode=em20250911-4364&refcode2=4364_400544_gBhdDq&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)_I was honored to be endorsed by New Mexico’s largest healthcare workers' union._
**One of my proudest moments from August was receiving an endorsement from CIR/SEIU** , our state’s largest healthcare workers’ union. Expanding health care access, fighting for working families, and leading fiercely are day-one priorities when I’m governor.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250911_1_recap?amount=5&refcode=em20250911-4364&refcode2=4364_400544_gBhdDq&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)_Listening to New Mexicans’ stories to learn how I can best serve them._
**I attended an early Labor Day celebration with United Association Local 412 and their loved ones.** I saw firsthand how solidarity, labor pride, and hard work bind this community together. As governor, I'll work to uplift all New Mexico workers and build ways for them and their families to thrive.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250911_1_recap?amount=5&refcode=em20250911-4364&refcode2=4364_400544_gBhdDq&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)_New Mexico is rich in tradition, and Zozobra is New Mexico True!_
It wouldn’t be a proper end to New Mexico’s summer without **Zozobra**. We came together in Santa Fe to say "Goodbye, old man gloom!" With all the challenges this year has brought, it was a much-needed reminder of the power of positivity and hope that our future holds.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250911_1_recap?amount=5&refcode=em20250911-4364&refcode2=4364_400544_gBhdDq&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)_Marching in the State Fair Parade is always a great time!_
**And of course, is there any better way to cap off a successful New Mexico summer than the State Fair?** It was incredible to feel the energy, see everyone’s smiles, and celebrate the rural communities and agricultural traditions that make our state great.
As you can see, we’re entering fall on a wave of energy, and that is all because of the support from people like you. Our campaign isn’t slowing down one bit, and I’m counting on your support to keep us going strong.
[**Will you chip in $5 right now to help keep us on the road and connecting with New Mexicans across the state? We still have a long way to go to win this race, and I can’t do it without you!**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250911_1_recap?amount=5&refcode=em20250911-4364&refcode2=4364_400544_gBhdDq&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250911_1_recap?amount=5&refcode=em20250911-4364&refcode2=4364_400544_gBhdDq&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)**
Be fierce,
Deb
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250911-1&t=2&refcode2=4364_400544_gBhdDq&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)**
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Sign up for Texts](https://deb4gov.us/text-me)
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?t=4&akid=4364%2E400544%2EgBhdDq)

Email us: [info@debhaaland.com](mailto:info@debhaaland.com)
PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO

name: Justice Democrats
subject: They targeted and sabotaged ceasefire negotiations
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

The Israeli government has now bombed six different countries this year
alone, after it carried out strikes in Qatar earlier this week.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em-20250911-israel-qatar/?source=em20250911-37015&akid=37015%2E1203867%2EZdfJxb ]The Intercept: Progressives urge U.S. arms embargo after Israel bombs
Qatar. Israel’s bombing of Qatar interrupted ceasefire negotiations in a
war made possible by U.S. support

These latest attacks targeted residential buildings and sabotaged
ceasefire negotiations, once again breaking international law without
accountability.

With billions of dollars in bombs and weapons provided by the United
States, Israel has now attacked Palestine, Lebanon, Iran, Syria, Yemen,
and Qatar – prioritizing never-ending war over peace and the safe release
of hostages.

Representatives Rashida Tlaib, Summer Lee, and other Justice Democrats are
still leading the call for action here at home: the U.S. government must
STOP arming Israel if we are going to end the genocide in Gaza and prevent
this from turning into an even larger, deadlier regional war.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em-20250911-israel-qatar/?source=em20250911-37015&akid=37015%2E1203867%2EZdfJxb ]Congresswoman Rashida Tlaib: The Israeli government is again attempting
to murder ceasefire negotiators, now bombing the capital of a U.S. ally
with total impunity. It is clear they have no interest in a ceasefire to
end their genocide of Palestinians. We must stop funding and arming this
insanity.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em-20250911-israel-qatar/?source=em20250911-37015&akid=37015%2E1203867%2EZdfJxb ]We can’t let Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his administration
keep starving Palestinians, committing war crimes, and bombing countries
without impunity: add your name to support a U.S. arms embargo against
Israel NOW!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em-20250911-israel-qatar/?source=em20250911-37015&akid=37015%2E1203867%2EZdfJxb ]Add Your Name

Our movement will not rest until our leaders finally listen to us and
start standing up for peace and human rights for all.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250911-37015-footer&t=1002&refcode2=37015_1203867_ZdfJxb&akid=37015%2E1203867%2EZdfJxb

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=37015%2E1203867%2EZdfJxb

1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603

name: Matthew Davis, LCV
subject: [🚨 TAKE ACTION ] Stop Trump’s climate denial scheme: Tell the EPA not to let polluters run amok
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTI5MTUyNjMS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjMwMDE1MTk0NQS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
URGENT ACTION NEEDED:

STOP Trump’s EPA from Letting Corporate Polluters Run Amok

Over 15 years ago, the EPA affirmed that greenhouse gases pose a threat to
public health and our environment. This “Endangerment Finding” serves as the
basis for federal climate protections, like cleaner vehicle standards and limits
on carbon pollution from power plants.

Now, the Trump EPA is trying to deny the harmful climate impacts that scientists
have confirmed — and communities around the country have felt firsthand — and
allow polluters to spew unlimited toxins into our air. Tell the Trump
administration that our lives are more important than corporate polluters’
profits.

We have a narrow window to act, and we need your help to fight this reckless, heartless, harmful move. TAKE ACTION NOW >> https://links.messages.lcv. TAKE ACTION NOW >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/X6bzs~ Peter — one of the Trump administration’s most dangerous attacks on our
climate and communities is underway, and we need your voice to help stop it. Trump’s EPA is trying to deny that climate pollutants (a.k.a. greenhouse gases) are a threat to public healthby removing the basis for all federal climate regulations known as the
Endangerment Finding. Leave a public comment to say NO to Trump’s reckless, pro-polluter scheme that would drastically harm our health, communities, and climate. Urge the EPA to keep the Endangerment Finding intact >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/w_ If EPA goes through with this, it would deny the experiences of tens of millions
of our neighbors, friends, and families who have experienced fossil-fueled
climate disasters like deadly floods, heatwaves, hurricanes, droughts, and
wildfires, and hand Big Polluters a free pass to continue spewing greenhouse
gases into our air and worsen the climate crisis. This is climate denial and fossil-fueled corruption at its absolute worst,
Peter, and it WILL harm our communities. We need every voice in our
movement to speak out before it’s too late. TAKE ACTION NOW https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/j9y2f0KL_PQ_ Here’s what’s at stake if we don’t take action now: More pollution and fewer guardrails for polluters. By denying that climate change harms our communities, the EPA will toss out limits on carbon pollution from cars, trucks, and power plants, essentially giving companies free rein to pollute.
Worsening public health. More tailpipe exhaust from vehicles means more asthma attacks, strokes, heart disease, cancer, and premature deaths, especially among communities of color, low-income communities, and children and seniors.
Higher costs for families. Rolling back cleaner cars standards means less fuel-efficient vehicles, deepens our reliance on climate-destroying fossil fuels, and could cost everyday people hundreds of dollars a year at the pump, while enriching Trump’s fossil fuel donors.
More climate chaos. With Trump’s EPA denying climate change and giving Big Polluters a free pass, we can expect even more frequent and severe wildfires, droughts, floods, and crop failures, driving up costs and food insecurity while deadly heat and storms ravage our communities. We need to be working to SOLVE the climate crisis, not ACCELERATING it. Please, fight back by telling the EPA that climate change is real, greenhouse gases are harmful to our health, and the 2009 Endangerment Finding and Cleaner Vehicle Standards MUST stay intact >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/bxyvTVgXq_ Thank you for standing up for science and helping to protect our climate,
health, and future. Matthew Davis
Vice President of Federal Policy
League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you,update your contact information https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=12915263&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjMwMDE1MTk0NQS2&spReportId=MjMwMDE1MTk0NQS2.Click here to unsubscribe https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=12915263&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjMwMDE1MTk0NQS2&spReportId=MjMwMDE1MTk0NQS2 from our supporter list.Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback here mailto:feedback@lcv.org?subject=, or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.

740 15th St NW, Suite 700
Washington, DC 20005
202-785-8683 DONATE https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/-

Leave a public comment to say NO to Trump’s reckless, pro-polluter scheme that would drastically harm our health, communities, and climate. Urge the EPA to keep the Endangerment Finding intact >> If EPA goes through with this, it would deny the experiences of tens of millions of our neighbors, friends, and families who have experienced fossil-fueled climate disasters like deadly floods, heatwaves, hurricanes, droughts, and wildfires, and hand Big Polluters a free pass to continue spewing greenhouse gases into our air and worsen the climate crisis. This is climate denial and fossil-fueled corruption at its absolute worst, Peter, and it WILL harm our communities. We need every voice in our movement to speak out before it’s too late. TAKE ACTION NOW Here’s what’s at stake if we don’t take action now: More pollution and fewer guardrails for polluters. By denying that climate change harms our communities, the EPA will toss out limits on carbon pollution from cars, trucks, and power plants, essentially giving companies free rein to pollute. Worsening public health. More tailpipe exhaust from vehicles means more asthma attacks, strokes, heart disease, cancer, and premature deaths, especially among communities of color, low-income communities, and children and seniors. Higher costs for families. Rolling back cleaner cars standards means less fuel-efficient vehicles, deepens our reliance on climate-destroying fossil fuels, and could cost everyday people hundreds of dollars a year at the pump, while enriching Trump’s fossil fuel donors. More climate chaos. With Trump’s EPA denying climate change and giving Big Polluters a free pass, we can expect even more frequent and severe wildfires, droughts, floods, and crop failures, driving up costs and food insecurity while deadly heat and storms ravage our communities. We need to be working to SOLVE the climate crisis, not ACCELERATING it. Please, fight back by telling the EPA that climate change is real, greenhouse gases are harmful to our health, and the 2009 Endangerment Finding and Cleaner Vehicle Standards MUST stay intact >> Thank you for standing up for science and helping to protect our climate, health, and future. Matthew Davis Vice President of Federal Policy League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you,update your contact information here to unsubscribe from our supporter list.Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback here mailto:feedback@lcv.org?subject=, or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support. 740 15th St NW, Suite 700 Washington, DC 20005 202-785-8683 DONATE
