name: ACTION via Team MGP
subject: e: Right to repair
body:
| | | **Marie is working to ensure everyone has access to repair materials for the products and equipment on which they rely – and she’s making sure that right to repair also extends to our servicemembers.**

[If you support Marie’s bipartisan work to lower repair costs and protect our right to fix what we own, add your name here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115050595/569020033/1272947930?refcode=MGP_EM_PT_90DO_250911_2_1&refcode2=730e431f-608f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MzBlNDMxZi02MDhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=S1IhKsHpt83mDwI0ZveDuBKoOHjf25sD8aFIk3tTMOA=&emci=24b1452f-5f8f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=730e431f-608f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=31138575)
---

Peter – Since day one, Marie has made it a priority to empower ordinary Americans to make their own repairs with access to the necessary data and tools, making sure we don’t leave rural communities or working families behind.

We must have the tools and freedom to repair our own things instead of being forced to buy cheap, disposable replacements. **Now, Marie is making sure that the right to repair also extends to military members and technicians.**

[Letting servicemembers fix their own gear honors their skill, increases military readiness, and reduces costs. It just makes sense. If you agree, add your name here:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115050597/569020035/1272947930?refcode=MGP_EM_PT_90DO_250911_2_1&refcode2=730e431f-608f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MzBlNDMxZi02MDhmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=S1IhKsHpt83mDwI0ZveDuBKoOHjf25sD8aFIk3tTMOA=&emci=24b1452f-5f8f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=730e431f-608f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=31138575)

Today is the 24th anniversary of the September 11th attacks on our country – a day that changed the world we live in forever. | | | [](http://links.briannaforco.com/s/c/RSYWYQ2yxq69dn8t1F8Nd8IE9AxhHIK3b1SB25kQJqSis_Y6Rw3MnxUJXwS5_d0kAJviud8q72BsmB6I2fp5jSiOFcF-JvlCbO-ij3lKgtWZ_Ge7KG4aZGfuEDJhFcUJyQ_YA9QD8sjvz8MwzsQFy0nCXJ8sIsIvaWBLoBLSBseb4Yvn1zTonRusTahEPZmAp66WzqVmq5vcUxoYsgpaHAhk21CbzA0LBMfxCQ-jTLrSQkLCZv9inDQ9X9HQk8izX-VwV310Xc1yWO5B_kQPH3-dDS7jvVDi/HieDh5s46QO7Ekj-FXJwIe55ZCl_ag-c/12) Peter, Today is the 24th anniversary of the September 11th attacks on our country – a day that changed the world we live in forever. Every year since 2001, we remember the countless first responders who rushed towards danger while so many ran away from it. They risked their lives that day to help as many people as possible, and their bravery will always be remembered. We honor the lives of the victims and hold the family members they left behind in our thoughts and in our hearts. [](http://links.briannaforco.com/s/c/nfnnIulYq28Khtp56heRkFDq7JV3u0ctoanDZXF62vy61Snm5iU9GkfdtUbXGfNw2Ro8ZcvlcgW6ZP4BqmWywMZatniZBSM1nQ9XMncQZHPgdMmuOspHsgQfNMt-EngnF5gIfOvNmBYJGfKWhQAzDCAaL2eAuURmYpxSWpOxF4ogTIzMByceV0aIbUPU92kRKRYK3zSj47X1OTvgPD6nE3HQokM0IlHmgU7TXhVdrPqQ-xgCEIAj7D1MWDudzFUcXlHjTkIW0tCiAzBSCreVGP7n5rLBYGFD/G3kbI0a42PK-h_whEn8pj_tX3pXU7SC0/12) _A photo I took at the 9/11 Memorial in New York City_ **Our country came together after 9/11 – we supported each other, helped one another, and remembered the values of being an American. Let us continue to carry these lessons forward, even at times when we feel more divided than ever, and recommit ourselves to unity, compassion, and resilience.** It was in those moments of national unity and resilience after 9/11 that I felt an even stronger calling to dedicate my life to public service – to do my part in strengthening and protecting our communities. Today, I hope you’ll join me in taking a moment to reflect on the courage of those we lost, to honor the first responders who rushed into danger without hesitation, and to remember that we are strongest when we stand together. – Brianna Follow Brianna Online: I take a month to adjust to my new job and the world somehow got even crazier and darker. Tomorrow, we will talk murder (again, sigh...). Until then, I am coming back positive, with a thank you to everyone for sticking around, and an answer to a question many of you have asked over the last month:
Will I ever run again?
It was a particularly hot question at the beginning of August, when something I hadn't even realized started going around: that in the midst of last year's Republican landslide, with your help I managed to get 49% of the vote in Missouri's 2nd Congressional District- a Republican held seat. At a time when Democrats are searching for answers, it seems that we must have done something right that could be built upon going forward.
So to cut to the chase: yes, I do plan to run again. The longer answer is below.
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.QQUUHK1cI8WG1UCd-v_KDKkLjXqzzoJlxlgQ6KW35uo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=173396211)
To be honest, I would run right now if I could. So much is at stake, and I'm not one to shy away from a challenge. But I won't be able to run again, at least not successfully, until I take care of a few things.
First, I took nearly four years off of work to run in back-to-back Senate cycles. As many of you know, I have no family money or other resources to fall back on, so I need to work for a little bit first to get back on my feet and afford another go- which is why I took a one year activation in the Marine Corps starting this summer.
On a related note: **thank you** to everyone who has stuck with this Substack over the last year, and particularly this last month when I had to take a pause to adjust to the new job. Your support here is what makes a future run possible. The type of campaign where we aren't beholden to anyone but each other and can continue to engage and experiment with messages that make it possible not just to get 49% in Republican territory in their landslide year, but to actually crest the hill and start winning again.
Now that I've gotten settled at work, I feel good about being able to write in the evenings and weekends again. So we are going to look at what is going on in our country, how we can respond effectively, and how we can build change.
We will examine the darkness to better learn how to overcome the hopelessness and rage that is smothering our society.
So, tomorrow, we will return to murder. And, after that, something I studied over the last month that I think is a clue to the way forward.
Thank you again for the continued support and engagement.
Lucas

name: Jose Javier Rodriguez
subject: Do you know what a People's Lawyer actually does?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSE JAVIER RODRIGUEZ FOR FLORIDA ATTORNEY GENERAL
body:
Let me explain. | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115050838/569020889/1546157161?refcode=em_JJR_FR_PeoplesLawyer_091125&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDkxMjYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTBmNDU5NWUtNjI4Zi1mMDExLWI0ODQtNjA0NWJkZWI3NDEzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=YY1vy2YpAZkNWGKP1DGcqWgD2WqmS06az4ybs12FDcs=&emci=e98f3ed9-478f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=e0f4595e-628f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=32374386)
---
Hey Peter, You'll often hear me talk about becoming the "People's Lawyer," but do you know why I use that phrase? Most people think of Attorneys General as lawyers who simply represent state government agencies and work with the legislature. And sure, that's part of the job. But here's what really matters: As Attorney General, I'll be Florida's representative of the public interest. That means I'll look out for everyday folks whose rights have been threatened, advocate for working families who are getting crushed by corruption and rising costs, and protect our communities from powerful interests. In this position, I'll have the authority to defend civil rights, investigate fraud, sue corrupt corporations, and protect the interests of our state and its people. **That's exactly why electing the right person as Attorney General is so critical, and why I need your help to win this fight.** **[Peter, we are building a movement to take our state back from corrupt special interests, and it’s going to take all of us to make it happen. Peter ,
We’ve got good news: Democrats are overperforming across the country, and Virginia just gave us another reason to celebrate!
In a special election on Tuesday, Democrat James Walkinshaw easily defeated his Republican opponent to succeed the late Gerry Connolly in Congress. That’s one more vote for democracy, and one less seat for Trump’s MAGA extremists to lean on!
But we can’t stop here. This November, Virginia voters will head back to the polls, this time to decide control of the House of Delegates. GOTV PAC is powering voter turnout operations in 12 critical House of Delegates races, and we don’t just want to win; we want to send a message. Americans are tired of Trump’s extremism, tired of Republicans trying to strip away rights, and ready to stand up for real solutions.
Your support makes it possible and allows us to fuel door-knocking, texting, and phone banking programs that cut through the noise and get Democrats to the polls. name: Arizona Democratic Party
subject: 100 voters showed up. Ciscomani didn't.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE ARIZONA DEMOCRATIC PARTY
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115051688/569023389/-1397648958?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250911_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzA2MTNkOGUtNjQ4Zi1mMDExLWI0ODQtNjA0NWJkZWI3NDEzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=IfggGaXs2bulZt_--MOk8bkMLpWRQ0AlMAzwBREH-Co=)
---
Hi there,

Last Thursday, nearly 100 Arizonans showed up at Juan Ciscomani's office demanding answers about his vote for the Big Beautiful Bill. **Do you know who wasn't there? Juan Ciscomani.** These weren't protesters looking for a fight. They were working families, single mothers, and community leaders worried about losing healthcare, Medicaid, and SNAP benefits so billionaires can get tax cuts. One organizer, a single mother with a special needs child, explained how Medicaid helps her get the care her child needs. "This will cut a lot of things in our community," she said. **And how has Ciscomani responded to these concerns in the past? He said, "With any decision we make, there will be a split decision. Some people are going to like it; some people aren't."** That's not leadership. That's indifference. For months, constituents have been asking Ciscomani for town halls. They've asked for his presence at community events. They've asked to meet in his office. **He won't show up. He won't listen. He won't engage.** "Our representative doesn't listen to his constituents," said Arizona Representative Nancy Gutierrez, who joined the protestors. "He doesn't show up at events." This is what happens when representatives stop paying attention to how their votes affect real families. Ciscomani voted to extend Trump's tax cuts for the wealthy while cutting programs that working families depend on. **He's not just voting against Arizona families — he's also refusing to meet with them and defend his decision to their faces.** We've seen this pattern from vulnerable Republicans across Arizona. They vote with the MAGA agenda, then hide from the consequences. David Schweikert backs out of debates. Eli Crane won’t host town halls. clean_body: | = Hi there, Last Thursday, nearly 100 Arizonans showed up at Juan Ciscomani's office demanding answers about his vote for the Big Beautiful Bill. Do you know who wasn't there? Juan Ciscomani. These weren't protesters looking for a fight. They were working families, single mothers, and community leaders worried about losing healthcare, Medicaid, and SNAP benefits so billionaires can get tax cuts. One organizer, a single mother with a special needs child, explained how Medicaid helps her get the care her child needs. "This will cut a lot of things in our community," she said. And how has Ciscomani responded to these concerns in the past? He said, "With any decision we make, there will be a split decision. Some people are going to like it; some people aren't." That's not leadership. That's indifference. For months, constituents have been asking Ciscomani for town halls. They've asked for his presence at community events. They've asked to meet in his office. He won't show up. He won't listen. He won't engage. "Our representative doesn't listen to his constituents," said Arizona Representative Nancy Gutierrez, who joined the protestors. "He doesn't show up at events." This is what happens when representatives stop paying attention to how their votes affect real families. Ciscomani voted to extend Trump's tax cuts for the wealthy while cutting programs that working families depend on. He's not just voting against Arizona families — he's also refusing to meet with them and defend his decision to their faces. We've seen this pattern from vulnerable Republicans across Arizona. They vote with the MAGA agenda, then hide from the consequences. David Schweikert backs out of debates. Eli Crane won’t host town halls. The list goes on... But here's what we can do about it: organize, support strong Democratic candidates, and flip more seats blue in 2026. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = With determination, Arizona Democratic Party Arizona Democratic Party PO Box 36123 Phoenix, AZ 85067-6123 United States mailto:info@azdem.org | PAID FOR BY THE ARIZONA DEMOCRATIC PARTY. NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. GREG FREEMAN, TREASURER. WWW.AZDEM.ORG

subject: Pesky

body:
#

To be honest with you, Peter, we hate sending these pesky emails asking for money.
We’d much rather be telling you about all the work Jon is doing to stand up to the Trump Administration and push back against the corruption in Washington. But the truth is these emails are absolutely critical to our campaign.
Jon won’t beg federal lobbyists, corporate PACs, or dark money special interests to cut him a check. Instead, our campaign is powered by folks like you giving $5, $10, $25 at a time to requests like this one.
And we have to ask because _this race is going to be particularly expensive._
**MAGA is going to use every dirty trick in the book and spend hundreds of millions of dollars on ads attacking Jon to try and replace him with a MAGA lackey.**
So even though we wish we didn’t have to ask, here it is:

name: Chris Ahuja
subject: Never Forget. Never Repeat.
body:
We must not sink into hate as a result of tragedy

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=0fe85f8f42&e=5a6ee82cdb)
| | | | | | Dear Peter,**Here in California’s 32nd District, we are honoring and remembering September 11th.** Many of our neighbors in the Valley still recall where they were that morning—feeling the same shock, grief, and unity as if it were right here at home. Some of our families served in the recovery and rebuilding efforts, or answered the call to military service in the years that followed.Today, we honor the nearly 3,000 lives lost and pay tribute to the first responders, service members, and ordinary citizens whose courage carried our nation through its darkest hours.**That day revealed the best of who we are** : neighbors helping neighbors, communities uniting across differences, and a country determined to rise from tragedy with resilience and hope.**But we must also be honest about what followed.** Too many of our Muslim, Arab, Sikh, and immigrant neighbors were demonized and treated as suspect simply because of who they were. That was wrong then, and it would be wrong today.**So when we say** _**Never Forget,**_**we must also say** _**Never Repeat.**_**** We cannot allow fear to divide us—or be weaponized to justify ICE raids, Islamophobic rhetoric, or politicians stoking hate for political gain.**I know this personally. My own family was racially profiled after 9/11.** We were made to feel like outsiders in our own country, singled out for how we looked and what people assumed about us. That pain has never left us, and it’s why I am determined to make sure no one else has to endure it.**We will never forget. And we will never repeat.**
---
| | **In solidarity,
**[****](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=075347f391&e=5a6ee82cdb)**Chris Ahuja****Democrat for Congress, CD-32**

---
| | | | | | | | | [ View email in browser](https://mailchi.mp/chrisahuja/ahuja-cost-of-living-too-high-11042112?e=5a6ee82cdb)
Christopher Ahuja for Congress * 21255 Burbank Blvd Ste 120 * Woodland Hills, CA 91367-6669 * USA
[update your preferences](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/profile?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=45384ee532&e=5a6ee82cdb&c=3683aa20ef) or [unsubscribe](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=45384ee532&t=b&e=5a6ee82cdb&c=3683aa20ef)

clean_body: We must not sink into hate as a result of tragedy | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Dear Peter,Here in California’s 32nd District, we are honoring and remembering September 11th. Many of our neighbors in the Valley still recall where they were that morning—feeling the same shock, grief, and unity as if it were right here at home. Some of our families served in the recovery and rebuilding efforts, or answered the call to military service in the years that followed.Today, we honor the nearly 3,000 lives lost and pay tribute to the first responders, service members, and ordinary citizens whose courage carried our nation through its darkest hours.That day revealed the best of who we are : neighbors helping neighbors, communities uniting across differences, and a country determined to rise from tragedy with resilience and hope.But we must also be honest about what followed. Too many of our Muslim, Arab, Sikh, and immigrant neighbors were demonized and treated as suspect simply because of who they were. That was wrong then, and it would be wrong today.So when we say Never Forget,we must also say Never Repeat.** We cannot allow fear to divide us—or be weaponized to justify ICE raids, Islamophobic rhetoric, or politicians stoking hate for political gain.I know this personally. My own family was racially profiled after 9/11. We were made to feel like outsiders in our own country, singled out for how we looked and what people assumed about us. That pain has never left us, and it’s why I am determined to make sure no one else has to endure it.We will never forget. And we will never repeat. | | In solidarity, Ahuja**Democrat for Congress, CD-32 | | | | | | | | | Christopher Ahuja for Congress 21255 Burbank Blvd Ste 120 Woodland Hills, CA 91367-6669 * USA or |

[**With just 54 days until Election Day, we can’t leave anything up to chance. While Cuomo caters to the wishes of landlords, CEOs, Trump donors, and billionaires from the Hamptons, Zohran Mamdani is laser-focused on making New York City more affordable for everyone. clean_body: We can’t help but be nervous about whatever they’re planning next. | | | Peter, The wealthiest people in New York City are having a meltdown about Zohran Mamdani.1 Under normal circumstances, we’d be celebrating all of this billionaire hysteria. But we can’t help but be nervous about whatever they’re planning next. Trump has already been urging candidates to clear the field for Cuomo.2 Head-to-head polling shows a close race.3 And big money donors are plotting to spend tens of millions on attack ads against Zohran over the next two months. Make no mistake, we’re confident in the strength of this movement. While Cuomo caters to the wishes of landlords, CEOs, Trump donors, and billionaires from the Hamptons, Zohran Mamdani is laser-focused on making New York City more affordable for everyone. That vision is why he won the primary by double digits. And with your help, our multiracial working-class movement can help him win again in November. In solidarity, Team WFP Sources: 1\. , New York Times, September 8, 2025 2\. , New York Times, September 3, 2025 3\. , Siena University, September 9, 2025 Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. | | Sent via .
